#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation: Set default Bash shell file-creation mask to 027 system-wide.
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

UMASK_VALUE="027"

if [[ "${EUID}" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

set_umask_in_file() {
    local file="$1"

    if [[ ! -f "${file}" ]]; then
        return 0
    fi

    if grep -qE '^[[:space:]]*umask[[:space:]]+[0-7]{3,4}[[:space:]]*$' "${file}"; then
        sed -ri "s/^([[:space:]]*umask[[:space:]]+)[0-7]{3,4}([[:space:]]*)\$/\1${UMASK_VALUE}\2/" "${file}"
    else
        # Avoid duplicating an identical line if it already exists somewhere
        if ! grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*umask[[:space:]]+${UMASK_VALUE}[[:space:]]*\$" "${file}"; then
            printf '\numask %s\n' "${UMASK_VALUE}" >> "${file}"
        fi
    fi
}

# 1. Ensure /etc/bashrc and /etc/profile enforce the umask for bash sessions.
for f in /etc/bashrc /etc/profile; do
    set_umask_in_file "${f}"
done

# 2. Ensure a dedicated profile.d script enforces the umask for all shells
#    sourcing /etc/profile (login shells, etc.).
PROFILE_D_FILE="/etc/profile.d/50-umask027.sh"

if [[ -f "${PROFILE_D_FILE}" ]]; then
    set_umask_in_file "${PROFILE_D_FILE}"
else
    cat > "${PROFILE_D_FILE}" <<EOF
# Enforce default umask of ${UMASK_VALUE} for interactive and login shells.
umask ${UMASK_VALUE}
EOF
fi
chmod 0644 "${PROFILE_D_FILE}"
chown root:root "${PROFILE_D_FILE}"

# 3. Ensure /etc/login.defs UMASK directive matches, so it's consistent
#    for services relying on it (e.g., useradd defaults).
LOGIN_DEFS="/etc/login.defs"
if [[ -f "${LOGIN_DEFS}" ]]; then
    if grep -qE '^[[:space:]]*UMASK[[:space:]]+[0-7]{3,4}[[:space:]]*$' "${LOGIN_DEFS}"; then
        sed -ri "s/^([[:space:]]*UMASK[[:space:]]+)[0-7]{3,4}([[:space:]]*)\$/\1${UMASK_VALUE}\2/" "${LOGIN_DEFS}"
    else
        printf '\nUMASK           %s\n' "${UMASK_VALUE}" >> "${LOGIN_DEFS}"
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure default umask for C shell (csh/tcsh) users is 077
#

set -euo pipefail

UMASK_VALUE="077"

# List of files to check/update for csh umask settings
CSH_FILES=(
    "/etc/csh.cshrc"
    "/etc/csh.login"
)

# Function to set or update umask in a given file
set_umask_in_file() {
    local file="$1"

    # If file does not exist, create it
    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        touch "$file"
        chmod 644 "$file"
    fi

    # Check if there is an existing umask line (commented or not)
    if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*umask[[:space:]]+[0-7]+' "$file"; then
        # Update existing umask line(s) to the desired value
        sed -ri "s/^[[:space:]]*umask[[:space:]]+[0-7]+.*/umask ${UMASK_VALUE}/" "$file"
    else
        # No umask line found, append one
        # Ensure file ends with a newline before appending
        if [ -s "$file" ] && [ "$(tail -c1 "$file")" != "" ]; then
            echo "" >> "$file"
        fi
        echo "umask ${UMASK_VALUE}" >> "$file"
    fi

    # Remove any duplicate umask lines, keeping only the last occurrence
    if [ "$(grep -Ec '^[[:space:]]*umask[[:space:]]+[0-7]+' "$file")" -gt 1 ]; then
        # Get line numbers of umask entries
        mapfile -t lines < <(grep -nE '^[[:space:]]*umask[[:space:]]+[0-7]+' "$file" | cut -d: -f1)
        last_line="${lines[-1]}"
        tmpfile=$(mktemp)
        awk -v last="$last_line" 'NR==FNR{if($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*umask[[:space:]]+[0-7]+/) {if(FNR!=last) next}} {print}' "$file" "$file" > "$tmpfile"
        mv "$tmpfile" "$file"
        chmod 644 "$file"
    fi
}

# Apply umask setting to all relevant csh configuration files
for f in "${CSH_FILES[@]}"; do
    set_umask_in_file "$f"
done

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Disable sending of IPv4 ICMP redirects (default interface)
# on RHEL 8 systems. Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

SYSCTL_PARAM="net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects"
DESIRED_VALUE="0"
SYSCTL_CONF_DIR="/etc/sysctl.d"
SYSCTL_CONF_FILE="${SYSCTL_CONF_DIR}/60-disable-send-redirects.conf"

# Ensure the sysctl.d directory exists
if [[ ! -d "${SYSCTL_CONF_DIR}" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "${SYSCTL_CONF_DIR}"
fi

# Function to check if a line already exists in the config file
line_exists() {
    local file="$1"
    local param="$2"
    local value="$3"
    if [[ -f "${file}" ]]; then
        grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${value}[[:space:]]*$" "${file}"
        return $?
    fi
    return 1
}

# Ensure the persistent configuration file has the correct setting
if [[ -f "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" ]]; then
    if line_exists "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" "${SYSCTL_PARAM}" "${DESIRED_VALUE}"; then
        :
    else
        # Remove any existing (possibly conflicting) entries for this parameter
        sed -i "/^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_PARAM//./\\.}[[:space:]]*=.*/d" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
        echo "${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}" >> "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
    fi
else
    echo "${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}" > "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
fi

chmod 0644 "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"

# Also ensure no other sysctl configuration files override this setting
# Search common sysctl config locations for conflicting definitions
CONFIG_LOCATIONS=(
    "/etc/sysctl.conf"
    "/etc/sysctl.d/*.conf"
    "/run/sysctl.d/*.conf"
    "/usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf"
)

for pattern in "${CONFIG_LOCATIONS[@]}"; do
    for file in ${pattern}; do
        [[ -e "${file}" ]] || continue
        [[ "${file}" == "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" ]] && continue

        if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_PARAM//./\\.}[[:space:]]*=" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
            sed -i "/^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_PARAM//./\\.}[[:space:]]*=.*/d" "${file}"
        fi
    done
done

# Apply the setting at runtime
CURRENT_VALUE="$(sysctl -n "${SYSCTL_PARAM}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"

if [[ "${CURRENT_VALUE}" != "${DESIRED_VALUE}" ]]; then
    sysctl -w "${SYSCTL_PARAM}=${DESIRED_VALUE}" >/dev/null
fi

# Reload sysctl settings from all configuration files to ensure consistency
sysctl --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# Final verification
FINAL_VALUE="$(sysctl -n "${SYSCTL_PARAM}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"

if [[ "${FINAL_VALUE}" == "${DESIRED_VALUE}" ]]; then
    echo "Success: ${SYSCTL_PARAM} is set to ${DESIRED_VALUE}."
    exit 0
else
    echo "Error: Failed to set ${SYSCTL_PARAM} to ${DESIRED_VALUE}. Current value: ${FINAL_VALUE}" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# using certificate-based (x.509) peer name verification before

# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times; only changes config when needed.

RSYSLOG_D_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
REQUIRED_DIRECTIVE='$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode x509/name'
DIRECTIVE_KEY='ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode'
BACKUP_SUFFIX=".bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Disable the cramfs kernel module on RHEL 8
#
# This script ensures that:
#   1. The cramfs module is blacklisted so it cannot be loaded automatically.
#   2. The cramfs module is set to "install /bin/false" so it cannot be
#      loaded on demand (manually or via modprobe).
#   3. If the module is currently loaded, it is unloaded.
#
# The script is idempotent: running it multiple times will not create
# duplicate entries or cause errors.

set -euo pipefail

MODULE_NAME="cramfs"
MODPROBE_DIR="/etc/modprobe.d"
CONF_FILE="${MODPROBE_DIR}/${MODULE_NAME}.conf"

# Ensure the modprobe.d directory exists
if [[ ! -d "${MODPROBE_DIR}" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "${MODPROBE_DIR}"
fi

# Ensure the configuration file exists
if [[ ! -f "${CONF_FILE}" ]]; then
    touch "${CONF_FILE}"
fi

# Add "install /bin/false" line if not already present
INSTALL_LINE="install ${MODULE_NAME} /bin/false"
if ! grep -Pq -- "^\s*install\s+${MODULE_NAME}\s+/bin/false\s*$" "${CONF_FILE}"; then
    echo "${INSTALL_LINE}" >> "${CONF_FILE}"
fi

# Add "blacklist" line if not already present
BLACKLIST_LINE="blacklist ${MODULE_NAME}"
if ! grep -Pq -- "^\s*blacklist\s+${MODULE_NAME}\s*$" "${CONF_FILE}"; then
    echo "${BLACKLIST_LINE}" >> "${CONF_FILE}"
fi

# If the module is currently loaded, unload it
if lsmod | grep -q "^${MODULE_NAME}[[:space:]]"; then
    modprobe -r "${MODULE_NAME}" || true
fi

exit 0

BLACKLIST_LINE="blacklist ${MODULE_NAME}"
if ! grep -Pq -- "^\s*blacklist\s+${MODULE_NAME}\s*$" "${CONF_FILE}"; then
    echo "${BLACKLIST_LINE}" >> "${CONF_FILE}"
fi

# If the module is currently loaded, unload it
if lsmod | grep -q "^${MODULE_NAME}[[:space:]]"; then
    modprobe -r "${MODULE_NAME}" || true
fi

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script to ensure that all files and directories within
# each local interactive user's home directory are group-owned by a
# group of which that user is a member.
#

set -u

# Determine the minimum UID considered "interactive" (default 1000)
UID_MIN="$(awk '/^\s*UID_MIN/{print $2}' /etc/login.defs 2>/dev/null)"
if [[ -z "${UID_MIN}" ]]; then
    UID_MIN=1000
fi

# Iterate over all local users defined in /etc/passwd
while IFS=: read -r username _ user_uid user_gid home_dir shell; do

    # Skip system accounts
    if [[ "${user_uid}" -lt "${UID_MIN}" ]]; then
        continue
    fi

    # Skip users with nologin/false shells (non-interactive)
    case "${shell}" in
        */nologin|*/false|"")
            continue
            ;;
    esac

    # Skip if home directory does not exist or is not a directory
    if [[ ! -d "${home_dir}" ]]; then
        continue
    fi

    # Build list of GIDs the user is a member of (primary + supplementary)
    mapfile -t user_gids < <(id -G "${username}" 2>/dev/null)

    if [[ "${#user_gids[@]}" -eq 0 ]]; then
        # Fall back to primary GID only
        user_gids=("${user_gid}")
    fi

    # Construct a find expression to exclude files already group-owned
    # by one of the user's groups.
    find_expr=()
    for gid in "${user_gids[@]}"; do
        if [[ ${#find_expr[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
            find_expr+=(-o)
        fi
        find_expr+=(-group "${gid}")
    done

    # Find all files/directories in the home directory NOT group-owned
    # by one of the user's groups, and fix their group ownership to the
    # user's primary GID.
    if [[ "${#find_expr[@]}" -gt 0 ]]; then
        find "${home_dir}" -xdev -mount -not \( "${find_expr[@]}" \) -exec chgrp "${user_gid}" {} + 2>/dev/null
    else
        find "${home_dir}" -xdev -mount -exec chgrp "${user_gid}" {} + 2>/dev/null
    fi

done < /etc/passwd

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Make login UIDs immutable via auditd persistent rules
#

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/99-loginuid-immutable.rules"
IMMUTABLE_RULE="--loginuid-immutable"

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
if [ ! -d "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"
fi

# Ensure the rules file exists
if [ ! -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    touch "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Idempotently ensure the immutable loginuid rule is present in the file
if ! grep -qE '^\s*--loginuid-immutable\s*$' "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "${IMMUTABLE_RULE}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Some systems also consolidate rules via augenrules; ensure it's the last rule
# Remove any duplicate blank lines and ensure the rule is present exactly once
TMP_FILE=$(mktemp)
awk '!seen[$0]++' "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" > "${TMP_FILE}"
mv "${TMP_FILE}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

chmod 640 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
chown root:root "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Regenerate the consolidated audit.rules file if augenrules is available
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Fallback: directly load the rule into the running kernel audit config
    if ! auditctl -s | grep -q "loginuid_immutable=1"; then
        auditctl "${IMMUTABLE_RULE}" || true
    fi
fi

# Ensure immediate application in the running kernel, if not already immutable
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    CURRENT_STATE=$(auditctl -s 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'loginuid_immutable=\K[0-9]+' || echo "0")
    if [ "${CURRENT_STATE}" != "1" ]; then
        auditctl "${IMMUTABLE_RULE}" || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure audit rules exist to record kernel module unload
# (delete_module syscall) events performed by non-system users (auid >= 1000),
# on all supported architectures, tagged with the key "modules".
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/modules.rules"
KEY="modules"

# Define the required rule lines for both possible architectures.
# Both b32 and b64 variants are included because even on 64-bit systems
# the 32-bit syscall table may be reachable (compat mode), and on
# 32-bit-only systems the b64 rule is simply ignored/not applicable.
declare -a REQUIRED_RULES=(
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S delete_module -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S delete_module -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"
)

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure audit rules exist to record kernel module unload
# (delete_module syscall) events performed by non-system users (auid >= 1000),
# on all supported architectures, tagged with the key "modules".
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/modules.rules"
KEY="modules"

# Define the required rule lines for both possible architectures.
# Both b32 and b64 variants are included because even on 64-bit systems
# the 32-bit syscall table may be reachable (compat mode), and on
# 32-bit-only systems the b64 rule is simply ignored/not applicable.
declare -a REQUIRED_RULES=(
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S delete_module -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S delete_module -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"
)

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Ensure the rules file exists
touch "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Add each required rule if not already present (idempotent)
for rule in "${REQUIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -qxF "${rule}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"; then
        echo "${rule}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    fi
done

# Deduplicate lines while preserving order, in case of repeated runs or
# pre-existing duplicate entries.
TMP_FILE="$(mktemp)"
awk '!seen[$0]++' "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" > "${TMP_FILE}"
mv "${TMP_FILE}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
chmod 0640 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Merge and load the rules into the running kernel audit system so the
# change is active immediately, and persist them for future boots.
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Ensure auditd is enabled and running so the persisted rules are applied
# on every subsequent boot as well.
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    systemctl is-active --quiet auditd || systemctl start auditd
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Lock user accounts after no more than 3 consecutive
# failed login attempts using authselect + pam_faillock on RHEL 8.
# Idempotent and persists across reboots (authselect + faillock.conf).

set -euo pipefail

FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"
DENY_VALUE=3
FAIL_INTERVAL=900
UNLOCK_TIME=600

# Ensure authselect is installed
if ! command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dnf -y install authselect
fi

# Capture current authselect state
CURRENT_OUTPUT="$(authselect current 2>&1 || true)"

if echo "${CURRENT_OUTPUT}" | grep -qi "No existing configuration"; then
    # No profile is currently selected - select sssd profile with faillock feature enabled
    authselect select sssd with-faillock --force
else
    # A profile is already selected; determine profile id
    PROFILE_LINE="$(echo "${CURRENT_OUTPUT}" | grep -m1 'Profile ID:' || true)"
    PROFILE_ID="$(echo "${PROFILE_LINE}" | awk -F': ' '{print $2}' | tr -d '[:space:]')"

    if [ -z "${PROFILE_ID}" ]; then
        PROFILE_ID="sssd"
    fi

    if echo "${CURRENT_OUTPUT}" | grep -q "with-faillock"; then
        : # feature already enabled - nothing to do
    else
        authselect enable-feature with-faillock
    fi
fi

# Apply any pending changes (idempotent, safe if nothing changed)
authselect apply-changes >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# Ensure faillock.conf exists
if [ ! -f "${FAILLOCK_CONF}" ]; then
    touch "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
fi

set_faillock_param() {
    local param="$1"
    local value="$2"

    if grep -qE "^\s*${param}\s*=" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"; then
        sed -i -E "s/^\s*${param}\s*=.*/${param} = ${value}/" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    elif grep -qE "^\s*#\s*${param}\s*=" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"; then
        sed -i -E "s/^\s*#\s*${param}\s*=.*/${param} = ${value}/" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    else
        echo "${param} = ${value}" >> "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    fi
}

set_faillock_param "deny" "${DENY_VALUE}"
set_faillock_param "fail_interval" "${FAIL_INTERVAL}"
set_faillock_param "unlock_time" "${UNLOCK_TIME}"

# Final apply to ensure PAM stacks are up to date
authselect apply-changes >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

exit 0

set -euo pipefail

CONFIG_FILE="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
PARAM="ucredit"
VALUE="-1"

    touch "${CONFIG_FILE}"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure auditd records execution attempts of the
# semanage command (SELinux management utility) for privileged access
# auditing, for all UID>=1000 users and root, persistently.

set -euo pipefail

RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules"
AUDIT_RULES_D="/etc/audit/rules.d"
KEY_TAG="privileged-priv_change"

# Locate the actual path to semanage (fall back to standard path if not found)
SEMANAGE_PATH="$(command -v semanage 2>/dev/null || echo /usr/sbin/semanage)"

if [ ! -e "${SEMANAGE_PATH}" ]; then
    SEMANAGE_PATH="/usr/sbin/semanage"
fi

# Ensure the rules directory exists
mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_D}"

# Ensure the audit rules file exists
touch "${RULES_FILE}"

# Construct the two required audit rule lines (32-bit and 64-bit arches)
RULE_B32="-a always,exit -F path=${SEMANAGE_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY_TAG}"
RULE_B64="-a always,exit -F path=${SEMANAGE_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY_TAG}"

# Since 'path' based rules do not require arch specification (they are
# resolved by inode/path, not syscall arch), a single rule suffices, but
# some STIG/CIS benchmarks provide both -F arch=b32 and -F arch=b64 syscall
# based rules for execve. We use the 'path' method (recommended by RHEL8
# STIG for monitoring specific privileged command execution), which does
# not need arch duplication. Nonetheless, we ensure idempotency below.

add_rule_if_missing() {
    local rule="$1"
    local file="$2"

    if ! grep -qF -- "${rule}" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "${rule}" >> "${file}"
        echo "Added audit rule to ${file}: ${rule}"
    else
        echo "Audit rule already present in ${file}: ${rule}"
    fi
}

# Remove any pre-existing (possibly outdated/incorrect) semanage related
# privileged rules that do not match our exact expected rule, to avoid
# duplicate or conflicting entries referencing a different path or key.
if grep -q "semanage" "${RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    grep -v "semanage" "${RULES_FILE}" > "${RULES_FILE}.tmp" || true
    mv "${RULES_FILE}.tmp" "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Add the canonical rule (idempotent)
add_rule_if_missing "${RULE_B64}" "${RULES_FILE}"

# Ensure proper permissions on the rules file
chmod 0640 "${RULES_FILE}"

# Reload/merge rules into the running audit daemon configuration.
# Prefer augenrules if it manages rules.d, else fall back to auditctl.
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Load the rule directly into the running kernel audit rules as well,
    # in case augenrules is unavailable.
    auditctl -R "${RULES_FILE}" || true
fi

# Restart auditd to ensure persistence and immediate effect, if service
# management is available (systemctl). This ensures rules load on boot too.
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
        systemctl restart auditd || true
    else
        systemctl start auditd || true
    fi
fi

echo "Audit rule for semanage privileged command execution has been configured."

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
DCONF_LOCKS_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks"
DCONF_PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"
DCONF_PROFILE_FILE="${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}/user"
SETTINGS_FILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/00-screensaver"
LOCKS_FILE="${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}/00-screensaver"

if ! command -v dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf install -y dconf
    else
        echo "dconf command not found and dnf not available to install it." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}"

if [ ! -f "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" ]; then
    cat > "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" <<'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF
else
    if ! grep -qxF "user-db:user" "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"; then
        sed -i '1i user-db:user' "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
    fi
    if ! grep -qxF "system-db:local" "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"; then
        if grep -qxF "user-db:user" "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"; then
            sed -i "/^user-db:user$/a system-db:local" "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
        else
            echo "system-db:local" >> "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
        fi
    fi
fi

cat > "${SETTINGS_FILE}" <<'EOF'
[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-enabled=true
lock-delay=0

[org/gnome/desktop/session]
idle-delay=300
EOF

cat > "${LOCKS_FILE}" <<'EOF'
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-enabled
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay
/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay
EOF

chmod 644 "${SETTINGS_FILE}" "${LOCKS_FILE}"

#
# Remediation script: Configure audit rules to record unsuccessful attempts
# by regular users (auid >= 1000, excluding unset) to open files via the
# open syscall, capturing EACCES and EPERM failures, tagged with key "access".
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/access.rules"

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
if [ ! -d "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"
fi

# Ensure the rules file exists
if [ ! -f "${RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    touch "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

declare -a REQUIRED_RULES=(
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k access"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k access"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k access"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k access"
)

    if ! grep -Fxq -- "${rule}" "${RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then

    fi

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Configure audit rules to record unsuccessful attempts
# by regular users (auid >= 1000, excluding unset) to open files via the
# open syscall, capturing EACCES and EPERM failures, tagged with key "access".
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/access.rules"

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
if [ ! -d "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"
fi

# Ensure the rules file exists
if [ ! -f "${RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    touch "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Define the required audit rules
declare -a REQUIRED_RULES=(
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k access"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k access"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k access"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k access"
)

# Add each rule to the rules file only if it is not already present
for rule in "${REQUIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -Fxq -- "${rule}" "${RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "${rule}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
    fi
done

# Reload the audit rules so they take effect immediately, if augenrules is available
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Fallback: reload rules directly via auditctl
    auditctl -R "${RULES_FILE}" || true
fi

# Restart the audit daemon to ensure persistence across reboots, if possible
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
        systemctl restart auditd || true
    fi
fi

exit 0
```

    echo "Error: $SSHD_CONFIG not found." >&2

sed -i -E "/^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${SETTING}[[:space:]]+/Id" "$SSHD_CONFIG"

        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        if grep -qiE "^[[:space:]]*${SETTING}[[:space:]]+" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
            cp -p "$f" "${f}.bak.${TIMESTAMP}"
            sed -i -E "/^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${SETTING}[[:space:]]+/Id" "$f"
        fi
    done
fi

# Append the correct setting at the end of the main sshd_config
echo "${SETTING} ${VALUE}" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"

    echo "Error: sshd configuration test failed:" >&2
    cat /tmp/sshd_test_err >&2
    echo "Restoring backup..." >&2
    cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak.${TIMESTAMP}" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    rm -f /tmp/sshd_test_err
    exit 1

    if ! systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q "^sshd.service"; then
        SERVICE_NAME="ssh"
    fi
fi

# Restart the SSH service to apply changes
if systemctl is-active --quiet "$SERVICE_NAME"; then
    systemctl restart "$SERVICE_NAME"
else
    systemctl start "$SERVICE_NAME"
fi

# Ensure the service is enabled to persist across reboots
systemctl enable "$SERVICE_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# Final verification
CURRENT_VALUE=$(sshd -T 2>/dev/null | grep -i "^${SETTING}" | awk '{print $2}')

    echo "Error: Failed to verify ${SETTING} is set to 'no' in active sshd configuration." >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "Success: ${SETTING} is set to 'no' and SSH service has been restarted."
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation: Enforce GNOME lock-screen-on-smartcard-removal as a
# mandatory, system-wide dconf policy (RHEL 8 default GNOME desktop).
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times; only writes files if content
# needs to change, and always finishes with a dconf database rebuild.

set -euo pipefail

PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"
PROFILE_FILE="${PROFILE_DIR}/user"

DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
LOCKS_DIR="${DB_DIR}/locks"

SETTINGS_FILE="${DB_DIR}/00-smartcard-removal-action"
LOCK_FILE="${LOCKS_DIR}/00-smartcard-removal-action"

SETTINGS_CONTENT="[org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard]
removal-action='lock-screen'
"

LOCK_CONTENT="/org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard/removal-action
"

write_if_changed() {
    local target="$1"
    local content="$2"

    if [[ -f "$target" ]] && diff -q <(printf '%s' "$content") "$target" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        return 0
    fi

    printf '%s' "$content" > "$target"
    chmod 0644 "$target"
}

# Ensure required directories exist
mkdir -p "$PROFILE_DIR" "$DB_DIR" "$LOCKS_DIR"

# Ensure the dconf user profile references the local system database
if [[ ! -f "$PROFILE_FILE" ]]; then
    printf 'user-db:user\nsystem-db:local\n' > "$PROFILE_FILE"
    chmod 0644 "$PROFILE_FILE"
else
    grep -qxF 'user-db:user' "$PROFILE_FILE" || sed -i '1i user-db:user' "$PROFILE_FILE"
    grep -qxF 'system-db:local' "$PROFILE_FILE" || printf 'system-db:local\n' >> "$PROFILE_FILE"
fi

# Write the smartcard removal-action setting
write_if_changed "$SETTINGS_FILE" "$SETTINGS_CONTENT"

# Write the lock so ordinary users cannot override the setting
write_if_changed "$LOCK_FILE" "$LOCK_CONTENT"

# Rebuild the binary dconf databases so the change takes effect immediately
dconf update

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

PARAM="page_poison=1"
GRUB_FILE="/etc/default/grub"

# --- Ensure the parameter is present in /etc/default/grub for future kernels ---
if [ -f "$GRUB_FILE" ]; then
    if grep -q '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "$GRUB_FILE"; then
        if ! grep -E '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="[^"]*\b'"${PARAM}"'\b[^"]*"' "$GRUB_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            sed -i -E 's/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="([^"]*)"/GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="\1 '"${PARAM}"'"/' "$GRUB_FILE"
        fi
    else
        echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${PARAM}\"" >> "$GRUB_FILE"
    fi
else
    echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${PARAM}\"" > "$GRUB_FILE"
fi

# Normalize whitespace inside the quotes (remove leading/extra spaces)
sed -i -E 's/GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="[[:space:]]+/GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="/' "$GRUB_FILE"
sed -i -E 's/[[:space:]]+"/"/' "$GRUB_FILE"
sed -i -E 's/GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="([^"]*[^[:space:]"])[[:space:]]+([^"]*)"/GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="\1 \2"/' "$GRUB_FILE"

# --- Apply the parameter to all currently installed kernels persistently ---
while IFS= read -r kernel; do
    [ -z "$kernel" ] && continue
    if ! grubby --info="$kernel" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "\b${PARAM}\b"; then
        grubby --update-kernel="$kernel" --args="${PARAM}"
    fi
done < <(grubby --info=ALL 2>/dev/null | sed -n 's/^kernel="\(.*\)"$/\1/p')

# --- Regenerate the main grub.cfg so future kernel installs inherit the setting ---
if [ -d /sys/firmware/efi ]; then
    GRUB_CFG=$(find /boot/efi -maxdepth 3 -name grub.cfg 2>/dev/null | head -n1)
    [ -z "$GRUB_CFG" ] && GRUB_CFG="/boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg"
else
    GRUB_CFG="/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"
fi

if command -v grub2-mkconfig >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    grub2-mkconfig -o "$GRUB_CFG" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation script: Set default maximum password age (PASS_MAX_DAYS)
# for newly created accounts to 60 days on RHEL 8.
#
# This script is idempotent: it can be run multiple times without
# creating duplicate entries or changing the result once the desired
# state is reached.

set -euo pipefail

LOGIN_DEFS="/etc/login.defs"
PARAM="PASS_MAX_DAYS"
DESIRED_VALUE="60"

# Ensure the file exists
if [[ ! -f "${LOGIN_DEFS}" ]]; then
    echo "Error: ${LOGIN_DEFS} not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Backup the original file once, before any modification
if [[ ! -f "${LOGIN_DEFS}.bak" ]]; then
    cp -p "${LOGIN_DEFS}" "${LOGIN_DEFS}.bak"
fi

# Check if the parameter already exists (commented or not) and update/insert accordingly
if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+[0-9]+" "${LOGIN_DEFS}"; then
    # Parameter exists and is active; ensure it has the desired value
    current_value=$(grep -E "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+[0-9]+" "${LOGIN_DEFS}" | awk '{print $2}' | head -n1)
    if [[ "${current_value}" != "${DESIRED_VALUE}" ]]; then
        sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+[0-9]+.*|${PARAM}   ${DESIRED_VALUE}|" "${LOGIN_DEFS}"
    fi
elif grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+[0-9]+" "${LOGIN_DEFS}"; then
    # Parameter exists but is commented out; uncomment and set value
    sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+[0-9]+.*|${PARAM}   ${DESIRED_VALUE}|" "${LOGIN_DEFS}"
else
    # Parameter does not exist at all; append it
    printf '%s\t%s\n' "${PARAM}" "${DESIRED_VALUE}" >> "${LOGIN_DEFS}"
fi

# Verify the change was applied correctly

if [[ "${final_value}" != "${DESIRED_VALUE}" ]]; then

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation script: Set default maximum password age (PASS_MAX_DAYS)
# for newly created accounts to 60 days on RHEL 8.
#
# This script is idempotent: it can be run multiple times without
# creating duplicate entries or changing the result once the desired
# state is reached.

set -euo pipefail

LOGIN_DEFS="/etc/login.defs"
PARAM="PASS_MAX_DAYS"
DESIRED_VALUE="60"

# Ensure the file exists
if [[ ! -f "${LOGIN_DEFS}" ]]; then
    echo "Error: ${LOGIN_DEFS} not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Backup the original file once, before any modification
if [[ ! -f "${LOGIN_DEFS}.bak" ]]; then
    cp -p "${LOGIN_DEFS}" "${LOGIN_DEFS}.bak"
fi

# Check if the parameter already exists (commented or not) and update/insert accordingly
if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+[0-9]+" "${LOGIN_DEFS}"; then
    # Parameter exists and is active; ensure it has the desired value
    current_value=$(grep -E "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+[0-9]+" "${LOGIN_DEFS}" | awk '{print $2}' | head -n1)
    if [[ "${current_value}" != "${DESIRED_VALUE}" ]]; then
        sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+[0-9]+.*|${PARAM}   ${DESIRED_VALUE}|" "${LOGIN_DEFS}"
    fi
elif grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+[0-9]+" "${LOGIN_DEFS}"; then
    # Parameter exists but is commented out; uncomment and set value
    sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+[0-9]+.*|${PARAM}   ${DESIRED_VALUE}|" "${LOGIN_DEFS}"
else
    # Parameter does not exist at all; append it
    printf '%s\t%s\n' "${PARAM}" "${DESIRED_VALUE}" >> "${LOGIN_DEFS}"
fi

# Verify the change was applied correctly
final_value=$(grep -E "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]+[0-9]+" "${LOGIN_DEFS}" | awk '{print $2}' | head -n1)
if [[ "${final_value}" != "${DESIRED_VALUE}" ]]; then
    echo "Error: Failed to set ${PARAM} to ${DESIRED_VALUE} in ${LOGIN_DEFS}." >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "Success: ${PARAM} is set to ${DESIRED_VALUE} in ${LOGIN_DEFS}."

exit 0
```

        printf '%s\n' "${desired_line}" >> "${CHRONY_CONF}"

    fi

# --- Helper: disable (comment out) all active lines matching a directive ---
disable_directive() {
    local directive="$1"

    if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*${directive}([[:space:]]|$)" "${CHRONY_CONF}"; then
        sed -i -E "s/^([[:space:]]*${directive}([[:space:]].*)?)$/# \1  # disabled by client-only remediation/" "${CHRONY_CONF}"

        systemctl restart chronyd
    fi

#
# Remediation: Ensure the audit configuration is immutable (auditd -e 2)
# and that this setting persists across reboots via augenrules.
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
FINALIZE_FILE="${RULES_DIR}/99-finalize.rules"
IMMUTABLE_LINE="-e 2"

# Ensure the rules directory exists
mkdir -p "${RULES_DIR}"

# Remove any pre-existing "-e 2" (or "-e 1"/"-e 0" that would conflict)
# lines from any rules.d file other than the finalize file, to avoid
# duplicate or conflicting -e directives, and to guarantee that the
# immutable flag is the very last rule applied.
for f in "${RULES_DIR}"/*.rules; do
    [ -e "${f}" ] || continue
    if [ "${f}" != "${FINALIZE_FILE}" ]; then
        if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*-e[[:space:]]+[0-2][[:space:]]*$' "${f}" 2>/dev/null; then
            sed -i -E '/^[[:space:]]*-e[[:space:]]+[0-2][[:space:]]*$/d' "${f}"
        fi
    fi
done

# Ensure the finalize file exists
touch "${FINALIZE_FILE}"

# Remove any existing -e lines from the finalize file (idempotency: avoid
# duplicates or stale non-"2" values), then append the correct one.
if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*-e[[:space:]]+[0-2][[:space:]]*$' "${FINALIZE_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    sed -i -E '/^[[:space:]]*-e[[:space:]]+[0-2][[:space:]]*$/d' "${FINALIZE_FILE}"
fi

echo "${IMMUTABLE_LINE}" >> "${FINALIZE_FILE}"

# Ensure correct permissions on the rules file
chmod 0640 "${FINALIZE_FILE}"

# which is the mechanism auditd uses at startup on RHEL 8 to build
# its ruleset (via /usr/sbin/augenrules --load or the auditd service).
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load || true
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -e 2 || true
fi

# Verify the immutable flag is in effect on the currently running daemon
# (this may already report "2" if a previous run applied it, or may
# require a reboot to fully take effect per the audit subsystem design).
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -s 2>/dev/null | grep -qi 'enabled.*2' || true
fi

echo "Audit immutable configuration ensured in ${FINALIZE_FILE}."
echo "A system reboot is required for the immutable (-e 2) setting to fully take effect if not already active."

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Configure auditd to log file-deletion attempts
# via the unlink syscall for login UIDs >= 1000 (excluding unset UID),
# covering both 32-bit and 64-bit call tables, tagged with key "delete".
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/50-delete.rules"
KEY="delete"

# Define the required rules (64-bit and 32-bit)
RULE_64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S unlink -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k ${KEY}"
RULE_32="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S unlink -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k ${KEY}"

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Ensure the rules file exists
if [ ! -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    touch "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Function to add a rule if not already present
add_rule_if_missing() {
    local rule="$1"
    if ! grep -qxF "${rule}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"; then
        echo "${rule}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    fi
}

# Add the 64-bit rule (always applicable)
add_rule_if_missing "${RULE_64}"

# Add the 32-bit rule only if the architecture supports 32-bit syscalls
# (on x86_64 systems, the 32-bit compatibility layer is typically present,
# so we include it unconditionally as recommended by CIS/STIG guidance)
add_rule_if_missing "${RULE_32}"

# Deduplicate the rules file while preserving order (in case of manual edits)
if [ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    TMP_FILE=$(mktemp)
    awk '!seen[$0]++' "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" > "${TMP_FILE}"
    mv "${TMP_FILE}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    chmod 640 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Merge rules into the running audit configuration persistently
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" || true
fi

# Ensure auditd service is enabled and running to apply changes
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    systemctl is-active --quiet auditd && systemctl restart auditd || systemctl start auditd
fi

exit 0

# This script is idempotent and persists across reboots by modifying

DROPIN_FILE="${DROPIN_DIR}/gtls-defaultnetstreamdriver.conf"
SETTING_LINE='global(DefaultNetstreamDriver="gtls")'

if ! command -v rsyslogd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "rsyslogd not found. Installing rsyslog package..."
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf install -y rsyslog
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum install -y rsyslog
    else
        echo "No supported package manager found to install rsyslog." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Ensure the /etc/rsyslog.d directory exists
if [ ! -d "$DROPIN_DIR" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$DROPIN_DIR"
fi

# Function to remove any existing (possibly incorrect or duplicate)
# DefaultNetstreamDriver settings from rsyslog.conf and all files
# under /etc/rsyslog.d/*.conf, to avoid conflicting or duplicate
# directives that could override or conflict with our setting.
remove_existing_setting() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        # Remove standalone $DefaultNetstreamDriver legacy directive lines
        sed -i '/^\s*\$DefaultNetstreamDriver\b/Id' "$file"
        # Remove any global() blocks that only set DefaultNetstreamDriver
        # (simple single-line global() directive matching our exact key)
        sed -i '/^\s*global(\s*DefaultNetstreamDriver\s*=/Id' "$file"
    fi
}

# Clean up rsyslog.conf
remove_existing_setting "$RSYSLOG_CONF"

# Clean up all existing drop-in files except the one we manage,
# to prevent duplicate or conflicting directives.
if [ -d "$DROPIN_DIR" ]; then
    for f in "$DROPIN_DIR"/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        if [ "$f" != "$DROPIN_FILE" ]; then
            remove_existing_setting "$f"
        fi
    done
fi

# Write (or overwrite) our dedicated drop-in configuration file
# idempotently with the correct setting.
cat > "$DROPIN_FILE" <<EOF
# This file is managed by an automated remediation script.
# It ensures that rsyslog uses the GnuTLS (gtls) driver as the
# default network stream driver for secure transport of logs
# to remote hosts.
${SETTING_LINE}
EOF

chmod 0644 "$DROPIN_FILE"

# Verify the setting is present and correct in the drop-in file
if ! grep -qF "$SETTING_LINE" "$DROPIN_FILE"; then

        rm -f /tmp/rsyslog_check.log
        exit 1

    rm -f /tmp/rsyslog_check.log
fi

# Restart/reload rsyslog service to apply changes, if systemd is present
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl is-enabled rsyslog.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl is-active rsyslog.service >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        systemctl restart rsyslog.service
    fi
fi

echo "rsyslog DefaultNetstreamDriver is set to 'gtls' (TLS/GnuTLS) and persisted."
exit 0

# to prevent duplicate or conflicting directives.

# idempotently with the correct setting.

    if systemctl is-enabled rsyslog.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl is-active rsyslog.service >/dev/null 2>&1; then

    current_value=$(echo "${existing_line}" | sed -E "s/^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*([0-9]+).*/\1/")

    if [ -z "${current_value}" ] || [ "${current_value}" -lt "${MIN_VALUE}" ]; then
        sed -i -E "s/^([[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*).*/\1${MIN_VALUE}/" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    fi

    sed -i -E "/^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=/d" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    printf '%s = %s\n' "${PARAM}" "${MIN_VALUE}" >> "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"

final_value=$(grep -E "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}" | tail -n 1 | sed -E "s/^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*([0-9]+).*/\1/")

    exit 1

            authselect select "${current_profile}" with-faillock --force >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        fi
    fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure SSH server drops unresponsive clients exactly at the

#

# final configuration and does not produce duplicate or conflicting entries.

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
BACKUP_SUFFIX=".bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

    exit 1
fi

    echo "Error: $SSHD_CONFIG not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# case-insensitive matches and any leading whitespace/comment markers.
set_sshd_directive() {
    local key="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local file="$3"

    if grep -Eiq "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]+" "$file"; then

        # desired directive, and remove any duplicate active/commented
        # occurrences further down to avoid ambiguity.

    else

    fi

    # Remove any additional duplicate active directives beyond the first,
    # keeping configuration deterministic and idempotent.
    local count
    count=$(grep -Eic "^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]+" "$file" || true)
    if [[ "$count" -gt 1 ]]; then
        local first_seen=0
        local tmpfile

        while IFS= read -r line; do
            if [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]+ ]]; then
                if [[ "$first_seen" -eq 0 ]]; then
                    echo "$line" >> "$tmpfile"
                    first_seen=1
                fi
                # skip subsequent duplicates
            else

            fi
        done < "$file"

    fi

    for f in "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR"/*.conf; do

# value, leave it untouched.

if [[ -z "${current_interval:-}" || "${current_interval}" == "0" ]]; then

fi

# Enforce zero additional keep-alive probes: the connection must terminate
# exactly at the configured idle interval.

    systemctl reload-or-restart sshd
elif systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q '^ssh\.service'; then

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure core dumps are never written to persistent storage.
# Configures systemd-coredump (via /etc/systemd/coredump.conf) to disable
# storage of core dumps, persisting across reboots.

set -euo pipefail

COREDUMP_CONF="/etc/systemd/coredump.conf"
COREDUMP_CONF_D="/etc/systemd/coredump.conf.d"
DROPIN_FILE="${COREDUMP_CONF_D}/99-disable-storage.conf"

# Ensure the main config file exists (it ships with systemd by default)
if [[ ! -f "${COREDUMP_CONF}" ]]; then
    touch "${COREDUMP_CONF}"
fi

# Function to idempotently set a key=value pair under [Coredump] section
set_coredump_option() {
    local file="$1"
    local key="$2"
    local value="$3"

    # Ensure [Coredump] section exists
    if ! grep -q '^\[Coredump\]' "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
        printf '\n[Coredump]\n' >> "${file}"
    fi

    # If the key already exists (commented or not), replace it; otherwise add it
    if grep -Eq "^[#[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=" "${file}"; then
        sed -i -E "s|^[#[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=.*|${key}=${value}|" "${file}"
    else
        # Insert right after [Coredump] section header
        sed -i -E "/^\[Coredump\]/a ${key}=${value}" "${file}"
    fi
}

# Preferred approach: use a drop-in directory so main config remains untouched
# and changes are clearly identifiable and idempotent.
mkdir -p "${COREDUMP_CONF_D}"

cat > "${DROPIN_FILE}" <<'EOF'
[Coredump]
Storage=none
ProcessSizeMax=0
EOF

# Also update the main config file to keep it consistent (idempotent),
# in case drop-in directory is not honored in some environment.
set_coredump_option "${COREDUMP_CONF}" "Storage" "none"
set_coredump_option "${COREDUMP_CONF}" "ProcessSizeMax" "0"

# Reload systemd-coredump configuration (no dedicated service to restart;
# settings are read by systemd-coredump on next crash. However, if the
# systemd-journald or systemd-coredump socket/service is active, restart
# journald to ensure any cached settings are refreshed).
systemctl daemon-reexec >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# Additionally, disable core dump size limits via /etc/security/limits.conf
# and /etc/security/limits.d to prevent shell-level core dumps as a defense
# in depth measure (idempotent).
LIMITS_DROPIN="/etc/security/limits.d/99-disable-coredump.conf"
cat > "${LIMITS_DROPIN}" <<'EOF'
*    hard    core    0
*    soft    core    0
EOF

# Ensure sysctl also prevents suid core dumps (defense in depth, idempotent)
SYSCTL_DROPIN="/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-coredump.conf"
cat > "${SYSCTL_DROPIN}" <<'EOF'
fs.suid_dumpable = 0
kernel.core_pattern = |/bin/false
EOF

sysctl --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

echo "Core dump storage has been disabled persistently (Storage=none in ${DROPIN_FILE} and ${COREDUMP_CONF})."

echo "Core dump storage has been disabled persistently (Storage=none in ${DROPIN_FILE} and ${COREDUMP_CONF})."

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Add audit watch rules for failed authentication
# and login/logout event log files, tagged with key "logins".
#
# Idempotent - safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/logins.rules"
AUGENRULES_BIN="$(command -v augenrules || true)"
AUDITCTL_BIN="$(command -v auditctl || true)"

# Files to monitor and the audit rule definitions.
# Format: "path"
declare -a WATCH_FILES=(
    "/var/log/faillock"
    "/var/log/lastlog"
    "/var/log/tallylog"
    "/var/run/faillock"
)

# Ensure the rules directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Ensure the rules file exists
touch "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

add_rule_if_missing() {
    local path="$1"
    local rule_line="-w ${path} -p wa -k logins"

    if [[ -e "${path}" ]] || [[ -d "$(dirname "${path}")" ]]; then
        if ! grep -Fxq "${rule_line}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "${rule_line}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
            echo "Added audit rule: ${rule_line}"
        else
            echo "Audit rule already present: ${rule_line}"
        fi
    else
        echo "Skipping ${path}: path and parent directory do not exist."
    fi
}

for f in "${WATCH_FILES[@]}"; do
    add_rule_if_missing "${f}"
done

# Deduplicate the rules file, preserving order, in case of prior partial runs
if [[ -s "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]]; then
    TMP_FILE="$(mktemp)"
    awk '!seen[$0]++' "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" > "${TMP_FILE}"
    mv "${TMP_FILE}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

chmod 640 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
chown root:root "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Load rules using whichever mechanism the audit daemon uses.
# Prefer augenrules (merges rules.d into audit.rules) if available.
if [[ -n "${AUGENRULES_BIN}" ]]; then
    echo "Loading audit rules via augenrules..."
    "${AUGENRULES_BIN}" --load
elif [[ -n "${AUDITCTL_BIN}" ]]; then
    echo "augenrules not found; loading rules directly via auditctl..."
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        [[ -z "${line}" ]] && continue
        # shellcheck disable=SC2086
        "${AUDITCTL_BIN}" ${line} || true
    done < "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
else
    echo "Warning: Neither augenrules nor auditctl found. Rules written but not loaded." >&2
fi

# Ensure auditd service is enabled and will apply rules on boot
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl is-enabled auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl enable auditd
fi

# Verify rules are currently active (best effort, non-fatal if auditctl unavailable)
if [[ -n "${AUDITCTL_BIN}" ]]; then
    echo "Current audit rules matching key 'logins':"
    "${AUDITCTL_BIN}" -l | grep -F "key=logins" || echo "No active 'logins' rules found (may require reboot or auditd restart with immutable config)."
fi

echo "Audit remediation for login/failed-auth file monitoring complete."

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Enforce minimum digit requirement in password quality policy
# on RHEL 8 systems via pwquality.conf (dcredit setting).
#
# This script is idempotent - it can be run multiple times without
# causing duplicate entries or unintended side effects.

set -euo pipefail

PWQUALITY_CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
PWQUALITY_DIR="/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d"
SETTING_NAME="dcredit"
REQUIRED_VALUE="-1"
DROPIN_FILE="${PWQUALITY_DIR}/50-digit-requirement.conf"

# Ensure the main pwquality.conf file exists
if [[ ! -f "${PWQUALITY_CONF}" ]]; then
    touch "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
    chmod 0644 "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
fi

# Prefer using a drop-in configuration directory if supported (RHEL 8 supports this),
# as it is cleaner and avoids modifying the main config file directly.
# Create the drop-in directory if it doesn't exist.
if [[ ! -d "${PWQUALITY_DIR}" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "${PWQUALITY_DIR}"
    chmod 0755 "${PWQUALITY_DIR}"
fi

# Function to set or update a pwquality setting in a given file
set_pwquality_setting() {
    local file="$1"
    local key="$2"
    local value="$3"

    # If the file doesn't exist, create it
    if [[ ! -f "${file}" ]]; then
        touch "${file}"
        chmod 0644 "${file}"
    fi

    # Remove any existing (commented or uncommented) lines for this key
    # to avoid duplicates or conflicting settings, then add the correct one.
    if grep -qP "^\s*#?\s*${key}\s*=" "${file}"; then
        sed -i -E "s/^\s*#?\s*(${key})\s*=.*/\1 = ${value}/" "${file}"
    else
        printf '%s = %s\n' "${key}" "${value}" >> "${file}"
    fi
}

# Apply the digit credit requirement in the drop-in file
set_pwquality_setting "${DROPIN_FILE}" "${SETTING_NAME}" "${REQUIRED_VALUE}"

# Ensure the main pwquality.conf does not have a conflicting dcredit setting
# that would override the drop-in configuration. If present, normalize it
# to match the desired value as well (belt-and-suspenders approach for
# systems where drop-in directories might not be read by all consumers).
if grep -qP "^\s*#?\s*${SETTING_NAME}\s*=" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"; then
    sed -i -E "s/^\s*#?\s*(${SETTING_NAME})\s*=.*/\1 = ${REQUIRED_VALUE}/" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
else
    printf '%s = %s\n' "${SETTING_NAME}" "${REQUIRED_VALUE}" >> "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
fi

# Validate final state: ensure at least one active (uncommented) dcredit
# setting with the correct value exists in either location.
FINAL_CHECK=$(grep -hP "^\s*${SETTING_NAME}\s*=\s*${REQUIRED_VALUE}\s*$" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}" "${DROPIN_FILE}" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)

if [[ "${FINAL_CHECK}" -lt 1 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to enforce ${SETTING_NAME} = ${REQUIRED_VALUE} in pwquality configuration." >&2
    exit 1
fi

exit 0

    logger -t "${LOG_TAG}" "ERROR: $*" 2>/dev/null || true
}

is_fips_enabled() {
    if command -v fips-mode-setup >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if fips-mode-setup --check 2>/dev/null | grep -qi "FIPS mode is enabled"; then
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    return 1
}

check_kernel_cmdline_fips() {
    if grep -qw "fips=1" /proc/cmdline 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi
    return 1
}

check_proc_fips_flag() {
    if [ -f /proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled ]; then
        val="$(cat /proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
        if [ "${val}" = "1" ]; then
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    return 1
}

ensure_dracut_fips_installed() {
    if ! rpm -q dracut-fips >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_info "Installing dracut-fips package."
        yum install -y dracut-fips
    else
        log_info "dracut-fips package already installed."
    fi
}

ensure_fips_mode_setup_available() {
    if ! command -v fips-mode-setup >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_info "fips-mode-setup not found, installing crypto-policies-scripts."
        yum install -y crypto-policies-scripts || true
    fi
    if ! command -v fips-mode-setup >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_error "fips-mode-setup command still not available after installation attempt."
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

ensure_grub_fips_param() {
    local grub_cfg_files=(
        "/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"
        "/boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg"
    )
    local grubenv_files=(
        "/boot/grub2/grubenv"
        "/boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grubenv"
    )

    if ! grep -qw "fips=1" /etc/default/grub 2>/dev/null; then
        log_info "Ensuring fips=1 is present in GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX."
        if grep -q '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' /etc/default/grub 2>/dev/null; then
            if ! grep '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' /etc/default/grub | grep -qw "fips=1"; then
                sed -ri 's/^(GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=")([^"]*)"/\1\2 fips=1"/' /etc/default/grub
            fi
        else
            echo 'GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="fips=1"' >> /etc/default/grub
        fi
    else
        log_info "fips=1 already present in /etc/default/grub."
    fi

    # Determine boot mode and root/boot devices for fips-mode-setup / grub2-mkconfig
    local grub_target_cfg=""
    for f in "${grub_cfg_files[@]}"; do
        if [ -f "${f}" ]; then
            grub_target_cfg="${f}"
            break
        fi
    done

    if [ -n "${grub_target_cfg}" ]; then
        log_info "Regenerating GRUB configuration: ${grub_target_cfg}"
        grub2-mkconfig -o "${grub_target_cfg}" || log_error "grub2-mkconfig failed for ${grub_target_cfg}"
    fi

    for gf in "${grubenv_files[@]}"; do
        if [ -f "${gf}" ]; then
            log_info "Found grubenv at ${gf}"
        fi
    done
}

rebuild_initramfs() {
    log_info "Rebuilding initramfs for the current kernel with FIPS support."
    local kver
    kver="$(uname -r)"
    dracut -f --regenerate-all || log_error "dracut regeneration reported issues, continuing."
}

remediate_fips_mode() {
    log_info "FIPS mode is not fully enabled. Beginning remediation."

    ensure_dracut_fips_installed
    ensure_fips_mode_setup_available

    log_info "Running fips-mode-setup --enable."
    if ! fips-mode-setup --enable; then
        log_error "fips-mode-setup --enable reported an error."
    fi

    ensure_grub_fips_param
    rebuild_initramfs

    if [ ! -f /etc/system-fips ]; then
        log_info "Creating /etc/system-fips marker file."
        echo "1" > /etc/system-fips
    fi

    log_info "Remediation actions applied. A reboot is required for FIPS mode to take full effect."
}

verify_system_fips_marker() {
    if [ ! -f /etc/system-fips ]; then

}

verify_crypto_policy() {
    if command -v update-crypto-policies >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local current_policy
        current_policy="$(update-crypto-policies --show 2>/dev/null || echo "")"

            log_info "System-wide crypto policy already set to FIPS."
        fi

#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
PARAMETER="GSSAPIAuthentication"
VALUE="no"
TIMESTAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

if [[ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}" ]]; then
    echo "Error: ${SSHD_CONFIG} not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Backup the main config before making changes
cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak.${TIMESTAMP}"

# Function to comment out all active occurrences of the parameter
# (case-insensitive) in a given file, leaving other lines untouched.
comment_out_existing() {
    local file="$1"
    if [[ -f "${file}" ]] && grep -Eiq "^[[:space:]]*${PARAMETER}[[:space:]]+" "${file}"; then
        sed -i -E "s/^[[:space:]]*(${PARAMETER}[[:space:]]+.*)$/# \1/I" "${file}"
    fi
}

# Comment out any existing settings in the main sshd_config file
comment_out_existing "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

# Comment out any existing settings in drop-in configuration files,
# since those are processed before the main config and would otherwise
# take precedence.
if [[ -d "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}" ]]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' dropin_file; do
        cp -p "${dropin_file}" "${dropin_file}.bak.${TIMESTAMP}"
        comment_out_existing "${dropin_file}"
    done < <(find "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' -print0 2>/dev/null)
fi

# Ensure the desired setting is present at the end of the main config file
if ! grep -Eiq "^[[:space:]]*${PARAMETER}[[:space:]]+${VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"; then
    printf '\n%s %s\n' "${PARAMETER}" "${VALUE}" >> "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
fi

# Validate the sshd configuration before attempting to reload the service
if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sshd -t; then
        echo "Error: sshd configuration test failed after modification." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Reload the sshd service to apply changes, if the service is present and active
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl list-unit-files 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^sshd\.service'; then
        if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd; then
            systemctl reload sshd || systemctl restart sshd
        fi
    elif systemctl list-unit-files 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^ssh\.service'; then
        if systemctl is-active --quiet ssh; then
            systemctl reload ssh || systemctl restart ssh
        fi
    fi
fi

echo "GSSAPIAuthentication has been set to '${VALUE}' in ${SSHD_CONFIG}."

#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
KEY="PrintLastLog"
VALUE="yes"

# Ensure the main sshd_config file exists
if [ ! -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]; then
    echo "Error: $SSHD_CONFIG not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Function to check if a directive is set (uncommented) in a file
directive_set_in_file() {
    local file="$1"
    grep -qiE "^[[:space:]]*${KEY}[[:space:]]+" "$file" 2>/dev/null
}

# Collect all files that might define this directive: main config + any Include'd drop-in files
declare -a FILES_TO_CHECK=("$SSHD_CONFIG")

if [ -d "$CONFIG_DIR" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
        FILES_TO_CHECK+=("$f")
    done < <(find "$CONFIG_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -name '*.conf' -type f -print0 2>/dev/null)
fi

FOUND_CORRECT="false"

for f in "${FILES_TO_CHECK[@]}"; do
    if directive_set_in_file "$f"; then
        # Get the last matching value (sshd uses last occurrence in effective config precedence,
        # but within a single file, later lines override earlier ones as well)
        current_value=$(grep -iE "^[[:space:]]*${KEY}[[:space:]]+" "$f" | tail -n1 | awk '{print $2}')
        current_value_lower=$(echo "$current_value" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')

        if [ "$current_value_lower" = "yes" ]; then
            FOUND_CORRECT="true"
        else
            # Comment out incorrect/conflicting settings to avoid ambiguity,
            # then we will add the correct one in the main config file.
            sed -i -E "s/^([[:space:]]*${KEY}[[:space:]]+.*)$/#\1/I" "$f"
        fi
    fi
done

if [ "$FOUND_CORRECT" != "true" ]; then
    # Ensure any existing (commented or not) instance of the key in the main file
    # is handled: since we may have commented out incorrect ones above, just append
    # the correct directive if not already present as an active correct line.
    if grep -qiE "^[[:space:]]*${KEY}[[:space:]]+yes[[:space:]]*$" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
        : # Already correct in main file
    else
        {
            echo ""
            echo "# Remediation: Ensure SSH shows last login information (PrintLastLog)"
            echo "${KEY} ${VALUE}"
        } >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    fi
fi

# Validate the resulting sshd configuration syntax
if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sshd -t; then
        echo "Error: sshd configuration test failed after remediation." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Restart sshd to apply changes, if the service is active
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd 2>/dev/null; then
        systemctl reload sshd 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart sshd
    elif systemctl is-active --quiet ssh 2>/dev/null; then
        systemctl reload ssh 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart ssh
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
DROPIN_FILE="${CONFIG_DIR}/50-x11forwarding.conf"

# Ensure the sshd_config.d directory exists (RHEL 8 default)
if [ ! -d "$CONFIG_DIR" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$CONFIG_DIR"
fi

# Function to comment out or remove existing X11Forwarding directives
# in a given file, matching case-insensitively and allowing leading whitespace
disable_existing_directive() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        # Comment out any active X11Forwarding line (yes or no, any case)
        sed -i -E 's/^[[:space:]]*([Xx]11[Ff]orwarding[[:space:]]+.*)$/#\1/' "$file"
    fi
}

# Disable any existing X11Forwarding directives in main sshd_config
disable_existing_directive "$SSHD_CONFIG"

# Disable any existing X11Forwarding directives in all drop-in files
if [ -d "$CONFIG_DIR" ]; then
    for f in "$CONFIG_DIR"/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        disable_existing_directive "$f"
    done
fi

# Create or update the dedicated drop-in file to enforce X11Forwarding no
if [ -f "$DROPIN_FILE" ]; then
    if grep -Eiq '^[[:space:]]*X11Forwarding[[:space:]]+no[[:space:]]*$' "$DROPIN_FILE"; then
        :
    else
        sed -i -E '/^[[:space:]]*[Xx]11[Ff]orwarding\b/d' "$DROPIN_FILE"
        echo "X11Forwarding no" >> "$DROPIN_FILE"
    fi
else
    printf '%s\n' "X11Forwarding no" > "$DROPIN_FILE"
fi

chmod 0600 "$DROPIN_FILE"
chown root:root "$DROPIN_FILE"

# Validate the SSH daemon configuration syntax
if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sshd -t; then
        echo "ERROR: sshd configuration test failed after remediation." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Restart or reload the SSH service to apply changes
SERVICE_NAME="sshd"
if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q "^${SERVICE_NAME}\.service"; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet "$SERVICE_NAME"; then
        systemctl reload "$SERVICE_NAME" 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart "$SERVICE_NAME"
    else
        systemctl restart "$SERVICE_NAME"
    fi
    systemctl enable "$SERVICE_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

fi

exit 0

#

# This script is idempotent and can be run multiple times safely.

set -eu

AIDE_CONF="/etc/aide.conf"
BACKUP_SUFFIX=".bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# List of audit tool binaries that must be tracked by AIDE
AUDIT_TOOLS=(
    "/sbin/auditctl"
    "/sbin/auditd"
    "/sbin/ausearch"
    "/sbin/aureport"
    "/sbin/autrace"
    "/sbin/augenrules"
    "/sbin/audispd"
    "/sbin/rsyslogd"
)

# The AIDE rule/selection to apply to each audit tool.
# p = permissions, i = inode, n = number of links, u = user, g = group,
# s = size, b = block count, m = mtime, c = ctime, acl, selinux, xattrs,
# sha256 = content hash
AIDE_RULE="p+i+n+u+g+s+b+m+c+acl+selinux+xattrs+sha256"

if ! command -v aide >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf install -y aide
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum install -y aide
    else
        echo "Error: No supported package manager found to install AIDE." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Ensure the AIDE configuration file exists
if [ ! -f "${AIDE_CONF}" ]; then
    echo "Error: AIDE configuration file ${AIDE_CONF} not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Backup the current AIDE configuration before modifying it
cp -p "${AIDE_CONF}" "${AIDE_CONF}${BACKUP_SUFFIX}"

# Function to add a monitoring rule for a given path if not already present
add_aide_rule() {
    local tool_path="$1"
    local rule="$2"

    local escaped_path
    escaped_path=$(printf '%s' "${tool_path}" | sed -e 's/[.[\*^$/]/\\&/g')

    if grep -E "^[[:space:]]*${escaped_path}[[:space:]]+" "${AIDE_CONF}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        # Entry exists; ensure it has the correct rule. If not matching, update it.
        if grep -E "^[[:space:]]*${escaped_path}[[:space:]]+${rule}[[:space:]]*$" "${AIDE_CONF}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            # Already correctly configured

            sed -i -E "s|^[[:space:]]*${escaped_path}[[:space:]]+.*$|${tool_path} ${rule}|" "${AIDE_CONF}"

    else
        # Entry does not exist; append it
        echo "${tool_path} ${rule}" >> "${AIDE_CONF}"

        if [ -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz ]; then

        elif [ -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new ]; then
            mv -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new /var/lib/aide/aide.db
        fi
    else
        # No existing database; initialize one
        aide --init
        if [ -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz ]; then
            mv -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz
        elif [ -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new ]; then
            mv -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new /var/lib/aide/aide.db
        fi
    fi
fi

echo "AIDE configuration for audit tools integrity monitoring is complete."
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Enforce pam_pwquality maxrepeat=3
# Ensures passwords are rejected if they contain more than 3
# consecutive identical characters (no run of 4 or more same chars).
#
# This script is idempotent - it can be run multiple times safely.

set -euo pipefail

PWQUALITY_CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
PWQUALITY_DIR="/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d"
DESIRED_PARAM="maxrepeat"
DESIRED_VALUE="3"

# Ensure the pwquality.conf file exists
if [[ ! -f "${PWQUALITY_CONF}" ]]; then
    touch "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
fi

# Function to set a parameter in a given conf file, idempotently.
# It will:
#  - Uncomment and update the line if the parameter exists (commented or not)
#  - Add the parameter if it doesn't exist at all
set_pwquality_param() {
    local file="$1"
    local param="$2"
    local value="$3"

    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
        # Parameter exists uncommented; update its value if different
        if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${value}[[:space:]]*$" "${file}"; then
            sed -i -E "s/^[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=.*/${param} = ${value}/" "${file}"
        fi
    elif grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
        # Parameter exists but commented out; uncomment and set value
        sed -i -E "s/^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=.*/${param} = ${value}/" "${file}"
    else
        # Parameter doesn't exist at all; append it
        # Ensure file ends with a newline before appending
        if [[ -s "${file}" ]] && [[ "$(tail -c1 "${file}")" != "" ]]; then
            echo "" >> "${file}"
        fi
        echo "${param} = ${value}" >> "${file}"
    fi
}

# Apply the setting in the main pwquality.conf file
set_pwquality_param "${PWQUALITY_CONF}" "${DESIRED_PARAM}" "${DESIRED_VALUE}"

# If a pwquality.conf.d directory exists (drop-in configs), check for
# conflicting settings there and remediate them too, to ensure the
# effective configuration is correct system-wide.
if [[ -d "${PWQUALITY_DIR}" ]]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' dropin_file; do
        if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${DESIRED_PARAM}[[:space:]]*=" "${dropin_file}" 2>/dev/null; then
            set_pwquality_param "${dropin_file}" "${DESIRED_PARAM}" "${DESIRED_VALUE}"
        fi
    done < <(find "${PWQUALITY_DIR}" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name "*.conf" -print0 2>/dev/null)
fi

# Verify the final effective value using pwquality's own tool if available
if command -v pwscore >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    :
fi

# Verify by re-reading the main config file for the final assertion
if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${DESIRED_PARAM}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${DESIRED_VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"; then
    echo "Successfully enforced ${DESIRED_PARAM} = ${DESIRED_VALUE} in ${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to enforce ${DESIRED_PARAM} = ${DESIRED_VALUE} in ${PWQUALITY_CONF}" >&2
    exit 1
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure audit rules exist to monitor write and
# attribute-change events on /etc/security/opasswd (the old-password store).
#
# This script is idempotent: it checks for existing rules before adding
# them, and configures both audit tools that may be in use:
#   - augenrules (rule-compilation program, using rules.d fragments)
#   - auditctl (direct rule-loading utility)
#
# Rules are tagged with the key "audit_rules_usergroup_modification" style
# identifier specific to this file: "identity" is commonly used, but per
# requirements we use a clear, identifiable key: "audit_time_rules" is not
# appropriate either; we use "audit_rules_login_events"-style naming is not
# accurate. We use a clear, purpose-specific key: "audit_password_changes".
#

set -euo pipefail

readonly AUDIT_FILE="/etc/security/opasswd"
readonly AUDIT_KEY="audit_password_changes"
readonly RULES_D_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
readonly RULES_D_FILE="${RULES_D_DIR}/50-audit_password_changes.rules"
readonly AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

# The canonical rule line(s) we want to ensure are present.
# Using -p wa to capture write and attribute change events.
build_rule() {
    local arch_suffix="$1"
    echo "-w ${AUDIT_FILE} -p wa -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"
}

RULE_LINE="-w ${AUDIT_FILE} -p wa -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"

ensure_line_in_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local line="$2"

    # Create the file if it doesn't exist
    if [[ ! -f "${file}" ]]; then
        touch "${file}"
    fi

    # Check if the exact rule line already exists (ignoring leading/trailing whitespace)
    if ! grep -Fxq -- "${line}" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "${line}" >> "${file}"
        return 0
    fi
    return 1
}

main() {
    local changed_rules_d=0
    local changed_auditctl_rules=0

    # Ensure the rules.d directory exists (should exist by default on RHEL 8)
    if [[ ! -d "${RULES_D_DIR}" ]]; then
        mkdir -p "${RULES_D_DIR}"
    fi

    # 1. Configure the rule for augenrules (rules.d fragment file)
    if ensure_line_in_file "${RULES_D_FILE}" "${RULE_LINE}"; then
        changed_rules_d=1
        echo "Added audit rule to ${RULES_D_FILE}"
    else
        echo "Audit rule already present in ${RULES_D_FILE}"
    fi

    # 2. Configure the rule for auditctl (direct rule-loading utility),
    #    which reads from /etc/audit/audit.rules for persistence across
    #    reboots in setups that don't rely on augenrules regeneration.
    if [[ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]] || [[ -d "$(dirname "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}")" ]]; then
        if ensure_line_in_file "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" "${RULE_LINE}"; then
            changed_auditctl_rules=1
            echo "Added audit rule to ${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
        else
            echo "Audit rule already present in ${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
        fi
    fi

    # 3. Determine which audit rule loading mechanism is active and load
    #    the rules immediately so they take effect without requiring a
    #    reboot, while also ensuring persistence.

    # Regenerate rules via augenrules if it exists, to merge rules.d
    # fragments into audit.rules and load them.
    if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        augenrules --load || true
    fi

    # If auditctl is available, ensure the rule is loaded into the
    # running kernel audit rules as well (idempotent: check first).
    if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -Fq -- "-w ${AUDIT_FILE}" ; then
            auditctl -w "${AUDIT_FILE}" -p wa -k "${AUDIT_KEY}" || true
        fi
    fi

    if [[ ${changed_rules_d} -eq 1 || ${changed_auditctl_rules} -eq 1 ]]; then
        echo "Audit configuration updated for monitoring ${AUDIT_FILE}."
    else
        echo "Audit configuration for ${AUDIT_FILE} was already compliant."
    fi
}

main "$@"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure the audit daemon records use of the kernel
# module-loading facility (init_module / finit_module syscalls) by all
# users with an audit UID of 1000 or greater, for both 32-bit and 64-bit
# syscall entry points as appropriate to the system architecture.
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/module_chng.rules"
KEY="module_chng"

# Determine architecture(s) to cover.
ARCH=$(uname -m)

# Build the list of rule lines needed for this system.
declare -a RULE_LINES=()

if [ "$ARCH" = "x86_64" ] || [ "$ARCH" = "aarch64" ] || [ "$ARCH" = "ppc64" ] || [ "$ARCH" = "ppc64le" ] || [ "$ARCH" = "s390x" ]; then
    RULE_LINES+=("-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S init_module,finit_module -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}")
    RULE_LINES+=("-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S init_module,finit_module -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}")
else
    RULE_LINES+=("-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S init_module,finit_module -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}")
fi

# Ensure the rules directory exists.
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Ensure the rules file exists.
touch "$RULES_FILE"

# Insert each required rule line if it is not already present.
for line in "${RULE_LINES[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -Fxq -- "$line" "$RULES_FILE"; then
        echo "$line" >> "$RULES_FILE"
    fi
done

# Regenerate and load the audit rules so they take effect immediately
# and are guaranteed to be loaded by auditd at startup.
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "$RULES_FILE" || true
fi

# Restart or reload the audit daemon service if available/active,
# to ensure persistence and immediate effect without requiring reboot.
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
        systemctl restart auditd || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Enforce encrypted (TLS) off-loading of audit/log
# records via rsyslog on RHEL 8. Idempotent - safe to run multiple times.
#

set -euo pipefail

RSYSLOG_CONF="/etc/rsyslog.conf"
RSYSLOG_D_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
TLS_CONF_FILE="${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}/99-tls-forwarding.conf"

CA_CERT="/etc/pki/tls/certs/ca.pem"
CLIENT_CERT="/etc/pki/tls/certs/client-cert.pem"
CLIENT_KEY="/etc/pki/tls/private/client-key.pem"

REMOTE_HOST="__REPLACE_WITH_REMOTE_LOG_COLLECTOR__"
REMOTE_PORT="6514"

BACKUP_SUFFIX=".bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

log() {
    echo "[remediation] $*"
}

ensure_rsyslog_installed() {
    if ! rpm -q rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "Installing rsyslog package..."
        yum install -y rsyslog
    fi

    if ! rpm -q rsyslog-gnutls >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "Installing rsyslog-gnutls package for TLS support..."
        yum install -y rsyslog-gnutls
    fi
}

remove_plaintext_forwarding_rules() {
    # Remove any existing plaintext forwarding directives (@host:port or @@host:port)
    # from rsyslog.conf and any files in rsyslog.d, except our TLS config file.
    local f
    for f in "$RSYSLOG_CONF" "$RSYSLOG_D_DIR"/*.conf; do
        [ -f "$f" ] || continue
        [ "$f" = "$TLS_CONF_FILE" ] && continue

        if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*(\*\.\*|[a-zA-Z0-9,.;!=_*-]+)[[:space:]]+@[^@].*' "$f" 2>/dev/null \
           || grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*(\*\.\*|[a-zA-Z0-9,.;!=_*-]+)[[:space:]]+@@[^@].*' "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
            log "Found plaintext forwarding rule(s) in $f, disabling them..."
            cp -p "$f" "${f}${BACKUP_SUFFIX}"
            sed -ri \
                -e 's/^([[:space:]]*[a-zA-Z0-9,.;!=_*-]+[[:space:]]+)@@?([^@].*)$/#REMEDIATED-PLAINTEXT-FORWARD# \1@\2/' \
                "$f"
        fi
    done
}

ensure_cert_paths_exist() {
    local missing=0
    for p in "$CA_CERT" "$CLIENT_CERT" "$CLIENT_KEY"; do
        if [ ! -f "$p" ]; then
            log "WARNING: Expected certificate/key file not found: $p"
            missing=1
        fi
    done
    if [ "$missing" -eq 1 ]; then
        log "WARNING: One or more TLS certificate files are missing."
        log "The rsyslog TLS configuration will be written, but you MUST"
        log "provision the CA cert, client cert, and client key at the"
        log "paths referenced in ${TLS_CONF_FILE} for TLS forwarding to work."
    fi
}

write_tls_forwarding_config() {
    mkdir -p "$RSYSLOG_D_DIR"

    if [ -f "$TLS_CONF_FILE" ]; then
        cp -p "$TLS_CONF_FILE" "${TLS_CONF_FILE}${BACKUP_SUFFIX}"
    fi

    cat > "$TLS_CONF_FILE" <<EOF
# File managed by remediation script - enforces encrypted (TLS) log
# forwarding to remote collector. Do not add plaintext forwarding rules.

global(
    DefaultNetstreamDriver="gtls"
    DefaultNetstreamDriverCAFile="${CA_CERT}"
    DefaultNetstreamDriverCertFile="${CLIENT_CERT}"
    DefaultNetstreamDriverKeyFile="${CLIENT_KEY}"
)

action(
    type="omfwd"
    Target="${REMOTE_HOST}"
    Port="${REMOTE_PORT}"
    Protocol="tcp"
    StreamDriver="gtls"
    StreamDriverMode="1"
    StreamDriverAuthMode="x509/name"
    StreamDriverPermittedPeers="${REMOTE_HOST}"
    action.resumeRetryCount="100"
    queue.type="LinkedList"
    queue.filename="fwd_tls_queue"
    queue.saveOnShutdown="on"
)
EOF

    chmod 640 "$TLS_CONF_FILE"
    chown root:root "$TLS_CONF_FILE"

    log "Wrote TLS forwarding configuration to $TLS_CONF_FILE"
}

validate_rsyslog_config() {
    if command -v rsyslogd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if ! rsyslogd -N1 -f "$RSYSLOG_CONF" >/tmp/rsyslog_validate.log 2>&1; then
            log "ERROR: rsyslog configuration validation failed. See /tmp/rsyslog_validate.log"
            cat /tmp/rsyslog_validate.log
            exit 1
        fi
        log "rsyslog configuration validated successfully."
    fi
}

enable_and_restart_rsyslog() {
    systemctl enable rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    if systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog; then
        log "Restarting rsyslog to apply changes..."
        systemctl restart rsyslog
    else
        log "Starting rsyslog..."
        systemctl start rsyslog
    fi

    if ! systemctl is-enabled --quiet rsyslog; then
        log "ERROR: rsyslog service is not enabled to persist across reboots."
        exit 1
    fi

    if ! systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog; then
        log "ERROR: rsyslog service failed to start."
        exit 1
    fi
}

main() {
    if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
        echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
