```bash
#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Enforce encrypted (TLS) off-loading of audit/log
# records via rsyslog on RHEL 8. Idempotent - safe to run multiple times.
#

set -euo pipefail

RSYSLOG_CONF="/etc/rsyslog.conf"
RSYSLOG_D_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
TLS_CONF_FILE="${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}/99-tls-forwarding.conf"

CA_CERT="/etc/pki/tls/certs/ca.pem"
CLIENT_CERT="/etc/pki/tls/certs/client-cert.pem"
CLIENT_KEY="/etc/pki/tls/private/client-key.pem"

REMOTE_HOST="__REPLACE_WITH_REMOTE_LOG_COLLECTOR__"
REMOTE_PORT="6514"

BACKUP_SUFFIX=".bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

log() {
    echo "[remediation] $*"
}

ensure_rsyslog_installed() {
    if ! rpm -q rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "Installing rsyslog package..."
        yum install -y rsyslog
    fi

    if ! rpm -q rsyslog-gnutls >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "Installing rsyslog-gnutls package for TLS support..."
        yum install -y rsyslog-gnutls
    fi
}

remove_plaintext_forwarding_rules() {
    # Remove any existing plaintext forwarding directives (@host:port or @@host:port)
    # from rsyslog.conf and any files in rsyslog.d, except our TLS config file.
    local f
    for f in "$RSYSLOG_CONF" "$RSYSLOG_D_DIR"/*.conf; do
        [ -f "$f" ] || continue
        [ "$f" = "$TLS_CONF_FILE" ] && continue

        if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*(\*\.\*|[a-zA-Z0-9,.;!=_*-]+)[[:space:]]+@[^@].*' "$f" 2>/dev/null \
           || grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*(\*\.\*|[a-zA-Z0-9,.;!=_*-]+)[[:space:]]+@@[^@].*' "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
            log "Found plaintext forwarding rule(s) in $f, disabling them..."
            cp -p "$f" "${f}${BACKUP_SUFFIX}"
            sed -ri \
                -e 's/^([[:space:]]*[a-zA-Z0-9,.;!=_*-]+[[:space:]]+)@@?([^@].*)$/#REMEDIATED-PLAINTEXT-FORWARD# \1@\2/' \
                "$f"
        fi
    done
}

ensure_cert_paths_exist() {
    local missing=0
    for p in "$CA_CERT" "$CLIENT_CERT" "$CLIENT_KEY"; do
        if [ ! -f "$p" ]; then
            log "WARNING: Expected certificate/key file not found: $p"
            missing=1
        fi
    done
    if [ "$missing" -eq 1 ]; then
        log "WARNING: One or more TLS certificate files are missing."
        log "The rsyslog TLS configuration will be written, but you MUST"
        log "provision the CA cert, client cert, and client key at the"
        log "paths referenced in ${TLS_CONF_FILE} for TLS forwarding to work."
    fi
}

write_tls_forwarding_config() {
    mkdir -p "$RSYSLOG_D_DIR"

    if [ -f "$TLS_CONF_FILE" ]; then
        cp -p "$TLS_CONF_FILE" "${TLS_CONF_FILE}${BACKUP_SUFFIX}"
    fi

    cat > "$TLS_CONF_FILE" <<EOF
# File managed by remediation script - enforces encrypted (TLS) log
# forwarding to remote collector. Do not add plaintext forwarding rules.

global(
    DefaultNetstreamDriver="gtls"
    DefaultNetstreamDriverCAFile="${CA_CERT}"
    DefaultNetstreamDriverCertFile="${CLIENT_CERT}"
    DefaultNetstreamDriverKeyFile="${CLIENT_KEY}"
)

action(
    type="omfwd"
    Target="${REMOTE_HOST}"
    Port="${REMOTE_PORT}"
    Protocol="tcp"
    StreamDriver="gtls"
    StreamDriverMode="1"
    StreamDriverAuthMode="x509/name"
    StreamDriverPermittedPeers="${REMOTE_HOST}"
    action.resumeRetryCount="100"
    queue.type="LinkedList"
    queue.filename="fwd_tls_queue"
    queue.saveOnShutdown="on"
)
EOF

    chmod 640 "$TLS_CONF_FILE"
    chown root:root "$TLS_CONF_FILE"

    log "Wrote TLS forwarding configuration to $TLS_CONF_FILE"
}

validate_rsyslog_config() {
    if command -v rsyslogd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if ! rsyslogd -N1 -f "$RSYSLOG_CONF" >/tmp/rsyslog_validate.log 2>&1; then
            log "ERROR: rsyslog configuration validation failed. See /tmp/rsyslog_validate.log"
            cat /tmp/rsyslog_validate.log
            exit 1
        fi
        log "rsyslog configuration validated successfully."
    fi
}

enable_and_restart_rsyslog() {
    systemctl enable rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    if systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog; then
        log "Restarting rsyslog to apply changes..."
        systemctl restart rsyslog
    else
        log "Starting rsyslog..."
        systemctl start rsyslog
    fi

    if ! systemctl is-enabled --quiet rsyslog; then
        log "ERROR: rsyslog service is not enabled to persist across reboots."
        exit 1
    fi

    if ! systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog; then
        log "ERROR: rsyslog service failed to start."
        exit 1
    fi
}

main() {
    if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
        echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    log "Starting remediation: enforce encrypted (TLS) log off-loading via rsyslog."

    ensure_rsyslog_installed
    remove_plaintext_forwarding_rules
    ensure_cert_paths_exist
    write_tls_forwarding_config
    validate_rsyslog_config
    enable_and_restart_rsyslog

    log "Remediation complete. Log forwarding is now configured to use TLS."
    log "Rem

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure auditd monitors /etc/sudoers.d directory
# for write and attribute changes, tagged for identification, and that
# the rule persists across reboots regardless of whether the system
# uses audit rules generated via augenrules or directly in audit.rules.

set -euo pipefail

readonly AUDIT_DIR="/etc/sudoers.d"
readonly KEY_NAME="actions"
readonly RULE_FILE_RULESD="/etc/audit/rules.d/audit_sudoers_d.rules"
readonly RULE_FILE_DIRECT="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

# Construct the canonical rule line
build_rule() {
    printf -- '-w %s -p wa -k %s' "${AUDIT_DIR}" "${KEY_NAME}"
}

RULE_LINE="$(build_rule)"

# Ensure the target directory exists (it should on RHEL 8, but be safe)
if [[ ! -d "${AUDIT_DIR}" ]]; then
    echo "Warning: ${AUDIT_DIR} does not exist. Creating it so the audit watch is valid." >&2
    mkdir -p "${AUDIT_DIR}"
    chmod 750 "${AUDIT_DIR}"
fi

# Function to idempotently append a rule to a given file if not already present
ensure_rule_in_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local rule="$2"

    # Create the file if it doesn't exist
    if [[ ! -f "${file}" ]]; then
        touch "${file}"
        chmod 640 "${file}"
    fi

    # Check if an equivalent rule already exists (ignoring exact spacing differences)
    if grep -Eq -- "^-w[[:space:]]+${AUDIT_DIR//\//\\/}[[:space:]]+-p[[:space:]]+wa[[:space:]]+-k[[:space:]]+${KEY_NAME}[[:space:]]*$" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Rule already present in ${file}, skipping."
        return 0
    fi

    # Also check for a rule with the same path/perm but different key, and skip only exact matches
    if grep -Fq -- "${rule}" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Rule already present in ${file}, skipping."
        return 0
    fi

    echo "Adding audit rule to ${file}"
    printf '%s\n' "${rule}" >> "${file}"
}

# 1. Ensure rule is present in /etc/audit/rules.d/ (used by augenrules)
if [[ -d /etc/audit/rules.d ]]; then
    ensure_rule_in_file "${RULE_FILE_RULESD}" "${RULE_LINE}"
fi

# 2. Ensure rule is present in /etc/audit/audit.rules (used when rules are loaded directly)
ensure_rule_in_file "${RULE_FILE_DIRECT}" "${RULE_LINE}"

# 3. If augenrules is available, regenerate the compiled rules so they take effect
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Running augenrules --load to merge and load rules."
    augenrules --load || echo "Warning: augenrules --load returned non-zero status." >&2
fi

# 4. Attempt to load the rule immediately via auditctl for current session,
#    without requiring a reboot, using the running kernel audit rules.
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -Eq -- "^-w[[:space:]]+${AUDIT_DIR//\//\\/}[[:space:]]+-p[[:space:]]+wa[[:space:]]+-k[[:space:]]+${KEY_NAME}[[:space:]]*$"; then
        echo "Loading rule into the running kernel audit rule list via auditctl."
        auditctl -w "${AUDIT_DIR}" -p wa -k "${KEY_NAME}" || \
            echo "Warning: Could not load rule via auditctl (may require augenrules/service restart)." >&2
    else
        echo "Rule already active in the running kernel audit rule list."
    fi
fi

# 5. Restart or reload auditd service if possible to ensure persistence path is consistent
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
        echo "Auditd service is active; rules will fully apply after a reload or reboot."
    fi
fi

echo "Audit rule for ${AUDIT_DIR} (write/attribute changes, key=${KEY_NAME}) ensured."
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure audit records are generated for all uses of
# the chacl command by users with auid >= 1000 and by root (auid = 0).
# Idempotent and compatible with both augenrules (rules.d) and auditctl
# (direct-load /etc/audit/audit.rules) configurations.
#

set -euo pipefail

readonly AUDIT_BINARY="/usr/bin/chacl"
readonly RULES_D_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
readonly RULES_D_FILE="${RULES_D_DIR}/perm_mod_chacl.rules"
readonly AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
readonly AUDITCTL_BIN="/sbin/auditctl"
readonly AUGENRULES_BIN="/sbin/augenrules"

if [[ ! -x "${AUDIT_BINARY}" ]]; then
    # chacl may live elsewhere on some systems; try to locate it
    if command -v chacl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        AUDIT_BINARY="$(command -v chacl)"
    fi
fi

# The two rules required: one for regular users (auid>=1000, excluding unset)
# and one for root (auid=0).
RULE_USER="-a always,exit -F path=${AUDIT_BINARY} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"
RULE_ROOT="-a always,exit -F path=${AUDIT_BINARY} -F perm=x -F auid=0 -F key=perm_mod"

add_rule_if_missing() {
    local file="$1"
    local rule="$2"

    if [[ -f "${file}" ]] && grep -Fxq -- "${rule}" "${file}"; then
        return 0
    fi

    # Ensure file exists
    touch "${file}"
    echo "${rule}" >> "${file}"
}

# --- 1. Handle the "rules.d" (augenrules) load method ---
if [[ -d "${RULES_D_DIR}" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "${RULES_D_DIR}"
    add_rule_if_missing "${RULES_D_FILE}" "${RULE_USER}"
    add_rule_if_missing "${RULES_D_FILE}" "${RULE_ROOT}"
fi

# --- 2. Handle the direct-load method (/etc/audit/audit.rules) ---

    "${AUGENRULES_BIN}" --load || true

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure audit records are generated for all uses of
# the chacl command by users with auid >= 1000 and by root (auid = 0).
# Idempotent and compatible with both augenrules (rules.d) and auditctl
# (direct-load /etc/audit/audit.rules) configurations.
#

set -euo pipefail

readonly AUDIT_BINARY="/usr/bin/chacl"
readonly RULES_D_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
readonly RULES_D_FILE="${RULES_D_DIR}/perm_mod_chacl.rules"
readonly AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
readonly AUDITCTL_BIN="/sbin/auditctl"
readonly AUGENRULES_BIN="/sbin/augenrules"

if [[ ! -x "${AUDIT_BINARY}" ]]; then
    # chacl may live elsewhere on some systems; try to locate it
    if command -v chacl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        AUDIT_BINARY="$(command -v chacl)"
    fi
fi

# The two rules required: one for regular users (auid>=1000, excluding unset)
# and one for root (auid=0).
RULE_USER="-a always,exit -F path=${AUDIT_BINARY} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"
RULE_ROOT="-a always,exit -F path=${AUDIT_BINARY} -F perm=x -F auid=0 -F key=perm_mod"

add_rule_if_missing() {
    local file="$1"
    local rule="$2"

    if [[ -f "${file}" ]] && grep -Fxq -- "${rule}" "${file}"; then
        return 0
    fi

    # Ensure file exists
    touch "${file}"
    echo "${rule}" >> "${file}"
}

# --- 1. Handle the "rules.d" (augenrules) load method ---
if [[ -d "${RULES_D_DIR}" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "${RULES_D_DIR}"
    add_rule_if_missing "${RULES_D_FILE}" "${RULE_USER}"
    add_rule_if_missing "${RULES_D_FILE}" "${RULE_ROOT}"
fi

# --- 2. Handle the direct-load method (/etc/audit/audit.rules) ---
if [[ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]] || [[ ! -d "${RULES_D_DIR}" ]]; then
    touch "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    add_rule_if_missing "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" "${RULE_USER}"
    add_rule_if_missing "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" "${RULE_ROOT}"
fi

# --- 3. Reload rules so the change takes effect immediately, matching
#         whichever load method is in use on this system. ---
if [[ -x "${AUGENRULES_BIN}" ]] && [[ -d "${RULES_D_DIR}" ]]; then
    "${AUGENRULES_BIN}" --load || true
elif [[ -x "${AUDITCTL_BIN}" ]]; then
    "${AUDITCTL_BIN}" -R "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" || true
fi

# --- 4. Verify the rules are currently loaded in the kernel audit rules;
#         if not (e.g., audit daemon not running), this is non-fatal since
#         the persistent rule files have already been updated. ---
if [[ -x "${AUDITCTL_BIN}" ]]; then
    if ! "${AUDITCTL_BIN}" -l 2>/dev/null | grep -Fq -- "path=${AUDIT_BINARY}" ; then
        : # Not currently loaded (e.g., auditd not running); files are persisted for next boot.
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Enforce minimum password length of 15 characters
# on RHEL 8 systems via /etc/security/pwquality.conf
#
# This script is idempotent and safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

PWQUALITY_CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
PWQUALITY_DIR="/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d"
MIN_LEN=15

# Ensure the main config file exists
if [[ ! -f "${PWQUALITY_CONF}" ]]; then
    touch "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
fi

# Backup original file once, if backup doesn't already exist
if [[ ! -f "${PWQUALITY_CONF}.bak" ]]; then
    cp -p "${PWQUALITY_CONF}" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}.bak"
fi

# Function to set a parameter in pwquality.conf idempotently
set_pwquality_param() {
    local param="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local file="$3"

    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=" "${file}"; then
        # Parameter exists (commented or not) - update its value
        sed -ri "s/^[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=.*/${param} = ${value}/" "${file}"
    elif grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=" "${file}"; then
        # Parameter exists but commented out - uncomment and set value
        sed -ri "s/^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=.*/${param} = ${value}/" "${file}"
    else
        # Parameter does not exist - append it
        echo "${param} = ${value}" >> "${file}"
    fi
}

# Ensure the pwquality.conf.d directory exists (RHEL 8 supports drop-in configs)
mkdir -p "${PWQUALITY_DIR}"

# Prefer using a drop-in file for cleaner, idempotent management
DROPIN_FILE="${PWQUALITY_DIR}/50-minlen.conf"

if [[ -f "${DROPIN_FILE}" ]]; then
    set_pwquality_param "minlen" "${MIN_LEN}" "${DROPIN_FILE}"
else
    echo "minlen = ${MIN_LEN}" > "${DROPIN_FILE}"
fi

chmod 0644 "${DROPIN_FILE}"

# Additionally, ensure minlen is not set to a conflicting weaker value
# directly in the main pwquality.conf (belt-and-suspenders approach)
if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*minlen[[:space:]]*=" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"; then
    current_val=$(grep -E "^[[:space:]]*minlen[[:space:]]*=" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}" | tail -1 | sed -E 's/^[[:space:]]*minlen[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*//')
    if [[ "${current_val}" -lt "${MIN_LEN}" ]] 2>/dev/null; then
        set_pwquality_param "minlen" "${MIN_LEN}" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
    fi
fi

# Verify the setting is correctly applied via pwquality's own tools if available
if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 && python3 -c "import pwquality" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    python3 -c "
import pwquality
pwq = pwquality.PWQSettings()
pwq.read_config()
if pwq.minlen < ${MIN_LEN}:
    raise SystemExit('minlen not properly enforced')
" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

echo "Password minimum length policy (minlen = ${MIN_LEN}) has been enforced in:"
echo "  - ${DROPIN_FILE}"
echo "Configuration persists across reboots via ${PWQUALITY_DIR}."

exit 0

if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*minlen[[:space:]]*=" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"; then

if pwq.minlen < ${MIN_LEN}:

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure auditd logs execution of setsebool command
# for all users with real UID >= 1000 (privileged command usage tracking)
#
# This script is idempotent and safe to re-run.

set -euo pipefail

RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules"
AUDIT_CMD_PATH="$(command -v setsebool || echo /usr/sbin/setsebool)"

# Ensure the audit rules directory and file exist
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d
touch "${RULES_FILE}"

# Determine architecture-specific rule lines
# On RHEL 8, auditctl syntax for privileged commands typically uses -F path=<cmd> -F perm=x
# combined with auid checks and key tagging.

RULE_KEY="privileged-priv_change"

# Build the expected rule line(s)
# We use arch-independent syntax since 'path' based rules do not require arch qualifiers
EXPECTED_RULE="-a always,exit -F path=${AUDIT_CMD_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${RULE_KEY}"

# Function to check if a matching rule already exists (ignoring exact whitespace differences)
rule_exists() {
    grep -Fxq -- "${EXPECTED_RULE}" "${RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null
}

# Remove any old/incomplete rules referencing setsebool to avoid duplicates or conflicting rules
if grep -q "path=${AUDIT_CMD_PATH}" "${RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    # Remove lines that reference this path but do not exactly match expected rule
    TMP_FILE="$(mktemp)"
    grep -v "path=${AUDIT_CMD_PATH}" "${RULES_FILE}" > "${TMP_FILE}" || true
    mv "${TMP_FILE}" "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Append the expected rule if not already present
if ! rule_exists; then
    echo "${EXPECTED_RULE}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Ensure file permissions are correct
chmod 0640 "${RULES_FILE}"
chown root:root "${RULES_FILE}"

# Reload audit rules to apply immediately without requiring reboot
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "${RULES_FILE}" || true
fi

# Verify the rule is active in the running audit configuration
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! auditctl -l | grep -Fq -- "${EXPECTED_RULE}"; then
        # Attempt to reload again in case of ordering issues
        augenrules --load 2>/dev/null || auditctl -R "${RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

#
# Remediation script: Enforce a minimum 4-second delay after a failed

#
# This script is idempotent - it can be run multiple times safely.

set -euo pipefail

LOGIN_DEFS="/etc/login.defs"
PARAM="FAIL_DELAY"
DESIRED_VALUE="4"

# Ensure the file exists
if [[ ! -f "$LOGIN_DEFS" ]]; then
    echo "Error: $LOGIN_DEFS does not exist." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Backup the original file before making changes (only if backup doesn't already exist)
BACKUP_FILE="${LOGIN_DEFS}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d)"
if [[ ! -f "$BACKUP_FILE" ]]; then
    cp -p "$LOGIN_DEFS" "$BACKUP_FILE"
fi

# Check if FAIL_DELAY parameter exists (commented or uncommented, with any spacing)
if grep -qE '^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*FAIL_DELAY[[:space:]]+' "$LOGIN_DEFS"; then
    # Parameter exists in some form - check current active (uncommented) value
    CURRENT_LINE=$(grep -E '^[[:space:]]*FAIL_DELAY[[:space:]]+' "$LOGIN_DEFS" | tail -n 1 || true)

    if [[ -n "$CURRENT_LINE" ]]; then
        CURRENT_VALUE=$(echo "$CURRENT_LINE" | awk '{print $2}')
        if [[ "$CURRENT_VALUE" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && [[ "$CURRENT_VALUE" -ge "$DESIRED_VALUE" ]]; then
            # Already compliant
            echo "FAIL_DELAY is already set to $CURRENT_VALUE (>= $DESIRED_VALUE). No changes needed."
            exit 0
        else
            # Uncommented but value is wrong - fix it
            sed -i -E "s/^([[:space:]]*)FAIL_DELAY[[:space:]]+[0-9]+(.*)$/\1FAIL_DELAY\t${DESIRED_VALUE}\2/" "$LOGIN_DEFS"
            echo "Updated existing FAIL_DELAY setting to ${DESIRED_VALUE}."
        fi
    else
        # Only commented instances exist - replace the last commented occurrence
        # or append if replacement fails
        if grep -qE '^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*FAIL_DELAY[[:space:]]+' "$LOGIN_DEFS"; then

            echo "Uncommented and set FAIL_DELAY to ${DESIRED_VALUE}."
        fi
    fi
else
    # Parameter does not exist at all - append it
    {
        echo ""
        echo "# Enforce minimum delay after failed login attempt (security remediation)"
        echo -e "FAIL_DELAY\t${DESIRED_VALUE}"
    } >> "$LOGIN_DEFS"
    echo "Added FAIL_DELAY setting with value ${DESIRED_VALUE}."
fi

# Final verification
FINAL_VALUE=$(grep -E '^[[:space:]]*FAIL_DELAY[[:space:]]+' "$LOGIN_DEFS" | tail -n 1 | awk '{print $2}')

if [[ "$FINAL_VALUE" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && [[ "$FINAL_VALUE" -ge "$DESIRED_VALUE" ]]; then
    echo "Remediation successful: FAIL_DELAY is now set to ${FINAL_VALUE} seconds in ${LOGIN_DEFS}."
    exit 0
else
    echo "Error: Failed to verify FAIL_DELAY setting after remediation." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Configure audit rules to record failed attempts
# to truncate files (EACCES/EPERM) for all regular users (UID >= 1000)
# on RHEL 8, for both 32-bit and 64-bit syscall ABIs, tagged with key "access".
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/access.rules"
AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
if [[ ! -d "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"
fi

# Ensure the rules file exists
if [[ ! -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]]; then
    touch "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Determine supported architectures on this system (b32/b64)
declare -a ARCHES=()
if command -v getconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    LONG_BIT=$(getconf LONG_BIT 2>/dev/null || echo "64")
else
    LONG_BIT="64"
fi

# Always attempt both b32 and b64 rules, as required (where applicable).
# auditctl/augenrules will simply not match irrelevant syscalls on
# architectures that don't support them, but per requirements we add both.
ARCHES=("b32" "b64")

declare -a ERRORS=("EACCES" "EPERM")

# Build the list of required rule lines
declare -a REQUIRED_RULES=()
for arch in "${ARCHES[@]}"; do
    for err in "${ERRORS[@]}"; do
        REQUIRED_RULES+=("-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S truncate,ftruncate -F exit=-${err} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k access")
    done
done

# Function to check if a rule already exists in the rules file
rule_exists() {
    local rule="$1"
    grep -Fxq -- "${rule}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
}

CHANGED=0

for rule in "${REQUIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! rule_exists "${rule}"; then
        echo "${rule}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
done

# Ensure file permissions are correct (audit rule files should be 0600)
chmod 0600 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Load the rules using the daemon's normal rule-loading mechanism
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" || true
fi

# Ensure auditd is running and rules persist across reboot
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl is-enabled auditd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        :
    else
        systemctl enable auditd || true
    fi

    if systemctl is-active auditd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if [[ "${CHANGED}" -eq 1 ]]; then
            systemctl restart auditd || true
        fi
    else
        systemctl start auditd || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

            echo "Stopping ${PACKAGE_NAME} service..."
            systemctl stop "${PACKAGE_NAME}.service"
        fi

        if systemctl is-enabled --quiet "${PACKAGE_NAME}.service" 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "Disabling ${PACKAGE_NAME} service..."
            systemctl disable "${PACKAGE_NAME}.service"
        fi
    fi

    # Remove the package
    if command -v dnf &>/dev/null; then
        dnf remove -y "${PACKAGE_NAME}"
    elif command -v yum &>/dev/null; then
        yum remove -y "${PACKAGE_NAME}"
    else
        echo "Error: Neither dnf nor yum package manager found." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    echo "Package '${PACKAGE_NAME}' has been successfully removed."
else
    echo "Package '${PACKAGE_NAME}' is not installed. No action needed."
fi

# Final verification
if rpm -q "${PACKAGE_NAME}" &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Error: Failed to remove '${PACKAGE_NAME}'. It is still present on the system." >&2
    exit 1
else
    echo "Verification successful: '${PACKAGE_NAME}' is absent from the system."
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure audit rules exist for lchown (32-bit and 64-bit)
# to track ownership changes by users with UID >= 1000 (excluding unset).
#
# This script is idempotent: running it multiple times will not create
# duplicate entries, and it will ensure the rules are loaded into the
# running audit configuration as well as persisted across reboots.

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_KEY="perm_mod"
RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/50-perm_mod-lchown.rules"

# Define the required audit rules
declare -a REQUIRED_RULES=(
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S lchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S lchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"
)

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
if [ ! -d /etc/audit/rules.d ]; then
    mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d
fi

# Ensure the rules file exists
if [ ! -f "${RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    touch "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Add each required rule if it is not already present (idempotent)
for rule in "${REQUIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -qF -- "${rule}" "${RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "${rule}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
    fi
done

# Remove any duplicate lines that may have been introduced
if [ -f "${RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    sort -u "${RULES_FILE}" -o "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Reload/merge audit rules using augenrules if available (preferred on RHEL 8)
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Fallback: load the rules file directly into the running configuration
    auditctl -R "${RULES_FILE}" || true
fi

# Verify the rules are active in the running audit configuration;
# if not, attempt to load them explicitly with auditctl for immediate effect.
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    CURRENT_RULES="$(auditctl -l 2>/dev/null || true)"
    for rule in "${REQUIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
        # Normalize "auid!=unset" to the numeric equivalent for comparison,
        # since auditctl -l typically reports the resolved value.
        normalized_rule="${rule//auid!=unset/auid!=4294967295}"
        if ! echo "${CURRENT_RULES}" | grep -qF -- "-S lchown" ; then
            auditctl ${rule} 2>/dev/null || true
        elif ! echo "${CURRENT_RULES}" | grep -qF -- "${normalized_rule#* }" ; then
            auditctl ${rule} 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    done
fi

# Ensure auditd service is enabled and running so persistence takes effect
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl is-enabled auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl enable auditd
    systemctl is-active auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl start auditd
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure system-wide crypto policy is set to DEFAULT
# on RHEL 8 systems. Idempotent - safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

readonly DESIRED_POLICY="DEFAULT"
readonly CONFIG_FILE="/etc/crypto-policies/config"
readonly UPDATE_CMD="/usr/bin/update-crypto-policies"

main() {
    if ! command -v "${UPDATE_CMD}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Error: ${UPDATE_CMD} not found. Is crypto-policies package installed?" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    local current_policy=""
    if [ -f "${CONFIG_FILE}" ]; then
        current_policy="$(tr -d '[:space:]' < "${CONFIG_FILE}")"
    fi

    if [ "${current_policy}" = "${DESIRED_POLICY}" ]; then
        echo "System-wide crypto policy already set to ${DESIRED_POLICY} in ${CONFIG_FILE}."
    else
        echo "Setting system-wide crypto policy to ${DESIRED_POLICY}..."
        "${UPDATE_CMD}" "${DESIRED_POLICY}"
    fi

    # Verify the runtime/show output matches expected policy as well
    local show_output
    show_output="$("${UPDATE_CMD}" --show 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]')"

    if [ "${show_output}" != "${DESIRED_POLICY}" ]; then
        echo "Policy mismatch detected via --show (got '${show_output}'). Re-applying..."
        "${UPDATE_CMD}" "${DESIRED_POLICY}"
        show_output="$("${UPDATE_CMD}" --show 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]')"
        if [ "${show_output}" != "${DESIRED_POLICY}" ]; then
            echo "Error: Failed to apply ${DESIRED_POLICY} crypto policy." >&2
            exit 1
        fi
    fi

    # Double-check the config file reflects the desired policy
    if [ -f "${CONFIG_FILE}" ]; then
        current_policy="$(tr -d '[:space:]' < "${CONFIG_FILE}")"
        if [ "${current_policy}" != "${DESIRED_POLICY}" ]; then
            echo "Error: ${CONFIG_FILE} does not reflect ${DESIRED_POLICY} after update." >&2
            exit 1
        fi
    else
        echo "Error: ${CONFIG_FILE} not found after applying policy." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    echo "System-wide crypto policy successfully set and verified as ${DESIRED_POLICY}."
    echo "Note: Some services may require a reboot or restart to pick up the new policy."
}

main "$@"

#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULE_FILE="${RULES_DIR}/modules.rules"
KEY="modules"

mkdir -p "${RULES_DIR}"
touch "${RULE_FILE}"

ARCH=$(uname -m)

case "${ARCH}" in
    x86_64)
        ARCH_LIST="b32 b64"
        ;;
    aarch64)
        ARCH_LIST="b64"
        ;;
    s390x)
        ARCH_LIST="b32 b64"
        ;;
    ppc64|ppc64le)
        ARCH_LIST="b32 b64"
        ;;
    i686|i386)
        ARCH_LIST="b32"
        ;;
    *)
        ARCH_LIST="b32 b64"
        ;;
esac

for arch in ${ARCH_LIST}; do
    RULE="-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S init_module,finit_module -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"

    if ! grep -Fxq -- "${RULE}" "${RULE_FILE}"; then
        echo "${RULE}" >> "${RULE_FILE}"
    fi
done

sort -u -o "${RULE_FILE}" "${RULE_FILE}"

if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        [ -z "${line}" ] && continue
        auditctl ${line} || true
    done < "${RULE_FILE}"
fi

if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
        systemctl try-restart auditd || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

#
# Remediation script: Configure GDM login screen banner (message-text)
# Ensures the banner is set to the approved organizational warning/consent
# text, enforced as the active value, and locked via dconf so
# non-privileged users cannot override it. Idempotent and persistent
# across reboots.

set -euo pipefail

# --- Configuration ---------------------------------------------------------

BANNER_TEXT="You are accessing a U.S. Government (USG) Information System (IS) that is provided for USG-authorized use only. By using this IS (which includes any device attached to this IS), you consent to the following conditions: -The USG routinely intercepts and monitors communications on this IS for purposes including, but not limited to, penetration testing, COMSEC monitoring, network operations and defense, personnel misconduct (PM), law enforcement (LE), and counterintelligence (CI) investigations. -At any time, the USG may inspect and seize data stored on this IS. -Communications using, or data stored on, this IS are not private, are subject to routine monitoring, interception, and search, and may be disclosed or used for any USG-authorized purpose. -This IS includes security measures (e.g., authentication and access controls) to protect USG interests--not for your personal benefit or privacy. -Notwithstanding the above, using this IS does not constitute consent to PM, LE or CI investigative searching or monitoring of the content of privileged communications, or work product, related to personal representation or services by attorneys, psychotherapists, or clergy, and their assistants. Such communications and work product are private and confidential. See User Agreement for details."

DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/gdm.d"
DCONF_KEYFILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/01-banner-message"
DCONF_LOCK_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/gdm.d/locks"
DCONF_LOCK_FILE="${DCONF_LOCK_DIR}/01-banner-message-lock"
DCONF_PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"
DCONF_PROFILE_FILE="${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}/gdm"

# --- Helper functions -------------------------------------------------------

log() {
    echo "[remediation] $*"
}

require_root() {
    if [[ "${EUID}" -ne 0 ]]; then
        echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
}

ensure_packages() {
    if ! rpm -q gdm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "GDM package not found. Installing gdm..."
        yum install -y gdm
    fi

    if ! rpm -q dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "dconf package not found. Installing dconf..."
        yum install -y dconf
    fi
}

ensure_dconf_profile() {
    mkdir -p "${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}"

    local desired_content
    desired_content=$(cat <<'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:gdm
file-db:/usr/share/gdm/greeter-dconf-defaults
EOF
)

    if [[ ! -f "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" ]] || ! diff -q <(printf '%s\n' "${desired_content}") "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "Configuring dconf profile at ${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
        printf '%s\n' "${desired_content}" > "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
    else
        log "dconf profile already correctly configured."
    fi
}

ensure_dconf_db_dir() {
    mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
    mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCK_DIR}"
}

escape_for_gvariant() {
    # Escapes double quotes and backslashes for embedding into a
    # dconf/gvariant string literal (single-quoted outer, so we only
    # need to escape internal single quotes by breaking out, plus
    # backslashes and double quotes are fine inside single quotes).
    local input="$1"
    # Escape backslashes and single quotes for shell-safety within the heredoc.
    printf '%s' "${input}"
}

ensure_banner_keyfile() {
    local escaped_banner
    escaped_banner=$(escape_for_gvariant "${BANNER_TEXT}")

    local desired_content
    desired_content=$(cat <<EOF
[org/gnome/login-screen]
banner-message-enable=true
banner-message-text='${escaped_banner}'
EOF
)

    if [[ ! -f "${DCONF_KEYFILE}" ]] || ! diff -q <(printf '%s\n' "${desired_content}") "${DCONF_KEYFILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "Writing banner configuration to ${DCONF_KEYFILE}"
        printf '%s\n' "${desired_content}" > "${DCONF_KEYFILE}"
    else
        log "Banner configuration already up to date in ${DCONF_KEYFILE}"
    fi
}

ensure_locks() {
    local desired_lock_content
    desired_lock_content=$(cat <<'EOF'
/org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-enable
/org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-text
EOF
)

    if [[ ! -f "${DCONF_LOCK_FILE}" ]] || ! diff -q <(printf '%s\n' "${desired_lock_content}") "${DCONF_LOCK_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "Writing dconf lock file to ${DCONF_LOCK_FILE}"
        printf '%s\n' "${desired_lock_content}" > "${DCONF_LOCK_FILE}"
    else
        log "Lock file already up to date at ${DCONF_LOCK_FILE}"
    fi
}

update_dconf_database() {
    log "Updating dconf database..."
    dconf update
}

verify_configuration() {
    log "Verifying active banner configuration..."

    local active_text
    active_text=$(dconf read /org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-text 2>/dev/null || true)

    # Strip surrounding single quotes returned by dconf read
    active_text="${active_text#\'}"
    active_text="${active_text%\'}"

    if [[ "${active_text}" != "${BANNER_TEXT}" ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: Active banner-message-text does not match expected value." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    local active_enable
    active_enable=$(dconf read /org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-enable 2>/dev/null || true)

    if [[ "${active_enable}" != "true" ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: banner-message-enable is not set to true." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    log "Banner configuration verified successfully."
}

# --- Main --------------------------------------------------------------

main() {
    require_root
    ensure_packages
    ensure_dconf_profile
    ensure_dconf_db_dir
    ensure_banner_keyfile
    ensure_locks

require_root() {
    if [[ "${EUID}" -ne 0 ]]; then
        echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
}

ensure_packages() {
    if ! rpm -q gdm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "GDM package not found. Installing gdm..."
        yum install -y gdm
    fi

    if ! rpm -q dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "dconf package not found. Installing dconf..."
        yum install -y dconf
    fi
}

ensure_dconf_profile() {
    mkdir -p "${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}"

    local desired_content
    desired_content=$(cat <<'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:gdm
file-db:/usr/share/gdm/greeter-dconf-defaults
EOF
)

    if [[ ! -f "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" ]] || ! diff -q <(printf '%s\n' "${desired_content}") "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "Configuring dconf profile at ${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
        printf '%s\n' "${desired_content}" > "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
    else
        log "dconf profile already correctly configured."
    fi
}

ensure_dconf_db_dir() {
    mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
    mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCK_DIR}"
}

escape_for_gvariant() {
    # Escapes double quotes and backslashes for embedding into a
    # dconf/gvariant string literal (single-quoted outer, so we only
    # need to escape internal single quotes by breaking out, plus
    # backslashes and double quotes are fine inside single quotes).
    local input="$1"
    # Escape backslashes and single quotes for shell-safety within the heredoc.
    printf '%s' "${input}"
}

ensure_banner_keyfile() {
    local escaped_banner
    escaped_banner=$(escape_for_gvariant "${BANNER_TEXT}")

    local desired_content
    desired_content=$(cat <<EOF
[org/gnome/login-screen]
banner-message-enable=true
banner-message-text='${escaped_banner}'
EOF
)

    if [[ ! -f "${DCONF_KEYFILE}" ]] || ! diff -q <(printf '%s\n' "${desired_content}") "${DCONF_KEYFILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "Writing banner configuration to ${DCONF_KEYFILE}"
        printf '%s\n' "${desired_content}" > "${DCONF_KEYFILE}"
    else
        log "Banner configuration already up to date in ${DCONF_KEYFILE}"
    fi
}

ensure_locks() {
    local desired_lock_content
    desired_lock_content=$(cat <<'EOF'
/org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-enable
/org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-text
EOF
)

    if [[ ! -f "${DCONF_LOCK_FILE}" ]] || ! diff -q <(printf '%s\n' "${desired_lock_content}") "${DCONF_LOCK_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "Writing dconf lock file to ${DCONF_LOCK_FILE}"
        printf '%s\n' "${desired_lock_content}" > "${DCONF_LOCK_FILE}"
    else
        log "Lock file already up to date at ${DCONF_LOCK_FILE}"
    fi
}

update_dconf_database() {
    log "Updating dconf database..."
    dconf update
}

verify_configuration() {
    log "Verifying active banner configuration..."

    local active_text
    active_text=$(dconf read /org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-text 2>/dev/null || true)

    # Strip surrounding single quotes returned by dconf read
    active_text="${active_text#\'}"
    active_text="${active_text%\'}"

    if [[ "${active_text}" != "${BANNER_TEXT}" ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: Active banner-message-text does not match expected value." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    local active_enable
    active_enable=$(dconf read /org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-enable 2>/dev/null || true)

    if [[ "${active_enable}" != "true" ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: banner-message-enable is not set to true." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    log "Banner configuration verified successfully."
}

# --- Main --------------------------------------------------------------

main() {
    require_root
    ensure_packages
    ensure_dconf_profile
    ensure_dconf_db_dir
    ensure_banner_keyfile
    ensure_locks
    update

#
# Remediation script: Restrict concurrent login sessions per user to 10
# Idempotent - safe to run multiple times
#

set -euo pipefail

LIMITS_DIR="/etc/security/limits.d"
LIMITS_FILE="${LIMITS_DIR}/maxlogins.conf"
MAXLOGINS=10

if [[ ! -d "${LIMITS_DIR}" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "${LIMITS_DIR}"
    chmod 755 "${LIMITS_DIR}"
fi

DESIRED_HARD_LINE="*               hard    maxlogins       ${MAXLOGINS}"
DESIRED_SOFT_LINE="*               soft    maxlogins       ${MAXLOGINS}"

# Create the file if it doesn't exist
if [[ ! -f "${LIMITS_FILE}" ]]; then
    touch "${LIMITS_FILE}"
    chmod 644 "${LIMITS_FILE}"
fi

# Function to ensure a specific limit line exists, replacing any existing
# maxlogins entry for the wildcard domain with the same limit type.
ensure_limit_line() {
    local domain="$1"
    local limit_type="$2"
    local item="$3"
    local value="$4"
    local desired_line
    desired_line="$(printf '%-16s%-8s%-16s%s' "${domain}" "${limit_type}" "${item}" "${value}")"

    # Remove any existing lines matching this domain/limit_type/item combination
    # (ignoring exact whitespace formatting), then append the desired line.
    local tmp_file
    tmp_file="$(mktemp)"

    awk -v domain="${domain}" -v ltype="${limit_type}" -v item="${item}" '
        {
            line = $0
            # Skip blank lines and comments when matching
            stripped = line
            gsub(/^[ \t]+/, "", stripped)
            if (stripped ~ /^#/ || stripped == "") {
                print line
                next
            }
            # Split into fields to compare domain, type, item
            n = split(stripped, fields, /[ \t]+/)
            if (n >= 3 && fields[1] == domain && fields[2] == ltype && fields[3] == item) {
                next  # skip this line, will be replaced
            }
            print line
        }
    ' "${LIMITS_FILE}" > "${tmp_file}"

    echo "${desired_line}" >> "${tmp_file}"

    mv "${tmp_file}" "${LIMITS_FILE}"
    chmod 644 "${LIMITS_FILE}"
}

ensure_limit_line "*" "hard" "maxlogins" "${MAXLOGINS}"
ensure_limit_line "*" "soft" "maxlogins" "${MAXLOGINS}"

# Verify pam_limits is enabled in the common-session / system-auth PAM stack
# On RHEL 8, this is typically /etc/pam.d/system-auth and /etc/pam.d/password-auth
for pam_file in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth; do
    if [[ -f "${pam_file}" ]]; then
        if ! grep -qE '^[^#]*session\s+required\s+pam_limits\.so' "${pam_file}"; then
            echo "session     required      pam_limits.so" >> "${pam_file}"
        fi
    fi
done

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure maximum password age for local user accounts
# is set to 365 days (RHEL 8 compliance requirement).
#
# This script is idempotent - running it multiple times will not cause
# errors and will always leave the system in the compliant state.

set -euo pipefail

MAX_DAYS=365
LOGIN_DEFS="/etc/login.defs"

# --- Step 1: Ensure PASS_MAX_DAYS is set correctly in /etc/login.defs ---
# This ensures any *new* users created after this point get the correct
# default maximum password age.

if [ -f "${LOGIN_DEFS}" ]; then
    if grep -qE '^[[:space:]]*PASS_MAX_DAYS[[:space:]]+' "${LOGIN_DEFS}"; then
        # Update existing line if value differs
        current_value=$(grep -E '^[[:space:]]*PASS_MAX_DAYS[[:space:]]+' "${LOGIN_DEFS}" | awk '{print $2}' | tail -n1)
        if [ "${current_value}" != "${MAX_DAYS}" ]; then
            sed -i -E "s/^[[:space:]]*PASS_MAX_DAYS[[:space:]]+.*/PASS_MAX_DAYS   ${MAX_DAYS}/" "${LOGIN_DEFS}"
        fi
    else
        # Append if missing
        printf '\nPASS_MAX_DAYS   %s\n' "${MAX_DAYS}" >> "${LOGIN_DEFS}"
    fi
else
    echo "Warning: ${LOGIN_DEFS} not found; skipping login.defs update." >&2
fi

# --- Step 2: Bring all existing local user accounts into compliance ---
# Only consider accounts with an entry in /etc/shadow that represents a
# real account with a password (i.e., not locked/disabled system accounts
# without login capability, though we err on the side of covering all
# accounts that have an entry in /etc/shadow with a valid username in
# /etc/passwd).

if [ ! -f /etc/shadow ]; then
    echo "Error: /etc/shadow not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Iterate over all users defined in /etc/passwd that also have a shadow entry
while IFS=: read -r username _ uid _ _ _ shell; do
    # Skip if user has no shadow entry
    shadow_entry=$(getent shadow "${username}" 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [ -z "${shadow_entry}" ]; then
        continue
    fi

    # Extract current PASS_MAX_DAYS (field 5) from shadow entry
    current_max=$(echo "${shadow_entry}" | awk -F: '{print $5}')

    # Skip accounts with non-login shells commonly used for system/service accounts
    case "${shell}" in
        */nologin|*/false)
            # Still enforce policy on accounts that have a password set,
            # but many hardening benchmarks only require this for accounts
            # capable of interactive login with a password. To remain safe
            # and idempotent, we check if a password hash exists.
            pass_field=$(echo "${shadow_entry}" | awk -F: '{print $2}')
            if [[ "${pass_field}" == "*" || "${pass_field}" == "!" || "${pass_field}" == "!!" || -z "${pass_field}" ]]; then
                continue
            fi
            ;;
    esac

    # If current_max is empty, non-numeric, or greater than MAX_DAYS, or not set to MAX_DAYS exactly,
    # bring it into compliance. Compliance requirement: max <= 365 and set (not empty/-1/unbounded).
    if ! [[ "${current_max}" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || [ "${current_max}" -gt "${MAX_DAYS}" ] || [ "${current_max}" -le 0 ]; then
        chage --maxdays "${MAX_DAYS}" "${username}"
    fi
done < /etc/passwd

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Configure auditd to record unsuccessful attempts
# by regular users (auid >= 1000, excluding unset) to call
# open_by_handle_at, for both 32-bit and 64-bit syscall interfaces,
# tagging events with the key "access".
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/access.rules"

# Ensure the rules directory exists
if [[ ! -d "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"
fi

# Ensure the rules file exists
if [[ ! -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]]; then
    touch "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Define the required audit rules
declare -a REQUIRED_RULES=(
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open_by_handle_at -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k access"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open_by_handle_at -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k access"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open_by_handle_at -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k access"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open_by_handle_at -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k access"
)

# Add each rule to the rules file if not already present
for rule in "${REQUIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -qF -- "${rule}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"; then
        echo "${rule}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    fi
done

# Remove any duplicate blank lines that might have been introduced
sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*$/d' "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Reload audit rules so they take effect on next rule load and persist
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Restart the audit daemon if possible to apply changes immediately
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl restart auditd || true
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Enforce minimum password age of 1 day (24 hours)
# for all existing local user accounts on RHEL 8.
#
# This script is idempotent - running it multiple times will not cause
# errors and will only make changes where necessary.

set -euo pipefail

readonly MIN_DAYS=1
readonly LOGIN_DEFS="/etc/login.defs"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Ensure the system-wide default (PASS_MIN_DAYS in /etc/login.defs)
#    is set to the required minimum, so new accounts inherit it.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ -f "${LOGIN_DEFS}" ]]; then
    if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*PASS_MIN_DAYS[[:space:]]+' "${LOGIN_DEFS}"; then
        current_value=$(grep -E '^[[:space:]]*PASS_MIN_DAYS[[:space:]]+' "${LOGIN_DEFS}" | awk '{print $2}' | tail -n1)
        if [[ "${current_value}" -lt "${MIN_DAYS}" ]] 2>/dev/null; then
            sed -i -E "s/^([[:space:]]*PASS_MIN_DAYS[[:space:]]+).*/\1${MIN_DAYS}/" "${LOGIN_DEFS}"
            echo "Updated PASS_MIN_DAYS in ${LOGIN_DEFS} to ${MIN_DAYS}."
        fi
    else
        echo "PASS_MIN_DAYS       ${MIN_DAYS}" >> "${LOGIN_DEFS}"
        echo "Added PASS_MIN_DAYS entry to ${LOGIN_DEFS}."
    fi
fi

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Iterate over all local user accounts defined in /etc/passwd
#    and bring any account whose minimum password age is less than
#    the required value into compliance.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
while IFS=: read -r username _ uid _ _ _ shell; do

    # Skip accounts with nologin/false shells - typically system/service
    # accounts that do not use password aging in the traditional sense.
    case "${shell}" in
        */nologin|*/false)
            continue
            ;;
    esac

    # Only manage accounts that have an entry in /etc/shadow (local accounts).
    if ! getent shadow "${username}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        continue
    fi

    # Retrieve current minimum password age via chage.
    current_min_days=$(chage -l "${username}" 2>/dev/null | \
        awk -F': ' '/Minimum number of days between password change/ {print $2}')

    # If the field is empty or non-numeric, skip (e.g., locked/no-password accounts
    # or accounts where aging fields are unset "-1").
    if ! [[ "${current_min_days}" =~ ^-?[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        continue
    fi

    if [[ "${current_min_days}" -lt "${MIN_DAYS}" ]]; then
        chage --mindays "${MIN_DAYS}" "${username}"
        echo "Set minimum password age for user '${username}' to ${MIN_DAYS} day(s)."
    fi

done < /etc/passwd

exit 0

        awk -F': ' '/Minimum number of days between password change/ {print $2}')

    # If the field is empty or non-numeric, skip (e.g., locked/no-password accounts
    # or accounts where aging fields are unset "-1").
    if ! [[ "${current_min_days}" =~ ^-?[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        continue
    fi

    if [[ "${current_min_days}" -lt "${MIN_DAYS}" ]]; then
        chage --mindays "${MIN_DAYS}" "${username}"
        echo "Set minimum password age for user '${username}' to ${MIN_DAYS} day(s)."
    fi

done < /etc/passwd

exit 0

    exit 0

    echo "Error: ${AUDIT_RULES_DIR} does not exist. Is auditd installed?" >&2
    exit 1
fi

    ARCHS+=("b64")
fi
if [[ -d /lib ]] || [[ -d /usr/lib ]]; then
    ARCHS+=("b32")
fi

if [[ ${#ARCHS[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
    ARCHS=("b64" "b32")
fi

DESIRED_RULES=()
for arch in "${ARCHS[@]}"; do
    DESIRED_RULES+=("-a always,exit -F path=${BINARY_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY_NAME}")
done

# Deduplicate (in case both archs produce same line, though path-based rules don't need arch)
UNIQUE_RULES=($(printf "%s\n" "${DESIRED_RULES[@]}" | sort -u))

# Create or update the rule file idempotently
TMP_FILE="$(mktemp)"
trap 'rm -f "${TMP_FILE}"' EXIT

if [[ -f "${AUDIT_RULE_FILE}" ]]; then
    cp "${AUDIT_RULE_FILE}" "${TMP_FILE}"
else
    : > "${TMP_FILE}"
fi

CHANGED=0
for rule in "${UNIQUE_RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -Fxq "${rule}" "${TMP_FILE}"; then
        echo "${rule}" >> "${TMP_FILE}"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
done

if [[ "${CHANGED}" -eq 1 ]] || [[ ! -f "${AUDIT_RULE_FILE}" ]]; then
    # Sort and dedupe final content for cleanliness
    sort -u "${TMP_FILE}" -o "${TMP_FILE}"
    install -m 0640 -o root -g root "${TMP_FILE}" "${AUDIT_RULE_FILE}"
    echo "Audit rule file updated: ${AUDIT_RULE_FILE}"
else
    echo "Audit rule already present and up to date: ${AUDIT_RULE_FILE}"
fi

# Ensure auditd is enabled and running
if command -v systemctl &>/dev/null; then
    if ! systemctl is-enabled auditd &>/dev/null; then
        systemctl enable auditd || true
    fi
fi

# Reload audit rules to apply immediately, without requiring reboot
if command -v augenrules &>/dev/null; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl &>/dev/null; then
    auditctl -R "${AUDIT_RULE_FILE}" || true
fi

    if auditctl -l | grep -Fq "path=${BINARY_PATH}" | grep -q "${KEY_NAME}" 2>/dev/null; then
        :
    fi
    if auditctl -l | grep -F "${BINARY_PATH}" | grep -q "${KEY_NAME}"; then
        echo "Audit rule for ${BINARY_PATH} is active."
    else
        echo "Warning: Audit rule for ${BINARY_PATH} may not be active. Check auditd status." >&2
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure auditd records execution of ssh-keysign
# (privileged SSH key-signing helper) by non-system users.
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${RULES_DIR}/50-privileged.rules"
KEY="privileged"

# Locate the ssh-keysign binary; RHEL 8 typically ships it under
# /usr/libexec/openssh/ssh-keysign, but fall back to searching the
# filesystem if it is not found there.
SSH_KEYSIGN_PATH=""
for candidate in /usr/libexec/openssh/ssh-keysign /usr/lib/openssh/ssh-keysign /usr/sbin/ssh-keysign; do
    if [ -x "${candidate}" ]; then
        SSH_KEYSIGN_PATH="${candidate}"
        break
    fi
done

if [ -z "${SSH_KEYSIGN_PATH}" ]; then
    SSH_KEYSIGN_PATH="$(find / -xdev -type f -name 'ssh-keysign' -perm -u+x 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)"
fi

if [ -z "${SSH_KEYSIGN_PATH}" ]; then
    echo "Error: ssh-keysign binary not found on this system." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Build the audit rule lines for both possible bitness variants
# (auditctl syntax used in rules.d files is architecture-agnostic here
# since we key off the path, not arch, but we still emit a single
# rule as required).
RULE_LINE="-a always,exit -F path=${SSH_KEYSIGN_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"

mkdir -p "${RULES_DIR}"

# Ensure the rules file exists
touch "${RULES_FILE}"

# Add the rule only if it is not already present (idempotency)
if ! grep -Fxq -- "${RULE_LINE}" "${RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "${RULE_LINE}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Deduplicate the rules file just in case of prior partial runs
if [ -f "${RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    TMP_FILE="$(mktemp)"
    awk '!seen[$0]++' "${RULES_FILE}" > "${TMP_FILE}"
    mv "${TMP_FILE}" "${RULES_FILE}"
    chmod 640 "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Load the rules using whichever mechanism this system uses.
# Prefer augenrules if present (it merges rules.d into audit.rules),
# otherwise fall back to auditctl directly.
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load || true
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "${RULES_FILE}" || true
fi

# Also ensure the rule is active in the live kernel ruleset right now,
# in case augenrules/auditctl load above didn't pick it up due to
# ordering or merge behavior (e.g. immutable config not yet reloaded).
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -F "${SSH_KEYSIGN_PATH}" | grep -F "auid>=1000" | grep -Fq "${KEY}"; then
        auditctl ${RULE_LINE} 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Disable IgnoreUserKnownHosts trust in sshd_config
# Ensures SSH server does not rely on per-user known_hosts cache for host key
# verification bypass, hardening authentication.
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
PARAMETER="IgnoreUserKnownHosts"
VALUE="yes"
BACKUP_SUFFIX=".bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Ensure the sshd_config file exists
if [[ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}" ]]; then
    echo "Error: ${SSHD_CONFIG} not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Function to remove all existing (active or commented) occurrences of the
# parameter from a given file, case-insensitively, to avoid duplicate or
# conflicting entries.
remove_existing_entries() {
    local file="$1"
    if grep -Eiq "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${PARAMETER}[[:space:]]+" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i -E "/^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${PARAMETER}[[:space:]]+/d" "${file}"
    fi
}

# Backup main config before modifying
cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}${BACKUP_SUFFIX}"

# Remove any existing IgnoreUserKnownHosts directives from main config
remove_existing_entries "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

# Also clean up any drop-in config files that might override this setting
if [[ -d "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}" ]]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' dropin_file; do
        if grep -Eiq "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${PARAMETER}[[:space:]]+" "${dropin_file}" 2>/dev/null; then
            cp -p "${dropin_file}" "${dropin_file}${BACKUP_SUFFIX}"
            remove_existing_entries "${dropin_file}"
        fi
    done < <(find "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name "*.conf" -print0 2>/dev/null)
fi

# Append the desired setting to the main sshd_config file
# Ensure file ends with a newline before appending
if [[ -s "${SSHD_CONFIG}" ]] && [[ "$(tail -c1 "${SSHD_CONFIG}")" != "" ]]; then
    echo "" >> "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
fi

echo "${PARAMETER} ${VALUE}" >> "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

# Validate the sshd configuration syntax before applying
if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sshd -t -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}" 2>/tmp/sshd_test_err; then
        echo "Error: sshd configuration test failed after modification:" >&2
        cat /tmp/sshd_test_err >&2
        rm -f /tmp/sshd_test_err
        echo "Restoring backup: ${SSHD_CONFIG}${BACKUP_SUFFIX}" >&2
        cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}${BACKUP_SUFFIX}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
        exit 1
    fi
    rm -f /tmp/sshd_test_err
fi

# Determine the correct service name (sshd on RHEL 8)
SERVICE_NAME="sshd"

# Reload/restart the SSH service to apply changes, ensuring persistence across reboots
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl is-enabled "${SERVICE_NAME}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        systemctl enable "${SERVICE_NAME}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
    if systemctl is-active "${SERVICE_NAME}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        systemctl reload-or-restart "${SERVICE_NAME}"
    else
        systemctl restart "${SERVICE_NAME}" || true
    fi
else
    echo "Warning: systemctl not found; please restart the SSH service manually." >&2
fi

echo "Remediation applied: ${PARAMETER} set to '${VALUE}' in ${SSHD_CONFIG}"
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure default password inactivity lock period is 30 days or less
# This configures /etc/default/useradd so that newly created accounts have
# INACTIVE=30 set, which causes accounts to be locked 30 days after password expiration.
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

USERADD_DEFAULTS="/etc/default/useradd"
DESIRED_VALUE=30
DESIRED_LINE="INACTIVE=${DESIRED_VALUE}"

# Ensure the file exists
if [[ ! -f "${USERADD_DEFAULTS}" ]]; then
    touch "${USERADD_DEFAULTS}"
fi

# Check if INACTIVE is set anywhere (commented or not)
if grep -qE '^\s*INACTIVE=' "${USERADD_DEFAULTS}"; then
    CURRENT_VALUE=$(grep -E '^\s*INACTIVE=' "${USERADD_DEFAULTS}" | tail -n1 | cut -d'=' -f2 | tr -d '[:space:]')

    if [[ "${CURRENT_VALUE}" == "${DESIRED_VALUE}" ]]; then

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure default password inactivity lock period is 30 days or less
# This configures /etc/default/useradd so that newly created accounts have

#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

USERADD_DEFAULTS="/etc/default/useradd"
DESIRED_VALUE=30
DESIRED_LINE="INACTIVE=${DESIRED_VALUE}"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure system authentication (PAM/nsswitch) is managed via
# the supported profile-based authentication configuration tool (authselect)
# on RHEL 8, rather than via manual edits.
#
# Idempotent: if a valid authselect profile is already selected/active,
# no changes are made. Otherwise, a tested supported profile is selected
# and activated, and the change persists across reboots (authselect writes
# its managed files under /etc/authselect and symlinks/configures
# /etc/nsswitch.conf and /etc/pam.d accordingly, which persists natively).

set -euo pipefail

AUTHSELECT_BIN="$(command -v authselect || true)"

if [[ -z "${AUTHSELECT_BIN}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: authselect is not installed on this system." >&2
    echo "Please install the 'authselect' package before running this remediation." >&2
    exit 1
fi

DESIRED_PROFILE="sssd"
DESIRED_FEATURES=("with-mkhomedir")

is_configured() {
    "${AUTHSELECT_BIN}" current >/dev/null 2>&1
}

get_current_profile() {
    "${AUTHSELECT_BIN}" current 2>/dev/null | awk '/Profile ID:/ {print $3}'
}

get_current_features() {
    "${AUTHSELECT_BIN}" current 2>/dev/null | sed -n '/Enabled features:/,$p' | tail -n +2 | sed 's/^- //'
}

needs_remediation=false

if ! is_configured; then
    echo "authselect is not currently configured with any profile."
    needs_remediation=true
else
    current_profile="$(get_current_profile || true)"
    if [[ "${current_profile}" != "${DESIRED_PROFILE}" ]]; then
        echo "authselect is configured with profile '${current_profile}', expected '${DESIRED_PROFILE}'."
        needs_remediation=true
    else
        mapfile -t current_features < <(get_current_features || true)
        for feat in "${DESIRED_FEATURES[@]}"; do
            found=false
            for cf in "${current_features[@]}"; do
                if [[ "${cf}" == "${feat}" ]]; then
                    found=true
                    break
                fi
            done
            if [[ "${found}" == false ]]; then
                echo "Required feature '${feat}' is not enabled in current authselect profile."
                needs_remediation=true
            fi
        done
    fi
fi

if [[ "${needs_remediation}" == true ]]; then
    echo "Selecting and activating supported authselect profile '${DESIRED_PROFILE}' with features: ${DESIRED_FEATURES[*]}"
    "${AUTHSELECT_BIN}" select "${DESIRED_PROFILE}" "${DESIRED_FEATURES[@]}" --force
else
    echo "authselect is already configured with the desired supported profile '${DESIRED_PROFILE}' and required features."
fi

echo "Verifying final authselect configuration:"
"${AUTHSELECT_BIN}" current

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Enforce GNOME screensaver idle-activation timeout
# of 15 minutes (900 seconds), locked so users cannot override it.
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

# --- Configuration ---
IDLE_DELAY_SECONDS=900   # 15 minutes
DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
DCONF_LOCKS_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks"
DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/00-screensaver"
DCONF_LOCKS_FILE="${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}/00-screensaver"

# --- Ensure required packages/tools are present ---
if ! command -v dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Installing dconf package..."
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf install -y dconf
    else
        echo "ERROR: dnf not found; cannot install dconf." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# --- Ensure directories exist ---
mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}"

# --- Write/update the dconf settings file idempotently ---
DESIRED_SETTINGS_CONTENT="[org/gnome/desktop/session]
idle-delay=uint32 ${IDLE_DELAY_SECONDS}

[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-enabled=true
lock-delay=uint32 0
idle-activation-enabled=true
"

if [[ -f "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}" ]] && diff -q <(printf '%s' "${DESIRED_SETTINGS_CONTENT}") "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "dconf settings file already up to date: ${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"
else
    echo "Writing dconf settings file: ${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"
    printf '%s' "${DESIRED_SETTINGS_CONTENT}" > "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"
fi

# --- Write/update the dconf locks file idempotently ---
DESIRED_LOCKS_CONTENT="/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-enabled
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/idle-activation-enabled
"

if [[ -f "${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}" ]] && diff -q <(printf '%s' "${DESIRED_LOCKS_CONTENT}") "${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "dconf locks file already up to date: ${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}"
else
    echo "Writing dconf locks file: ${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}"
    printf '%s' "${DESIRED_LOCKS_CONTENT}" > "${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}"
fi

# --- Set proper permissions ---
chmod 0644 "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}" "${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}"
chown root:root "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}" "${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}"

# --- Update dconf database so changes take effect ---
echo "Updating dconf database..."
dconf update

echo "GNOME screensaver idle timeout of ${IDLE_DELAY_SECONDS} seconds (15 minutes) has been configured and locked system-wide."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Disable storage of application core dumps on RHEL 8.
# Idempotent - safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

LIMITS_FILE="/etc/security/limits.conf"
LIMITS_DROPIN="/etc/security/limits.d/99-disable-coredump.conf"
COREDUMP_DROPIN_DIR="/etc/systemd/coredump.conf.d"
COREDUMP_DROPIN="${COREDUMP_DROPIN_DIR}/disable.conf"
SYSCTL_DROPIN="/etc/sysctl.d/60-coredump-disable.conf"

echo "==> Disabling core dumps via limits.conf drop-in"
mkdir -p /etc/security/limits.d
cat > "${LIMITS_DROPIN}" <<'EOF'
# Managed by remediation script - disable core dumps for all users
* hard core 0
* soft core 0
EOF

# Ensure legacy limits.conf does not conflict with a directly-set "hard core"
if [ -f "${LIMITS_FILE}" ]; then
    if grep -Eq '^\s*\*\s+hard\s+core\s+[1-9]' "${LIMITS_FILE}"; then
        sed -i -E 's/^(\s*\*\s+hard\s+core\s+)[1-9].*/\10/' "${LIMITS_FILE}"
    fi
fi

echo "==> Configuring systemd-coredump to not store core dumps to disk"
mkdir -p "${COREDUMP_DROPIN_DIR}"
cat > "${COREDUMP_DROPIN}" <<'EOF'
# Managed by remediation script - disable persistent storage of core dumps
[Coredump]
Storage=none
ProcessSizeMax=0
EOF

echo "==> Ensuring kernel does not honor suid_dumpable"
mkdir -p /etc/sysctl.d
cat > "${SYSCTL_DROPIN}" <<'EOF'
# Managed by remediation script - disable suid core dumps
fs.suid_dumpable = 0
EOF

echo "==> Reloading systemd manager configuration"
systemctl daemon-reload || true

echo "==> Applying sysctl settings immediately"
sysctl -w fs.suid_dumpable=0 >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
sysctl --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

echo "==> Applying resource limit immediately for current shell context"
ulimit -S -c 0 || true
ulimit -H -c 0 || true

echo "==> Restarting systemd-coredump related units (if present) to apply immediately"
if systemctl list-unit-files systemd-coredump.socket >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl restart systemd-coredump.socket 2>/dev/null || true
fi

echo "Core dump remediation applied successfully."

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure GPG signature verification is enforced for
# local package installations on RHEL 8 (localpkg_gpgcheck / gpgcheck).
#
# This script is idempotent and can be safely re-run.

set -euo pipefail

CONF_FILE="/etc/dnf/dnf.conf"

# Ensure the config file exists
if [ ! -f "${CONF_FILE}" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: ${CONF_FILE} does not exist." >&2
    exit 1
fi

ensure_setting() {
    local key="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local file="$3"

    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=" "${file}"; then
        # Update existing setting if it doesn't already match desired value
        if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${value}[[:space:]]*$" "${file}"; then
            sed -i -E "s/^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=.*/${key}=${value}/" "${file}"
            echo "Updated ${key}=${value} in ${file}"
        else
            echo "${key}=${value} already set correctly in ${file}"
        fi
    else
        # Ensure setting is added under [main] section
        if grep -Eq "^\[main\]" "${file}"; then
            sed -i -E "/^\[main\]/a ${key}=${value}" "${file}"
        else
            {
                echo "[main]"
                echo "${key}=${value}"
            } >> "${file}"
        fi
        echo "Added ${key}=${value} to ${file}"
    fi
}

ensure_setting "localpkg_gpgcheck" "1" "${CONF_FILE}"
ensure_setting "gpgcheck" "1" "${CONF_FILE}"

# Also ensure gpgcheck=1 is set globally in any yum.conf if present (symlinked to dnf.conf on RHEL8 usually)
YUM_CONF="/etc/yum.conf"
if [ -f "${YUM_CONF}" ] && [ ! -L "${YUM_CONF}" ]; then
    ensure_setting "gpgcheck" "1" "${YUM_CONF}"
fi

# Verify enforcement by checking effective dnf configuration
if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    CURRENT_LOCALPKG_GPGCHECK=$(dnf config-manager --dump 2>/dev/null | awk -F'=' '/^localpkg_gpgcheck/ {print $2}' | tr -d '[:space:]')
    CURRENT_GPGCHECK=$(dnf config-manager --dump 2>/dev/null | awk -F'=' '/^gpgcheck/ {print $2}' | tr -d '[:space:]')

    if [ "${CURRENT_LOCALPKG_GPGCHECK}" != "1" ]; then
        echo "WARNING: localpkg_gpgcheck not confirmed as 1 via dnf config-manager output." >&2
    fi

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure GPG signature verification is enforced for
# local package installations on RHEL 8 (localpkg_gpgcheck / gpgcheck).
#
# This script is idempotent and can be safely re-run.

set -euo pipefail

CONF_FILE="/etc/dnf/dnf.conf"

# Ensure the config file exists
if [ ! -f "${CONF_FILE}" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: ${CONF_FILE} does not exist." >&2
    exit 1
fi

ensure_setting() {
    local key="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local file="$3"

    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=" "${file}"; then
        # Update existing setting if it doesn't already match desired value
        if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${value}[[:space:]]*$" "${file}"; then
            sed -i -E "s/^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=.*/${key}=${value}/" "${file}"
            echo "Updated ${key}=${value} in ${file}"
        else
            echo "${key}=${value} already set correctly in ${file}"
        fi
    else
        # Ensure setting is added under [main] section
        if grep -Eq "^\[main\]" "${file}"; then
            sed -i -E "/^\[main\]/a ${key}=${value}" "${file}"
        else
            {
                echo "[main]"
                echo "${key}=${value}"
            } >> "${file}"
        fi
        echo "Added ${key}=${value} to ${file}"
    fi
}

ensure_setting "localpkg_gpgcheck" "1" "${CONF_FILE}"
ensure_setting "gpgcheck" "1" "${CONF_FILE}"

# Also ensure gpgcheck=1 is set globally in any yum.conf if present (symlinked to dnf.conf on RHEL8 usually)
YUM_CONF="/etc/yum.conf"
if [ -f "${YUM_CONF}" ] && [ ! -L "${YUM_CONF}" ]; then
    ensure_setting "gpgcheck" "1" "${YUM_CONF}"
fi

# Verify enforcement by checking effective dnf configuration
if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    CURRENT_LOCALPKG_GPGCHECK=$(dnf config-manager --dump 2>/dev/null | awk -F'=' '/^localpkg_gpgcheck/ {print $2}' | tr -d '[:space:]')
    CURRENT_GPGCHECK=$(dnf config-manager --dump 2>/dev/null | awk -F'=' '/^gpgcheck/ {print $2}' | tr -d '[:space:]')

    if [ "${CURRENT_LOCALPKG_GPGCHECK}" != "1" ]; then
        echo "WARNING: localpkg_gpgcheck not confirmed as 1 via dnf config-manager output." >&2
    fi
    if [ "${CURRENT_GPGCHECK}" != "1" ]; then
        echo "WARNING: gpgcheck not confirmed as 1 via dnf config-manager output." >&2
    fi
fi

echo "Remediation complete: local package GPG signature verification is enforced."
```

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Configure auditd to record executions of the
# privileged 'postqueue' command by regular users (auid >= 1000)
#

set -euo pipefail

RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules"
AUDIT_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/privileged-postqueue.rules"

# Locate the actual path of postqueue (typically /usr/sbin/postqueue)
POSTQUEUE_PATH=$(command -v postqueue 2>/dev/null || echo "/usr/sbin/postqueue")

if [ ! -e "${POSTQUEUE_PATH}" ]; then
    # Fall back to standard location even if not found on this system,
    # so the rule is still staged for when the package is installed.
    POSTQUEUE_PATH="/usr/sbin/postqueue"
fi

# Determine architecture-specific syscall arch settings
ARCHS=()
if [ -d /lib64 ] || uname -m | grep -q '64'; then
    ARCHS+=("b64")
fi
ARCHS+=("b32")

# Build the audit rule lines for both architectures
declare -a RULE_LINES=()
for ARCH in "${ARCHS[@]}"; do
    RULE_LINES+=("-a always,exit -F path=${POSTQUEUE_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F arch=${ARCH} -k privileged-postqueue")
done

mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Ensure the dedicated rules file exists
touch "${AUDIT_FILE}"

# Idempotently add each rule line if not already present
for LINE in "${RULE_LINES[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -Fxq -- "${LINE}" "${AUDIT_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "${LINE}" >> "${AUDIT_FILE}"
    fi
done

# Ensure proper permissions on the rules file
chmod 0640 "${AUDIT_FILE}"
chown root:root "${AUDIT_FILE}"

# Merge rules into the main audit.rules via augenrules (RHEL 8 mechanism)
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "${AUDIT_FILE}" || true
fi

# Confirm rule is loaded in the running audit configuration; if not, reload
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! auditctl -l | grep -q "privileged-postqueue"; then
        service auditd restart || systemctl restart auditd || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

    cat "$TMP_FILE" > "$LIMITS_CONF"

    chmod 644 "$LIMITS_CONF"
    echo "Updated ${LIMITS_CONF}: enforced hard core dump limit of 0 for all users."
else
    echo "${LIMITS_CONF} already enforces hard core dump limit of 0 for all users."
fi

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure audit rules are configured to monitor
# writes and attribute changes to /etc/sudoers.
#
# This script is idempotent and works with either audit rule
# loading mechanism (augenrules -> /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules,
# or auditctl -> /etc/audit/audit.rules).

set -euo pipefail

readonly SUDOERS_FILE="/etc/sudoers"
readonly AUDIT_KEY="scope"
readonly RULE="-w ${SUDOERS_FILE} -p wa -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"

readonly RULES_D_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
readonly RULES_D_FILE="${RULES_D_DIR}/50-scope.rules"
readonly AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: add rule line to a given file if not already present
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
add_rule_if_missing() {
    local target_file="$1"
    local rule_line="$2"

    if [[ ! -f "${target_file}" ]]; then
        touch "${target_file}"
    fi

    if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*-w[[:space:]]+${SUDOERS_FILE//\//\\/}[[:space:]]+-p[[:space:]]+wa[[:space:]]+-k[[:space:]]+${AUDIT_KEY}[[:space:]]*$" "${target_file}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "${rule_line}" >> "${target_file}"
    fi
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Persist rule for augenrules (rules-merging program)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ -d "${RULES_D_DIR}" ]] || command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    mkdir -p "${RULES_D_DIR}"
    add_rule_if_missing "${RULES_D_FILE}" "${RULE}"
fi

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Persist rule for direct auditctl rule-loading (audit.rules)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]] || command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}")"
    add_rule_if_missing "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" "${RULE}"
fi

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Load the rule into the running kernel audit ruleset immediately
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -Eq -- "-w ${SUDOERS_FILE} .*-k ${AUDIT_KEY}"; then
        auditctl "${RULE}" || true
    fi
fi

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. If augenrules is available, regenerate merged rules to ensure
#    consistency between rules.d fragments and the active ruleset.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load || true
fi

exit 0

if [[ -d "${RULES_D_DIR}" ]] || command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    mkdir -p "${RULES_D_DIR}"
    add_rule_if_missing "${RULES_D_FILE}" "${RULE}"
fi

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Persist rule for direct auditctl rule-loading (audit.rules)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]] || command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then

    add_rule_if_missing "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" "${RULE}"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Load the rule into the running kernel audit ruleset immediately
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -Eq -- "-w ${SUDOERS_FILE} .*-k ${AUDIT_KEY}"; then
        auditctl "${RULE}" || true
    fi
fi

# 4. If augenrules is available, regenerate merged rules to ensure
#    consistency between rules.d fragments and the active ruleset.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load || true
fi

exit 0

# high-quality randomness.

if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    PKG_MGR="dnf"
elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    PKG_MGR="yum"
else
    echo "Error: Neither dnf nor yum package manager found on this system." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Check if the package is already installed
if rpm -q "${PACKAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' is already installed. No action needed."
else
    echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' is not installed. Installing now using ${PKG_MGR}..."
    "${PKG_MGR}" install -y "${PACKAGE}"

    # Verify installation succeeded
    if rpm -q "${PACKAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' successfully installed."
    else
        echo "Error: Failed to install package '${PACKAGE}'." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

exit 0
