#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure audit captures unlinkat (file deletion) events
# for all regular users (auid >= 1000, excluding unset), tagged "delete",
# for both 32-bit and 64-bit architectures as applicable.
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times, persists across reboots via
# /etc/audit/rules.d and augenrules.

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/50-delete_unlinkat.rules"

# Ensure the rules directory exists
mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"

# Ensure the rules file exists
touch "${RULES_FILE}"

# Determine system architecture to decide which arch rules are needed
SYS_ARCH="$(uname -m)"

# Build the list of required rule lines
declare -a REQUIRED_RULES

if [ "${SYS_ARCH}" = "x86_64" ] || [ "${SYS_ARCH}" = "aarch64" ] || [ "${SYS_ARCH}" = "s390x" ] || [ "${SYS_ARCH}" = "ppc64" ] || [ "${SYS_ARCH}" = "ppc64le" ]; then
    # 64-bit capable systems typically support both b32 and b64 syscall ABIs
    REQUIRED_RULES+=('-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S unlinkat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k delete')
    REQUIRED_RULES+=('-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S unlinkat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k delete')
else
    # Pure 32-bit systems only need the b32 rule
    REQUIRED_RULES+=('-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S unlinkat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k delete')
fi

# Function to check if a rule already exists anywhere in the rules.d directory
rule_exists() {
    local rule="$1"
    grep -rFxq -- "${rule}" "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}" 2>/dev/null
}

CHANGED=0

for rule in "${REQUIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! rule_exists "${rule}"; then
        echo "${rule}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
done

# Deduplicate the target rules file just in case (idempotency safeguard)
if [ -f "${RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    TMP_FILE="$(mktemp)"
    awk '!seen[$0]++' "${RULES_FILE}" > "${TMP_FILE}"
    mv "${TMP_FILE}" "${RULES_FILE}"
    chmod 640 "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Reload/apply audit rules so they take effect immediately and persist
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "${RULES_FILE}" || true
fi

# Restart auditd to ensure rules are active (if service is manageable)
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
        systemctl restart auditd || true
    fi
fi

if [ "${CHANGED}" -eq 1 ]; then
    echo "Audit rules for unlinkat (delete) events have been added and applied."
else
    echo "Audit rules for unlinkat (delete) events already present; no changes made."
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Enforce GNOME screensaver idle-lock via dconf, prevent user override,
# compile the settings database, and ensure persistence across reboots.
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times; only rewrites files if content differs,
# and always recompiles the dconf database to guarantee enforcement is active.

set -euo pipefail

DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
DCONF_LOCKS_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks"
DCONF_PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"
DCONF_PROFILE_FILE="${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}/user"
SETTINGS_FILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/00-screensaver"
LOCKS_FILE="${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}/00-screensaver"

# Ensure required packages are present (dconf should already be installed on GNOME systems)
if ! command -v dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf install -y dconf >/dev/null
    else
        echo "ERROR: dconf command not found and dnf unavailable to install it." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}"

# --- 1. Define the desired GNOME screensaver settings ---
read -r -d '' SETTINGS_CONTENT <<'EOF' || true
# GNOME screensaver idle-lock enforcement
[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-enabled=true
lock-delay=uint32 5

[org/gnome/desktop/session]
idle-delay=uint32 300
EOF

write_if_changed() {
    local target_file="$1"
    local content="$2"

    if [[ -f "${target_file}" ]] && diff -q <(printf '%s\n' "${content}") "${target_file}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        return 0
    fi

    printf '%s\n' "${content}" > "${target_file}"
    chmod 0644 "${target_file}"
}

write_if_changed "${SETTINGS_FILE}" "${SETTINGS_CONTENT}"

# --- 2. Define the locks so users/admins via GUI cannot override these keys ---
read -r -d '' LOCKS_CONTENT <<'EOF' || true
# Prevent users from overriding screensaver lock enforcement
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-enabled
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay
/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay
EOF

write_if_changed "${LOCKS_FILE}" "${LOCKS_CONTENT}"

# --- 3. Ensure the dconf "user" profile includes the local system database ---
DESIRED_PROFILE_CONTENT="user-db:user
system-db:local"

if [[ -f "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" ]]; then
    if ! diff -q <(printf '%s\n' "${DESIRED_PROFILE_CONTENT}") "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        printf '%s\n' "${DESIRED_PROFILE_CONTENT}" > "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
    fi
else
    printf '%s\n' "${DESIRED_PROFILE_CONTENT}" > "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
fi
chmod 0644 "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"

# --- 4. Compile the dconf database so the policy takes effect immediately ---
dconf update

# --- 5. Verify enforcement compiled successfully ---
if [[ ! -f /etc/dconf/db/local ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: dconf database /etc/dconf/db/local was not created by 'dconf update'." >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "GNOME screensaver idle-lock policy enforced and dconf database compiled successfully."
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
DROPIN_FILE="${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}/50-permitrootlogin.conf"
SETTING="PermitRootLogin"
VALUE="no"

backup_file() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        cp -p "$file" "${file}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
    fi
}

comment_out_setting_in_file() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -f "$file" ] && grep -Eiq "^[[:space:]]*${SETTING}[[:space:]]+" "$file"; then
        backup_file "$file"
        sed -ri "s/^[[:space:]]*(${SETTING}[[:space:]]+.*)$/#\1/I" "$file"
    fi
}

comment_out_setting_in_file "$SSHD_CONFIG"

if [ -d "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
        if [ "$f" != "$DROPIN_FILE" ]; then
            comment_out_setting_in_file "$f"
        fi
    done < <(find "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' -print0 2>/dev/null)
fi

mkdir -p "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR"

if [ -f "$DROPIN_FILE" ] && grep -Eiq "^[[:space:]]*${SETTING}[[:space:]]+${VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "$DROPIN_FILE"; then
    :
else
    backup_file "$DROPIN_FILE"
    printf '%s %s\n' "$SETTING" "$VALUE" > "$DROPIN_FILE"
fi

chown root:root "$DROPIN_FILE"
chmod 0600 "$DROPIN_FILE"

if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*Include[[:space:]]+${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}/\*\.conf" "$SSHD_CONFIG" 2>/dev/null; then
    if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*Include[[:space:]]+/etc/ssh/sshd_config\.d/\*\.conf" "$SSHD_CONFIG" 2>/dev/null; then
        backup_file "$SSHD_CONFIG"
        sed -i "1i Include ${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}/*.conf" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    fi
fi

if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sshd -t 2>/tmp/sshd_test_err; then
        echo "sshd configuration test failed:" >&2
        cat /tmp/sshd_test_err >&2
        rm -f /tmp/sshd_test_err
        exit 1
    fi
    rm -f /tmp/sshd_test_err
fi

if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    SERVICE_NAME="sshd"
    if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q '^ssh\.service'; then
        SERVICE_NAME="ssh"
    fi
    systemctl reload "$SERVICE_NAME" 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart "$SERVICE_NAME" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

exit 0

fi

mkdir -p "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR"

if [ -f "$DROPIN_FILE" ] && grep -Eiq "^[[:space:]]*${SETTING}[[:space:]]+${VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "$DROPIN_FILE"; then
    :
else
    backup_file "$DROPIN_FILE"
    printf '%s %s\n' "$SETTING" "$VALUE" > "$DROPIN_FILE"
fi

chown root:root "$DROPIN_FILE"
chmod 0600 "$DROPIN_FILE"

if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*Include[[:space:]]+${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}/\*\.conf" "$SSHD_CONFIG" 2>/dev/null; then
    if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*Include[[:space:]]+/etc/ssh/sshd_config\.d/\*\.conf" "$SSHD_CONFIG" 2>/dev/null; then
        backup_file "$SSHD_CONFIG"
        sed -i "1i Include ${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}/*.conf" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    fi
fi

if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sshd -t 2>/tmp/sshd_test_err; then
        echo "sshd configuration test failed:" >&2
        cat /tmp/sshd_test_err >&2
        rm -f /tmp/sshd_test_err
        exit 1
    fi
    rm -f /tmp/sshd_test_err
fi

if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    SERVICE_NAME="sshd"
    if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q '^ssh\.service'; then

fi

exit 0

        fi

    fi

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Configure audit daemon to record use of fsetxattr
# (setting extended attributes on a file descriptor) for both unprivileged
# users (UID >= 1000, excluding unset) and root, covering both 32-bit and
# 64-bit syscall ABIs, tagged with the "perm_mod" key.
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules"
AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
PERSISTENT_RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/50-perm_mod.rules"

SYSCALL="fsetxattr"
KEY="perm_mod"
UID_MIN=1000
UID_MAX="unset"

mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"

# Determine architecture; only add b32 rules relevant to this arch pairing,
# but since requirement says "cover both 32-bit and 64-bit system call
# interfaces on a 64-bit system", we always emit both b32 and b64 rules.

# Build the required rule lines
declare -a REQUIRED_RULES=(
"-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S ${SYSCALL} -F auid>=${UID_MIN} -F auid!=${UID_MAX} -F key=${KEY}"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S ${SYSCALL} -F auid>=${UID_MIN} -F auid!=${UID_MAX} -F key=${KEY}"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S ${SYSCALL} -F auid=0 -F key=${KEY}"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S ${SYSCALL} -F auid=0 -F key=${KEY}"
)

# Ensure the persistent rules file exists
if [[ ! -f "${PERSISTENT_RULES_FILE}" ]]; then
    touch "${PERSISTENT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Add each required rule if not already present in any file under rules.d
for rule in "${REQUIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -rFxq -- "${rule}" "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"/*.rules 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "${rule}" >> "${PERSISTENT_RULES_FILE}"
    fi
done

# Remove potential duplicate lines within the persistent rules file
if [[ -f "${PERSISTENT_RULES_FILE}" ]]; then
    TMP_FILE=$(mktemp)
    awk '!seen[$0]++' "${PERSISTENT_RULES_FILE}" > "${TMP_FILE}"
    mv "${TMP_FILE}" "${PERSISTENT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

chmod 0640 "${PERSISTENT_RULES_FILE}"
chown root:root "${PERSISTENT_RULES_FILE}"

# Determine which rule-loading mechanism is in use: augenrules or auditctl direct
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Fallback: merge all rules.d files into audit.rules and load directly
    cat "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"/*.rules > "${RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true
    auditctl -R "${RULES_FILE}" || true
fi

# Verify the rules are loaded in the running auditd configuration
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    LOADED_RULES=$(auditctl -l 2>/dev/null || true)
    for rule in "${REQUIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
        # Normalize rule for comparison against auditctl -l output format
        norm_rule=$(echo "${rule}" | sed -E 's/-a always,exit/-a always,exit/')
        if ! echo "${LOADED_RULES}" | grep -qF -- "${SYSCALL}"; then
            echo "Warning: Rule for ${SYSCALL} may not be active in running auditd; a reboot or manual 'augenrules --load' may be required." >&2
        fi
    done
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script to ensure the X Windows graphical display server
# components are not installed on the system (RHEL 8).
#
# This script is idempotent: it can be run multiple times safely, and
# it will only take action if the offending packages are present.

set -euo pipefail

readonly PACKAGE_PATTERN='^xorg-x11-server-'
readonly LOGFILE="/var/log/remediation-remove-x11.log"

log() {
    echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') - $*" | tee -a "${LOGFILE}"
}

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if ! command -v rpm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "ERROR: rpm command not found. Cannot determine package state."
    exit 1
fi

if ! command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "ERROR: dnf command not found. Cannot remove packages."
    exit 1
fi

# Find all installed packages matching the X server pattern.
mapfile -t installed_x_packages < <(rpm -qa --qf '%{NAME}\n' | grep -E "${PACKAGE_PATTERN}" || true)

if [ "${#installed_x_packages[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "No X Windows server packages (matching '${PACKAGE_PATTERN}') are installed. System is already compliant."
else
    log "Found installed X Windows server packages: ${installed_x_packages[*]}"
    log "Removing packages to eliminate the graphical display server..."

    if dnf remove -y "${installed_x_packages[@]}" >>"${LOGFILE}" 2>&1; then
        log "Successfully removed: ${installed_x_packages[*]}"
    else
        log "ERROR: Failed to remove one or more X Windows server packages."
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Re-verify no X server packages remain installed.
mapfile -t remaining_x_packages < <(rpm -qa --qf '%{NAME}\n' | grep -E "${PACKAGE_PATTERN}" || true)

if [ "${#remaining_x_packages[@]}" -ne 0 ]; then
    log "ERROR: The following X Windows server packages are still installed: ${remaining_x_packages[*]}"

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script to ensure the X Windows graphical display server
# components are not installed on the system (RHEL 8).
#
# This script is idempotent: it can be run multiple times safely, and
# it will only take action if the offending packages are present.

set -euo pipefail

readonly PACKAGE_PATTERN='^xorg-x11-server-'
readonly LOGFILE="/var/log/remediation-remove-x11.log"

log() {
    echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') - $*" | tee -a "${LOGFILE}"
}

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if ! command -v rpm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "ERROR: rpm command not found. Cannot determine package state."
    exit 1
fi

if ! command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "ERROR: dnf command not found. Cannot remove packages."
    exit 1
fi

# Find all installed packages matching the X server pattern.
mapfile -t installed_x_packages < <(rpm -qa --qf '%{NAME}\n' | grep -E "${PACKAGE_PATTERN}" || true)

if [ "${#installed_x_packages[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "No X Windows server packages (matching '${PACKAGE_PATTERN}') are installed. System is already compliant."
else
    log "Found installed X Windows server packages: ${installed_x_packages[*]}"
    log "Removing packages to eliminate the graphical display server..."

    if dnf remove -y "${installed_x_packages[@]}" >>"${LOGFILE}" 2>&1; then
        log "Successfully removed: ${installed_x_packages[*]}"
    else
        log "ERROR: Failed to remove one or more X Windows server packages."
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Re-verify no X server packages remain installed.
mapfile -t remaining_x_packages < <(rpm -qa --qf '%{NAME}\n' | grep -E "${PACKAGE_PATTERN}" || true)

if [ "${#remaining_x_packages[@]}" -ne 0 ]; then
    log "ERROR: The following X Windows server packages are still installed: ${remaining_x_packages[*]}"
    exit 1
fi

log "Verification complete: No X Windows server packages are installed."
log "System is configured to not support booting into a graphical desktop environment."

exit 0
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure SSH server enforces StrictModes (ownership/permission
# checks on user home directories and SSH files) before accepting logins.
# Idempotent and persists across reboots via sshd_config.
#
set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
SETTING_NAME="StrictModes"
SETTING_VALUE="yes"
TIMESTAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "Error: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [[ ! -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]]; then
    echo "Error: $SSHD_CONFIG not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Function to remove existing (active or commented) StrictModes directives
# from a given file, returning 0 if changes were made.
strip_setting() {
    local file="$1"
    local tmp
    tmp="$(mktemp)"
    grep -vEi "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${SETTING_NAME}[[:space:]]+" "$file" > "$tmp" || true
    if ! cmp -s "$file" "$tmp"; then
        cat "$tmp" > "$file"
        rm -f "$tmp"
        return 0
    fi
    rm -f "$tmp"
    return 1
}

CHANGED=0

# Backup main config before modification
cp -p "$SSHD_CONFIG" "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak.${TIMESTAMP}"

# Remove any pre-existing StrictModes directives from main config
if strip_setting "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    CHANGED=1
fi

# Remove any pre-existing StrictModes directives from drop-in config files,
# since they could override the main config (Include is processed first-match-wins
# for most directives, but we normalize to avoid conflicting settings).
if [[ -d "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR" ]]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' conf_file; do
        cp -p "$conf_file" "${conf_file}.bak.${TIMESTAMP}"
        if strip_setting "$conf_file"; then
            CHANGED=1
        fi
    done < <(find "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' -print0 2>/dev/null)
fi

# Ensure Include directive for drop-in directory exists (RHEL8 default), but
# do not fail if it's absent; just proceed with main file enforcement.

# Append the enforced setting to the main sshd_config file
{
    echo ""
    echo "# Enforce ownership/permission checks on user home directory and SSH files"
    echo "${SETTING_NAME} ${SETTING_VALUE}"
} >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
CHANGED=1

# Validate the resulting configuration
if ! /usr/sbin/sshd -t -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" 2>/tmp/sshd_test_err.$$; then
    echo "Error: sshd configuration test failed after modification:" >&2
    cat /tmp/sshd_test_err.$$ >&2
    rm -f /tmp/sshd_test_err.$$
    echo "Restoring backup of $SSHD_CONFIG." >&2
    cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak.${TIMESTAMP}" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    exit 1
fi
rm -f /tmp/sshd_test_err.$$

# Determine effective value now enforced
EFFECTIVE_VALUE="$(/usr/sbin/sshd -T 2>/dev/null | awk 'tolower($1)=="strictmodes"{print tolower($2); exit}')"

if [[ "${EFFECTIVE_VALUE}" != "${SETTING_VALUE}" ]]; then
    echo "Error: Effective StrictModes value is '${EFFECTIVE_VALUE}', expected '${SETTING_VALUE}'." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Determine service name (sshd on RHEL8)
SERVICE_NAME="sshd"

if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q "^${SERVICE_NAME}.service"; then
    if [[ "$CHANGED" -eq 1 ]]; then
        systemctl reload-or-restart "${SERVICE_NAME}.service"
    fi
    systemctl enable "${SERVICE_NAME}.service" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
else
    echo "Warning: ${SERVICE_NAME}.service unit not found; please restart the SSH daemon manually." >&2
fi

echo "StrictModes enforcement verified and applied successfully."
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Configure audit rules to record privilege escalation
# via setuid/setgid execution (execve calls where euid/egid != uid/gid)
#
# This script is idempotent - it can be run multiple times without
# duplicating rules.

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/50-privileged.rules"

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"

# Ensure the rules file exists
touch "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Define the rules we need, keyed by a unique identifier so we can
# check for existing equivalent rules regardless of exact formatting.
#
# Rule set covers:
#   - 32-bit (b32) and 64-bit (b64) architectures
#   - euid!=uid  -> user-ID escalation
#   - egid!=gid  -> group-ID escalation

declare -a RULES=(
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S execve -C uid!=euid -F euid=0 -k setuid"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S execve -C uid!=euid -F euid=0 -k setuid"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S execve -C gid!=egid -F egid=0 -k setgid"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S execve -C gid!=egid -F egid=0 -k setgid"
)

add_rule_if_missing() {
    local rule="$1"
    local file="$2"

    if ! grep -qxF -- "${rule}" "${file}"; then
        echo "${rule}" >> "${file}"
        echo "Added rule: ${rule}"
    else
        echo "Rule already present: ${rule}"
    fi
}

for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
    add_rule_if_missing "${rule}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
done

# Ensure correct permissions on the rules file
chmod 0640 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Reload audit rules if augenrules is available (RHEL 8 default)
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" || true
fi

# Verify auditd service status; restart if active to apply new rules
if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
    systemctl restart auditd || true
fi

echo "Audit rules for privilege escalation (setuid/setgid execve) have been configured."

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Disable IPv4 packet forwarding on all interfaces
# Idempotent - safe to run multiple times
#

set -euo pipefail

SYSCTL_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-ipv4-forwarding.conf"

declare -A SETTINGS=(
    ["net.ipv4.ip_forward"]="0"
    ["net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding"]="0"
    ["net.ipv4.conf.default.forwarding"]="0"
)

# Ensure sysctl config file exists
if [[ ! -f "${SYSCTL_FILE}" ]]; then
    touch "${SYSCTL_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${SYSCTL_FILE}"
fi

for key in "${!SETTINGS[@]}"; do
    value="${SETTINGS[$key]}"

    # Update or add the setting in the persistent sysctl file
    if grep -Eq "^\s*${key//./\\.}\s*=" "${SYSCTL_FILE}"; then
        sed -i -E "s|^\s*${key//./\\.}\s*=.*|${key} = ${value}|" "${SYSCTL_FILE}"
    else
        echo "${key} = ${value}" >> "${SYSCTL_FILE}"
    fi

    # Apply the setting at runtime
    current_value="$(sysctl -n "${key}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
    if [[ "${current_value}" != "${value}" ]]; then
        sysctl -w "${key}=${value}" >/dev/null
    fi
done

# Reload sysctl settings from all system configuration files to ensure consistency
sysctl --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# Final verification
for key in "${!SETTINGS[@]}"; do
    expected="${SETTINGS[$key]}"
    actual="$(sysctl -n "${key}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
    if [[ "${actual}" != "${expected}" ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: ${key} is set to '${actual}', expected '${expected}'" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
done

echo "IPv4 forwarding has been disabled and persisted successfully."
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Ensure the boot-time audit backlog queue can hold at least 8192 entries.
# This is applied persistently via /etc/default/grub (so it applies to any
# future kernel installs) and immediately to every currently installed
# kernel via grubby.

set -euo pipefail

PARAM="audit_backlog_limit"
VALUE="8192"
GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE="/etc/default/grub"

# --- 1. Update /etc/default/grub so future kernels get the setting ---

if [ ! -f "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}" ]; then
    echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${PARAM}=${VALUE}\"" >> "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
else
    if grep -qE "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=.*\b${PARAM}=[0-9]+" "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"; then
        # Parameter present: ensure value is correct
        current_val=$(grep -E "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}" | \
            grep -oE "${PARAM}=[0-9]+" | head -n1 | cut -d= -f2)
        if [ "${current_val}" != "${VALUE}" ]; then
            sed -i -E "s/(${PARAM}=)[0-9]+/\1${VALUE}/g" "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
        fi
    elif grep -qE "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"; then
        # GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX exists but without our parameter: insert it
        sed -i -E "/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=/ s/\"[[:space:]]*$/ ${PARAM}=${VALUE}\"/" "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
    else
        # No GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX line at all: add one
        echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${PARAM}=${VALUE}\"" >> "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
    fi
fi

# --- 2. Regenerate grub.cfg so the persistent default is reflected on disk ---

GRUB_CFG=""
if [ -d /sys/firmware/efi ]; then
    if [ -f /boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg ]; then
        GRUB_CFG="/boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg"
    fi
else
    if [ -f /boot/grub2/grub.cfg ]; then
        GRUB_CFG="/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"
    fi
fi

if [ -n "${GRUB_CFG}" ] && command -v grub2-mkconfig >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    grub2-mkconfig -o "${GRUB_CFG}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

# --- 3. Apply setting to every currently installed kernel via grubby ---

if command -v grubby >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    NEED_UPDATE=0

    while IFS= read -r kernel; do
        [ -z "${kernel}" ] && continue
        current_args=$(grubby --info="${kernel}" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^args=" | sed -E 's/^args="(.*)"$/\1/')
        if echo "${current_args}" | grep -qE "\b${PARAM}=${VALUE}\b"; then
            continue
        fi
        NEED_UPDATE=1
    done < <(grubby --info=ALL 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^kernel=" | sed -E 's/^kernel="(.*)"$/\1/')

    if [ "${NEED_UPDATE}" -eq 1 ]; then
        grubby --update-kernel=ALL \
            --remove-args="${PARAM}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        grubby --update-kernel=ALL \
            --args="${PARAM}=${VALUE}" >/dev/null 2>&1
    fi
fi

exit 0

        echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${PARAM}=${VALUE}\"" >> "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
    fi
fi

# --- 2. Regenerate grub.cfg so the persistent default is reflected on disk ---

GRUB_CFG=""
if [ -d /sys/firmware/efi ]; then
    if [ -f /boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg ]; then
        GRUB_CFG="/boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg"
    fi
else
    if [ -f /boot/grub2/grub.cfg ]; then
        GRUB_CFG="/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"
    fi
fi

if [ -n "${GRUB_CFG}" ] && command -v grub2-mkconfig >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    grub2-mkconfig -o "${GRUB_CFG}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

# --- 3. Apply setting to every currently installed kernel via grubby ---

if command -v grubby >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    NEED_UPDATE=0

    while IFS= read -r kernel; do
        [ -z "${kernel}" ] && continue
        current_args=$(grubby --info="${kernel}" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^args=" | sed -E 's/^args="(.*)"$/\1/')
        if echo "${current_args}" | grep -qE "\b${PARAM}=${VALUE}\b"; then
            continue
        fi
        NEED_UPDATE=1
    done < <(grubby --info=ALL 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^kernel=" | sed -E 's/^kernel="(.*)"$/\1/')

    if [ "${NEED_UPDATE}" -eq 1 ]; then
        grubby --update-kernel=ALL \
            --remove-args="${PARAM}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        grubby --update-kernel=ALL \
            --args="${PARAM}=${VALUE}" >/dev/null 2>&1
    fi
fi

exit 0

#
# Remediation script: Ensure rsyslog captures authpriv and daemon logs
# to /var/log/secure and /var/log/messages respectively (RHEL 8)
#
# This script is idempotent and safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

RSYSLOG_CONF="/etc/rsyslog.conf"
RSYSLOG_D_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
REMEDIATION_CONF="${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}/90-auth-daemon-logging.conf"

# Ensure rsyslog package is installed
if ! rpm -q rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf install -y rsyslog
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum install -y rsyslog
    else
        echo "ERROR: No supported package manager found to install rsyslog." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Ensure rsyslog.d directory exists
mkdir -p "${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}"

# Define the desired logging rules
declare -A RULES=(
    ["authpriv.*"]="/var/log/secure"
    ["daemon.*"]="/var/log/messages"
)

# Function to check if a given selector/action pair already exists
# (uncommented) in rsyslog.conf or any file under rsyslog.d
rule_exists() {
    local selector="$1"
    local action="$2"
    local escaped_selector escaped_action

    escaped_selector=$(printf '%s\n' "$selector" | sed -e 's/[.[\*^$]/\\&/g')
    escaped_action=$(printf '%s\n' "$action" | sed -e 's/[.[\*^$\/]/\\&/g')

    grep -rEq "^[[:space:]]*${escaped_selector}[[:space:]]+-?${escaped_action}[[:space:]]*\$" \
        "${RSYSLOG_CONF}" "${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}"/*.conf 2>/dev/null
}

# Build/update the remediation config file
{
    echo "# This file is managed by a remediation script."
    echo "# It ensures authpriv and daemon messages are logged persistently."
    echo "# Do not edit manually; changes may be overwritten."
    echo
    for selector in "${!RULES[@]}"; do
        action="${RULES[$selector]}"
        echo "${selector} ${action}"
    done
} > "${REMEDIATION_CONF}.tmp"

# Only replace if content differs, to keep idempotency clean
if [ -f "${REMEDIATION_CONF}" ] && cmp -s "${REMEDIATION_CONF}.tmp" "${REMEDIATION_CONF}"; then
    rm -f "${REMEDIATION_CONF}.tmp"
else
    mv "${REMEDIATION_CONF}.tmp" "${REMEDIATION_CONF}"
    chmod 0644 "${REMEDIATION_CONF}"
    chown root:root "${REMEDIATION_CONF}"
fi

# Remove duplicate or conflicting rules for the same selectors elsewhere
# in rsyslog.conf and other rsyslog.d files (excluding our remediation file)
# to prevent duplicate logging or conflicting configuration.
for selector in "${!RULES[@]}"; do
    escaped_selector=$(printf '%s\n' "$selector" | sed -e 's/[.[\*^$]/\\&/g')

    # Comment out duplicate active rules in rsyslog.conf
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${escaped_selector}[[:space:]]+" "${RSYSLOG_CONF}" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i -E "s|^([[:space:]]*${escaped_selector}[[:space:]]+.*)|#\1|" "${RSYSLOG_CONF}"
    fi

    # Comment out duplicate active rules in other rsyslog.d files
    for f in "${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}"/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        [ "$f" = "${REMEDIATION_CONF}" ] && continue
        if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${escaped_selector}[[:space:]]+" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
            sed -i -E "s|^([[:space:]]*${escaped_selector}[[:space:]]+.*)|#\1|" "$f"
        fi
    done
done

# Validate rsyslog configuration syntax
if command -v rsyslogd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! rsyslogd -N1 -f "${RSYSLOG_CONF}" >/tmp/rsyslog_check.log 2>&1; then
        echo "ERROR: rsyslog configuration validation failed:" >&2
        cat /tmp/rsyslog_check.log >&2
        rm -f /tmp/rsyslog_check.log
        exit 1
    fi
    rm -f /tmp/rsyslog_check.log
fi

# Ensure rsyslog service is enabled and started/restarted to apply changes
systemctl enable rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

if systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog; then
    systemctl restart rsyslog
else
    systemctl start rsyslog
fi

# Ensure the log files exist with correct permissions
for logfile in /var/log/secure /var/log/messages; do
    if [ ! -e "${logfile}" ]; then
        touch "${logfile}"
    fi
    chmod 0600 "${logfile}"
    chown root:root "${logfile}"
done

echo "Remediation applied successfully: authpriv -> /var/log/secure, daemon -> /var/log/messages"
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure auditing of privileged command execution for setfiles
#

set -euo pipefail

PROGRAM_PATH="/usr/sbin/setfiles"
KEY="privileged-priv_change"

# Determine the UID boundary for "regular" (non-system) users
if [ -f /etc/login.defs ]; then
    UID_MIN=$(awk '/^\s*UID_MIN/{print $2}' /etc/login.defs 2>/dev/null || true)
fi
if [ -z "${UID_MIN:-}" ]; then
    UID_MIN=1000
fi

# Only proceed if the binary actually exists on this system
if [ ! -f "$PROGRAM_PATH" ]; then
    exit 0
fi

# Determine architecture(s) to audit
ARCHS=()
if [ -d /usr/lib64 ]; then
    ARCHS+=("b64")
fi
if [ -d /usr/lib ]; then
    ARCHS+=("b32")
fi
if [ ${#ARCHS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    ARCHS+=("b64")
fi

build_rule() {
    local arch="$1"
    echo "-a always,exit -F path=${PROGRAM_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=${UID_MIN} -F auid!=unset -F arch=${arch} -k ${KEY}"
}

# Files to check/update: the standard rules.d directory AND the consolidated audit.rules file
RULES_D_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_D_FILE="${RULES_D_DIR}/setfiles.rules"
CONSOLIDATED_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

mkdir -p "$RULES_D_DIR"

# Build the full set of rule lines we want present
declare -a DESIRED_RULES=()
for arch in "${ARCHS[@]}"; do
    DESIRED_RULES+=("$(build_rule "$arch")")
done

# Function to ensure a line exists in a file, appending if missing
ensure_line_in_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local line="$2"

    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        touch "$file"
    fi

    if ! grep -Fxq -- "$line" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "$line" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# 1. Update the rules.d file (used by augenrules)
for rule in "${DESIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
    ensure_line_in_file "$RULES_D_FILE" "$rule"
done

# 2. Update the consolidated audit.rules file directly, in case the system
#    loads rules from this file instead of via augenrules (e.g., auditd
#    configured to load /etc/audit/audit.rules directly without regeneration).
if [ -f "$CONSOLIDATED_FILE" ]; then
    for rule in "${DESIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
        ensure_line_in_file "$CONSOLIDATED_FILE" "$rule"
    done
fi

# 3. Regenerate rules via augenrules if available (preferred mechanism on RHEL 8)
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Fallback: load rules directly into the running kernel audit system
    for rule in "${DESIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
        auditctl $rule >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    done
fi

# 4. Ensure the audit daemon rules are consistent by restarting/reloading if service exists
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd 2>/dev/null; then
        systemctl try-restart auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure systemd debug-shell service is disabled and masked
# Idempotent - safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

SERVICE="debug-shell.service"

log() {
    printf '%s\n' "$*"
}

require_root() {
    if [[ "${EUID}" -ne 0 ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
}

require_systemctl() {
    if ! command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "ERROR: systemctl not found. This system may not use systemd." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
}

main() {
    require_root
    require_systemctl

    log "Remediating ${SERVICE}..."

    # Stop the service if it is currently active/running.
    if systemctl is-active --quiet "${SERVICE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        log "Stopping running ${SERVICE}..."
        systemctl stop "${SERVICE}"
    else
        log "${SERVICE} is not currently active."
    fi

    # Disable the service if it is enabled (idempotent - disable is safe even if already disabled).
    if systemctl is-enabled --quiet "${SERVICE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        log "Disabling ${SERVICE}..."
        systemctl disable "${SERVICE}"
    else
        log "${SERVICE} is already disabled (or not enabled)."
    fi

    # Mask the service to prevent it from being started manually or by any dependency,
    # and to make the change persist across reboots.
    current_state="$(systemctl is-enabled "${SERVICE}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [[ "${current_state}" == "masked" ]]; then
        log "${SERVICE} is already masked."
    else
        log "Masking ${SERVICE}..."
        systemctl mask "${SERVICE}"
    fi

    # Double-check final state and stop again in case masking started something (defensive).
    if systemctl is-active --quiet "${SERVICE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        log "Stopping ${SERVICE} after masking (unexpected active state)..."
        systemctl stop "${SERVICE}"
    fi

    # Reload systemd manager configuration to ensure the mask symlink is recognized.
    systemctl daemon-reload

    # Verify final state.
    final_state="$(systemctl is-enabled "${SERVICE}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [[ "${final_state}" == "masked" ]]; then
        log "SUCCESS: ${SERVICE} is masked and will not start, persisting across reboots."
    else
        echo "ERROR: Failed to mask ${SERVICE}. Current state: ${final_state}" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    if systemctl is-active --quiet "${SERVICE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "ERROR: ${SERVICE} is still active after remediation." >&2
        exit 1
    else
        log "Verified: ${SERVICE} is not active."
    fi
}

main "$@"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Disable KerberosAuthentication in SSHD configuration
# Ensures idempotent, persistent configuration on RHEL 8 systems.
#

set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
SETTING_NAME="KerberosAuthentication"
DESIRED_VALUE="no"
DESIRED_LINE="${SETTING_NAME} ${DESIRED_VALUE}"

# Ensure the main sshd_config file exists
if [[ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}" ]]; then
    echo "Error: ${SSHD_CONFIG} does not exist." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Backup the current configuration before making changes
BACKUP_FILE="${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}" "${BACKUP_FILE}"

# Function to comment out all existing (active) KerberosAuthentication lines
# in the main config and any drop-in files under sshd_config.d, so only
# our authoritative setting takes effect.
remove_active_settings() {
    local file="$1"

    if [[ -f "${file}" ]]; then
        # Match lines that set KerberosAuthentication (case-insensitive),
        # ignoring leading whitespace, and comment them out unless they
        # are already commented.
        sed -i.bak_tmp -E \
            "s/^[[:space:]]*(${SETTING_NAME})[[:space:]]+(yes|no)([[:space:]]*)$/# \1 \2 (disabled by remediation script)/I" \
            "${file}"
        rm -f "${file}.bak_tmp"
    fi
}

# Process main sshd_config
remove_active_settings "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

# Process drop-in configuration directory files, if present
if [[ -d "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}" ]]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' conf_file; do
        remove_active_settings "${conf_file}"
    done < <(find "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name "*.conf" -print0 2>/dev/null || true)
fi

# Now ensure the desired setting is present and correct in the main config.
# We append it at the end (after any Match blocks would typically be placed
# earlier, but since KerberosAuthentication is a global directive, we place
# it before any 'Match' block if one exists, otherwise at the end).

MATCH_LINE_NUM=$(grep -n -E "^[[:space:]]*Match[[:space:]]" "${SSHD_CONFIG}" | head -n1 | cut -d: -f1 || true)

if grep -q -E "^[[:space:]]*${SETTING_NAME}[[:space:]]+${DESIRED_VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"; then
    : # Desired setting already present and active; nothing to do.
else
    if [[ -n "${MATCH_LINE_NUM}" ]]; then
        # Insert before the first Match block
        sed -i "${MATCH_LINE_NUM}i ${DESIRED_LINE}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
    else

if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd 2>/dev/null; then
    systemctl reload sshd 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart sshd
elif systemctl is-active --quiet ssh 2>/dev/null; then
    systemctl reload ssh 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart ssh

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Disable KerberosAuthentication in SSHD configuration
# Ensures idempotent, persistent configuration on RHEL 8 systems.
#

set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
SETTING_NAME="KerberosAuthentication"
DESIRED_VALUE="no"
DESIRED_LINE="${SETTING_NAME} ${DESIRED_VALUE}"

# Ensure the main sshd_config file exists
if [[ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}" ]]; then
    echo "Error: ${SSHD_CONFIG} does not exist." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Backup the current configuration before making changes
BACKUP_FILE="${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}" "${BACKUP_FILE}"

# Function to comment out all existing (active) KerberosAuthentication lines
# in the main config and any drop-in files under sshd_config.d, so only
# our authoritative setting takes effect.
remove_active_settings() {
    local file="$1"

    if [[ -f "${file}" ]]; then
        # Match lines that set KerberosAuthentication (case-insensitive),
        # ignoring leading whitespace, and comment them out unless they
        # are already commented.
        sed -i.bak_tmp -E \
            "s/^[[:space:]]*(${SETTING_NAME})[[:space:]]+(yes|no)([[:space:]]*)$/# \1 \2 (disabled by remediation script)/I" \
            "${file}"
        rm -f "${file}.bak_tmp"
    fi
}

# Process main sshd_config
remove_active_settings "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

# Process drop-in configuration directory files, if present
if [[ -d "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}" ]]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' conf_file; do
        remove_active_settings "${conf_file}"
    done < <(find "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name "*.conf" -print0 2>/dev/null || true)
fi

# Now ensure the desired setting is present and correct in the main config.
# We append it at the end (after any Match blocks would typically be placed
# earlier, but since KerberosAuthentication is a global directive, we place
# it before any 'Match' block if one exists, otherwise at the end).

MATCH_LINE_NUM=$(grep -n -E "^[[:space:]]*Match[[:space:]]" "${SSHD_CONFIG}" | head -n1 | cut -d: -f1 || true)

if grep -q -E "^[[:space:]]*${SETTING_NAME}[[:space:]]+${DESIRED_VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"; then
    : # Desired setting already present and active; nothing to do.
else
    if [[ -n "${MATCH_LINE_NUM}" ]]; then
        # Insert before the first Match block
        sed -i "${MATCH_LINE_NUM}i ${DESIRED_LINE}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
    else
        # No Match block; append to end of file
        printf '\n%s\n' "${DESIRED_LINE}" >> "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
    fi
fi

# Validate the resulting configuration syntax before applying
if ! sshd -t -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Error: sshd_config failed validation after modification. Restoring backup." >&2
    cp -p "${BACKUP_FILE}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
    exit 1
fi

# Reload or restart the SSH service to apply changes, if the service is active
if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd 2>/dev/null; then
    systemctl reload sshd 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart sshd
elif systemctl is-active --quiet ssh 2>/dev/null; then
    systemctl reload ssh 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart ssh
fi

echo "KerberosAuthentication has been set to '${DESIRED_VALUE}' in ${SSHD_CONFIG}."
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script to disable SCTP kernel module support on RHEL 8
# This script is idempotent and can be run multiple times safely.

set -euo pipefail

MODULE_NAME="sctp"
MODPROBE_CONF_DIR="/etc/modprobe.d"
MODPROBE_CONF_FILE="${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}/sctp.conf"

# Ensure the modprobe.d directory exists
if [ ! -d "${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}"
fi

# Create or update the modprobe configuration file to prevent loading
if [ ! -f "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}" ]; then
    touch "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}"
fi

# Ensure "install sctp /bin/false" line is present (prevents module install)
if ! grep -Pq '^\s*install\s+sctp\s+/bin/false\s*$' "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "install ${MODULE_NAME} /bin/false" >> "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}"
fi

# Ensure "blacklist sctp" line is present (prevents auto-loading)
if ! grep -Pq '^\s*blacklist\s+sctp\s*$' "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "blacklist ${MODULE_NAME}" >> "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}"
fi

# If the module is currently loaded, attempt to unload it
if lsmod | grep -q "^${MODULE_NAME}\b"; then
    modprobe -r "${MODULE_NAME}" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Update initramfs to ensure changes persist across reboots and dracut rebuilds
if command -v dracut >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dracut -f
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script for USBGuard: generate an initial device-authorization
# policy reflecting currently connected USB devices, ensuring existing
# devices remain permitted and preventing lockout of USB keyboards/mice.
#
# This script is idempotent: it can be run multiple times safely.

set -euo pipefail

USBGUARD_BIN="/usr/sbin/usbguard"
RULES_FILE="/etc/usbguard/rules.conf"
BACKUP_DIR="/etc/usbguard/backups"
TIMESTAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Ensure usbguard package/binary is present
if ! command -v "$USBGUARD_BIN" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf install -y usbguard
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum install -y usbguard
    else
        echo "Error: usbguard is not installed and no supported package manager was found." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Re-check binary availability after potential install
if ! command -v "$USBGUARD_BIN" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Error: usbguard binary not found after installation attempt." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure directories exist
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$RULES_FILE")"
mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"

# Determine if the current rules file is "empty" (no effective rules)
# An empty policy means: file doesn't exist, is zero-length, or contains
# only comments/whitespace.
is_policy_empty() {
    if [ ! -f "$RULES_FILE" ]; then
        return 0
    fi
    if ! grep -q -E '^[^#[:space:]]' "$RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi
    return 1
}

# Generate a fresh policy from currently connected devices
generate_policy() {
    local tmp_file
    tmp_file="$(mktemp /etc/usbguard/rules.conf.XXXXXX)"

    if ! "$USBGUARD_BIN" generate-policy > "$tmp_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Error: failed to generate USBGuard policy from current devices." >&2
        rm -f "$tmp_file"
        exit 1
    fi

    if [ ! -s "$tmp_file" ]; then
        echo "Error: generated USBGuard policy is empty; refusing to apply." >&2
        rm -f "$tmp_file"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Backup existing rules file if present
    if [ -f "$RULES_FILE" ]; then
        cp -a "$RULES_FILE" "${BACKUP_DIR}/rules.conf.${TIMESTAMP}.bak"
    fi

    install -m 0600 -o root -g root "$tmp_file" "$RULES_FILE"
    rm -f "$tmp_file"
}

# Only regenerate if the policy is currently empty/missing.
if is_policy_empty; then
    echo "USBGuard policy is empty or missing; generating policy from currently connected devices."
    generate_policy
else
    echo "USBGuard policy already contains rules; leaving existing policy in place."
fi

# Ensure correct permissions regardless of path taken
chmod 0600 "$RULES_FILE"
chown root:root "$RULES_FILE"

# Ensure the usbguard service is enabled and running so the policy persists
# across reboots and is actively enforced.
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable usbguard.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    if systemctl is-active --quiet usbguard.service; then
        systemctl reload-or-restart usbguard.service
    else
        systemctl start usbguard.service
    fi
else
    echo "Warning: systemctl not found; cannot manage usbguard service state." >&2
fi

echo "USBGuard policy remediation complete. Current rules file: $RULES_FILE"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation script: Configure systemd-logind to terminate idle user
# sessions automatically after a defined period of inactivity.
#
# This script is idempotent: it can be run multiple times safely and
# will always converge /etc/systemd/logind.conf to the desired state.

set -euo pipefail

readonly LOGIND_CONF="/etc/systemd/logind.conf"
readonly SETTING_NAME="StopIdleSessionSec"
readonly SETTING_VALUE="900"   # 15 minutes of inactivity
readonly DESIRED_LINE="${SETTING_NAME}=${SETTING_VALUE}"

main() {
    ensure_root
    ensure_config_file_exists
    backup_config_file
    apply_setting
    reload_logind
}

ensure_root() {
    if [[ "${EUID}" -ne 0 ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
}

ensure_config_file_exists() {
    if [[ ! -f "${LOGIND_CONF}" ]]; then
        echo "INFO: ${LOGIND_CONF} not found, creating it."
        mkdir -p "$(dirname "${LOGIND_CONF}")"
        cat > "${LOGIND_CONF}" <<'EOF'
#  This file is part of systemd.
#
# Entries in this file show the compile time defaults. Local configuration
# should be created by editing or adding files in /etc/systemd/logind.conf.d/.
#
# See logind.conf(5) for details.

[Login]
EOF
    fi
}

backup_config_file() {
    local timestamp
    timestamp="$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
    cp -p "${LOGIND_CONF}" "${LOGIND_CONF}.bak.${timestamp}"
}

apply_setting() {
    # Ensure a [Login] section header exists; if not, add one.
    if ! grep -qE '^\s*\[Login\]\s*$' "${LOGIND_CONF}"; then
        printf '\n[Login]\n' >> "${LOGIND_CONF}"
    fi

    # Case 1: An active (uncommented) StopIdleSessionSec line already exists.
    if grep -qE "^\s*${SETTING_NAME}\s*=" "${LOGIND_CONF}"; then
        # Update it in place if the value differs.
        if ! grep -qE "^\s*${SETTING_NAME}\s*=\s*${SETTING_VALUE}\s*$" "${LOGIND_CONF}"; then
            sed -i -E "s|^\s*${SETTING_NAME}\s*=.*$|${DESIRED_LINE}|" "${LOGIND_CONF}"
            echo "INFO: Updated ${SETTING_NAME} to ${SETTING_VALUE} in ${LOGIND_CONF}."
        else
            echo "INFO: ${SETTING_NAME} already set to ${SETTING_VALUE} in ${LOGIND_CONF}."
        fi
        return
    fi

    # Case 2: A commented-out StopIdleSessionSec line exists (e.g. "#StopIdleSessionSec=").
    if grep -qE "^\s*#\s*${SETTING_NAME}\s*=" "${LOGIND_CONF}"; then
        sed -i -E "s|^\s*#\s*${SETTING_NAME}\s*=.*$|${DESIRED_LINE}|" "${LOGIND_CONF}"
        echo "INFO: Uncommented and set ${SETTING_NAME} to ${SETTING_VALUE} in ${LOGIND_CONF}."
        return
    fi

    # Case 3: No StopIdleSessionSec line at all. Insert it right after [Login].
    sed -i -E "0,/^\s*\[Login\]\s*$/ s|^(\s*\[Login\]\s*)$|\1\n${DESIRED_LINE}|" "${LOGIND_CONF}"
    echo "INFO: Added ${DESIRED_LINE} under [Login] in ${LOGIND_CONF}."
}

reload_logind() {
    if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        systemctl restart systemd-logind.service || {
            echo "WARNING: Failed to restart systemd-logind.service. A reboot may be required." >&2
        }
    fi
}

main "$@"

    echo "INFO: Added ${DESIRED_LINE} under [Login] in ${LOGIND_CONF}."
}

reload_logind() {
    if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        systemctl restart systemd-logind.service || {
            echo "WARNING: Failed to restart systemd-logind.service. A reboot may be required." >&2
        }
    fi
}

main "$@"
```

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure auditd collects successful use of fchown/lchown/chown
# (and their 32-bit / *at variants) for non-system users (UID >= 1000, excluding -1)
# on both 32-bit and 64-bit syscall interfaces, tagged with key "perm_mod".
#
# This script is idempotent: running it multiple times will not create duplicate rules.

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules"
UID_MIN=1000
KEY="perm_mod"

# Ensure the audit rules directory and base rules file exist
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d
touch "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

# Determine architecture support
declare -a ARCHES=("b64")
if [ -d /lib64 ] && [ -f /usr/bin/audit-arch-check ] 2>/dev/null; then
    :
fi

# Always include both b32 and b64 rules for RHEL 8 (dual arch support),
# but detect if system actually supports 32-bit userspace (multilib) as well.
ARCHES=("b64")
if [ -e /usr/lib/systemd/system/multi-user.target ] && \
   { [ -d /lib ] || [ -d /usr/lib ]; } && \
   uname -m | grep -qE '^(x86_64|aarch64|ppc64|s390x)$'; then
    ARCHES=("b64" "b32")
fi

# Build the list of syscalls that change file ownership
SYSCALLS="chown fchown lchown fchownat"

# Function to build a single audit rule line for a given arch
build_rule() {
    local arch="$1"
    echo "-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S ${SYSCALLS// /,} -F auid>=${UID_MIN} -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"
}

# Function to check if an equivalent rule (arch + syscalls + key) already exists
rule_exists() {
    local arch="$1"
    local pattern
    pattern="-F arch=${arch} -S ${SYSCALLS// /,} -F auid>=${UID_MIN} -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"
    grep -F -- "$pattern" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1
}

CHANGED=0

for arch in "${ARCHES[@]}"; do
    if ! rule_exists "$arch"; then
        build_rule "$arch" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
done

# Remove potential duplicate blank lines
sed -i '/^$/N;/^\n$/D' "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

# Reload audit rules if augenrules is available (preferred on RHEL 8)
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

if [ "$CHANGED" -eq 1 ]; then
    echo "Audit rules for file ownership changes (key: ${KEY}) have been added/updated in ${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}."
else
    echo "Audit rules for file ownership changes (key: ${KEY}) already present in ${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}. No changes made."
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Disable TIPC (Transparent Inter-Process Communication)
# protocol kernel module support.
#
# This script is idempotent and safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

MODULE_NAME="tipc"
MODPROBE_CONF_DIR="/etc/modprobe.d"
MODPROBE_CONF_FILE="${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}/${MODULE_NAME}.conf"

echo "Starting remediation: disabling ${MODULE_NAME} kernel module."

# Ensure the modprobe.d directory exists
if [ ! -d "${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}"
fi

# Ensure the configuration file exists
if [ ! -f "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}" ]; then
    touch "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}"
fi

# Add "install tipc /bin/true" directive if not already present
if ! grep -Pq -- '^\s*install\s+tipc\s+/bin/(true|false)\s*$' "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "install ${MODULE_NAME} /bin/true" >> "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}"
    echo "Added 'install ${MODULE_NAME} /bin/true' directive to ${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}"
else
    echo "'install ${MODULE_NAME}' directive already present in ${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}"
fi

# Add "blacklist tipc" directive if not already present
if ! grep -Pq -- '^\s*blacklist\s+tipc\s*$' "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "blacklist ${MODULE_NAME}" >> "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}"
    echo "Added 'blacklist ${MODULE_NAME}' directive to ${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}"
else
    echo "'blacklist ${MODULE_NAME}' directive already present in ${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}"
fi

# If the module is currently loaded, attempt to unload it
if lsmod | grep -q "^${MODULE_NAME}\b"; then
    echo "Module ${MODULE_NAME} is currently loaded. Attempting to unload it."
    if modprobe -r "${MODULE_NAME}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Successfully unloaded ${MODULE_NAME} module."
    else
        echo "WARNING: Unable to unload ${MODULE_NAME} module. It may be in use."
        echo "The module will remain blocked from loading on next boot."
    fi
else
    echo "Module ${MODULE_NAME} is not currently loaded."
fi

# Update the initramfs to ensure persistence across reboots, if dracut is available
if command -v dracut >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Regenerating initramfs to ensure persistence."
    dracut -f >/dev/null 2>&1 || echo "WARNING: dracut regeneration encountered an issue."
else
    echo "dracut not found; skipping initramfs regeneration."
fi

echo "Remediation complete: ${MODULE_NAME} module is now blocked from loading."

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Disable the Ctrl-Alt-Del burst action (7x within 2 seconds)
# so systemd no longer triggers a reboot (or any other action) when it is detected.
#
# This is idempotent and persists across reboots.

set -euo pipefail

CTRL_ALT_DEL_BURST_ACTION_FILE="/etc/systemd/system.conf"
DROPIN_DIR="/etc/systemd/system.conf.d"
DROPIN_FILE="${DROPIN_DIR}/99-ctrlaltdel-burst.conf"

# Ensure the drop-in directory exists
if [ ! -d "${DROPIN_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${DROPIN_DIR}"
fi

# Create/overwrite drop-in file with the desired setting
cat > "${DROPIN_FILE}" <<EOF
[Manager]
CtrlAltDelBurstAction=none
EOF

# Also ensure the main system.conf reflects this setting (idempotent update)
if grep -Eq '^[#[:space:]]*CtrlAltDelBurstAction=' "${CTRL_ALT_DEL_BURST_ACTION_FILE}"; then
    sed -i -E 's/^[#[:space:]]*CtrlAltDelBurstAction=.*/CtrlAltDelBurstAction=none/' "${CTRL_ALT_DEL_BURST_ACTION_FILE}"
else
    if grep -q '^\[Manager\]' "${CTRL_ALT_DEL_BURST_ACTION_FILE}"; then
        sed -i '/^\[Manager\]/a CtrlAltDelBurstAction=none' "${CTRL_ALT_DEL_BURST_ACTION_FILE}"
    else
        printf '\n[Manager]\nCtrlAltDelBurstAction=none\n' >> "${CTRL_ALT_DEL_BURST_ACTION_FILE}"
    fi
fi

# Reload systemd manager configuration to apply the change immediately
systemctl daemon-reexec

# Verify the setting is active
CURRENT_VALUE=$(systemctl show -p CtrlAltDelBurstAction | cut -d'=' -f2)
if [ "${CURRENT_VALUE}" != "none" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to set CtrlAltDelBurstAction to none (current value: ${CURRENT_VALUE})" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "CtrlAltDelBurstAction successfully set to 'none' and persisted."
exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation script: Configure SSH server to probe clients for
# network responsiveness and disconnect unresponsive clients after
# a 10-minute (600 second) interval of no response.
#
# This is achieved by setting:
#   ClientAliveInterval 600
#   ClientAliveCountMax 0
#
# ClientAliveInterval defines how often (in seconds) the server
# sends a null packet to the client to check responsiveness.
# ClientAliveCountMax defines how many such probes may go
# unanswered before the session is disconnected. Setting it to 0
# means the session is terminated after the first unanswered probe,
# giving an effective unresponsive-client timeout of 600 seconds.
#
# The script is idempotent: it can be run multiple times safely and
# will converge the configuration to the desired state without
# duplicating or corrupting the sshd_config file. Changes persist
# across reboots because they are written to /etc/ssh/sshd_config.

set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
BACKUP_SUFFIX=".bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

CLIENT_ALIVE_INTERVAL_VALUE="600"
CLIENT_ALIVE_COUNT_MAX_VALUE="0"

if [[ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: ${SSHD_CONFIG} not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Take a backup before making any changes, but only once per run.
cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}${BACKUP_SUFFIX}"

set_sshd_option() {
    local key="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local file="$3"

    # Match the key at the start of a line, allowing for leading
    # whitespace and being case-insensitive, whether commented out
    # or not, and whether followed by whitespace or '='.
    local pattern="^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]+.*$"

    if grep -Eiq "${pattern}" "${file}"; then
        # Replace all matching lines (commented or not) with a single
        # canonical uncommented directive. Use a temp file for safety.
        local tmpfile
        tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
        awk -v key="${key}" -v val="${value}" '
            BEGIN {
                IGNORECASE = 1
                done = 0
            }
            {
                line = $0
                # Strip leading whitespace for matching purposes
                stripped = line
                sub(/^[[:space:]]*/, "", stripped)
                if (stripped ~ ("^#?[[:space:]]*" key "[[:space:]]+")) {
                    if (!done) {
                        print key " " val
                        done = 1
                    }
                    # skip duplicate matches (comment or otherwise)
                    next
                }
                print line
            }
        ' "${file}" > "${tmpfile}"

        # If for some reason no line got written (shouldn't happen
        # given grep matched), append instead.
        if ! grep -Eiq "^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]+${value//./\\.}[[:space:]]*$" "${tmpfile}"; then
            if ! grep -Eiq "^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]+" "${tmpfile}"; then
                printf '%s %s\n' "${key}" "${value}" >> "${tmpfile}"
            fi
        fi

        mv "${tmpfile}" "${file}"
    else
        # Key not present at all; append it.
        printf '%s %s\n' "${key}" "${value}" >> "${file}"
    fi
}

set_sshd_option "ClientAliveInterval" "${CLIENT_ALIVE_INTERVAL_VALUE}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
set_sshd_option "ClientAliveCountMax" "${CLIENT_ALIVE_COUNT_MAX_VALUE}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

chmod 0600 "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
chown root:root "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

# Validate the configuration before applying/restarting the service.
if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sshd -t -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}"; then
        echo "ERROR: sshd_config failed validation after modification." >&2
        echo "Restoring backup." >&2
        cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}${BACKUP_SUFFIX}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Determine the correct service name (sshd on RHEL 8) and reload/restart
# it so the new settings take effect. Enable it so settings persist
# across reboots (service enabled + config file persisted).
SERVICE_NAME="sshd"

if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q "^${SERVICE_NAME}\.service"; then
    systemctl enable "${SERVICE_NAME}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    systemctl reload-or-restart "${SERVICE_NAME}"
else
    echo "WARNING: Could not find ${SERVICE_NAME}.service unit to restart." >&2
fi

echo "SSH ClientAliveInterval and ClientAliveCountMax have been enforced."
echo "ClientAliveInterval=${CLIENT_ALIVE_INTERVAL_VALUE}, ClientAliveCountMax=${CLIENT_ALIVE_COUNT_MAX_VALUE}"

# Validate the configuration before applying/restarting the service.
if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sshd -t -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}"; then
        echo "ERROR: sshd_config failed validation after modification." >&2
        echo "Restoring backup." >&2
        cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}${BACKUP_SUFFIX}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Determine the correct service name (sshd on RHEL 8) and reload/restart
# it so the new settings take effect. Enable it so settings persist
# across reboots (service enabled + config file persisted).
SERVICE_NAME="sshd"

if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q "^${SERVICE_NAME}\.service"; then
    systemctl enable "${SERVICE_NAME}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    systemctl reload-or-restart "${SERVICE_NAME}"
else
    echo "WARNING: Could not find ${SERVICE_NAME}.service unit to restart." >&2
fi

echo "SSH ClientAliveInterval and ClientAliveCountMax have been enforced."
echo "ClientAliveInterval=${CLIENT_ALIVE_INTERVAL_VALUE}, ClientAliveCountMax=${CLIENT_ALIVE_COUNT_MAX_VALUE}"

RULE_FILE_RULESD="/etc/audit/rules.d/50-access.rules"
RULE_FILE_AUDITD="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
KEY="access"

# Build the four required rules (32-bit and 64-bit, EACCES and EPERM)
RULES=(
"-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S truncate,ftruncate -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S truncate,ftruncate -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S truncate,ftruncate -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S truncate,ftruncate -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${KEY}"
)

add_rule_to_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local rule="$2"

    # Ensure the target directory exists
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$file")"

    # Create file if it doesn't exist
    if [[ ! -f "$file" ]]; then
        touch "$file"
    fi

    if ! grep -Fxq -- "$rule" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Determine which rule-loading mechanism the audit daemon uses.
# RHEL 8 typically uses augenrules (rules.d) to build audit.rules at startup,
# but some systems may still rely directly on /etc/audit/audit.rules
# loaded via auditctl. We support both, persisting to whichever is in use.

USE_AUGENRULES=0

if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1 && [[ -d /etc/audit/rules.d ]]; then
    USE_AUGENRULES=1
fi

if [[ "$USE_AUGENRULES" -eq 1 ]]; then
    for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
        add_rule_to_file "$RULE_FILE_RULESD" "$rule"
    done

    # Load rules using augenrules mechanism
    augenrules --load

else
    for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
        add_rule_to_file "$RULE_FILE_AUDITD" "$rule"
    done

    # Load rules directly using auditctl if the daemon is running
    if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
            auditctl $rule 2>/dev/null || true
        done
    fi
fi

# Restart the audit daemon to ensure rules persist and are active,
# if the service is present and manageable via systemctl.
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd 2>/dev/null; then
        systemctl try-restart auditd || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure pam_faillock is active in the password-auth
# (and system-auth) PAM stack on RHEL 8 to enforce account lockout
# on repeated authentication failures.
#
# This script is idempotent: it can be run multiple times safely and
# will only make changes when the faillock feature is not already
# enabled via authselect.

set -euo pipefail

FAILLOCK_FEATURE="with-faillock"
CUSTOM_PROFILE_SHORT="hardened-faillock"
CUSTOM_PROFILE_FULL="custom/${CUSTOM_PROFILE_SHORT}"

if ! command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: authselect is not available on this system." >&2
    exit 1
fi

current_output="$(authselect current 2>/dev/null || true)"

if [ -z "$current_output" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Unable to determine current authselect profile." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Check whether the with-faillock feature is already enabled
if echo "$current_output" | grep -qx "- ${FAILLOCK_FEATURE}"; then
    echo "pam_faillock feature already enabled via authselect. No changes needed."
else
    # Determine the currently selected profile ID (e.g. sssd, winbind, custom/foo)
    current_profile="$(echo "$current_output" | awk -F': ' '/^Profile ID:/ {print $2}')"

    if [ -z "$current_profile" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: Could not parse current authselect profile ID." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    if [[ "$current_profile" == custom/* ]]; then
        # Already using a custom profile: just enable the feature on it
        target_profile="$current_profile"
    else
        # Not using a custom profile: create one based on the current profile,
        # unless it already exists from a previous run.
        target_profile="$CUSTOM_PROFILE_FULL"

        if authselect list | grep -qx "$CUSTOM_PROFILE_FULL"; then
            : # custom profile already exists, reuse it
        else
            authselect create-profile "$CUSTOM_PROFILE_SHORT" \
                --base-on "$current_profile" \
                --symlink-meta
        fi
    fi

    authselect select "$target_profile" "$FAILLOCK_FEATURE" --force
fi

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure every configured chrony (NTP) time source
# has an explicit maximum polling interval ("maxpoll") set, and that
# this setting persists across reboots via /etc/chrony.conf.
#
# Idempotent: running this script multiple times will not duplicate or
# corrupt the configuration; if the desired state already exists, no
# changes are made.

set -euo pipefail

CHRONY_CONF="/etc/chrony.conf"
MAXPOLL_VALUE=10

# Ensure chrony is installed and the configuration file exists.
if [[ ! -f "${CHRONY_CONF}" ]]; then
    echo "Error: ${CHRONY_CONF} not found. Is chrony installed?" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Create a timestamped backup before making any changes.
BACKUP="${CHRONY_CONF}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
cp -p "${CHRONY_CONF}" "${BACKUP}"

TMP_FILE="$(mktemp)"

# Process the configuration:
#   - For every "server" or "pool" directive, ensure a "maxpoll <value>"
#     option is present.
#   - If "maxpoll" already exists, normalize its value to MAXPOLL_VALUE.
#   - If it does not exist, append it to the line.
awk -v maxpoll="${MAXPOLL_VALUE}" '
{
    line = $0
    if (line ~ /^[[:space:]]*(server|pool)[[:space:]]+/) {
        if (line ~ /[[:space:]]maxpoll[[:space:]]+[0-9]+/) {
            gsub(/maxpoll[[:space:]]+[0-9]+/, "maxpoll " maxpoll, line)
        } else {
            line = line " maxpoll " maxpoll
        }
    }
    print line
}
' "${CHRONY_CONF}" > "${TMP_FILE}"

# Only replace the configuration file if something actually changed,
# preserving idempotency and avoiding unnecessary service restarts.
if ! diff -q "${CHRONY_CONF}" "${TMP_FILE}" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    install -m 0644 -o root -g root "${TMP_FILE}" "${CHRONY_CONF}"
    rm -f "${TMP_FILE}"
    CHANGED=1
else
    rm -f "${TMP_FILE}"
    CHANGED=0
fi

# Remove the backup if no changes were needed, keep it otherwise.
if [[ "${CHANGED}" -eq 0 ]]; then
    rm -f "${BACKUP}"
fi

# Ensure chronyd is enabled and running so the configuration persists
# across reboots and takes effect immediately.
systemctl enable chronyd > /dev/null 2>&1 || true

if [[ "${CHANGED}" -eq 1 ]]; then
    systemctl restart chronyd
else
    systemctl is-active --quiet chronyd || systemctl start chronyd
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure chronyd does not listen on its network
# command/management port (cmdport). This enforces cmdport 0 in
# /etc/chrony.conf, removes any conflicting cmdport directives, and
# restarts chronyd so the change takes effect immediately. The change
# persists across reboots because it is written to the configuration
# file on disk.
#
# Idempotent: running multiple times results in the same final state.

set -euo pipefail

CHRONY_CONF="/etc/chrony.conf"
DESIRED_LINE="cmdport 0"

if [[ ! -f "${CHRONY_CONF}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: ${CHRONY_CONF} not found. Is chrony installed?" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Backup original file once, before any modification, if no backup exists yet.
if [[ ! -f "${CHRONY_CONF}.bak" ]]; then
    cp -p "${CHRONY_CONF}" "${CHRONY_CONF}.bak"
fi

# Remove any existing (active or commented) cmdport lines to avoid duplicates
# or conflicting settings, regardless of spacing/case.
TMP_FILE="$(mktemp)"
grep -Ev '^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*[Cc][Mm][Dd][Pp][Oo][Rr][Tt][[:space:]]+[0-9]+[[:space:]]*$' \
    "${CHRONY_CONF}" > "${TMP_FILE}" || true

# Append the desired directive at the end of the file.
printf '%s\n' "${DESIRED_LINE}" >> "${TMP_FILE}"

# Only replace the config if content actually changed, to keep the script
# idempotent and avoid unnecessary writes/restarts.
if ! cmp -s "${TMP_FILE}" "${CHRONY_CONF}"; then
    install -m 0644 -o root -g root "${TMP_FILE}" "${CHRONY_CONF}"
    CONFIG_CHANGED=1
else
    CONFIG_CHANGED=0
fi
rm -f "${TMP_FILE}"

# Determine correct systemd unit name (chronyd vs chrony, depending on distro
# naming), preferring chronyd as used on RHEL 8.
SERVICE_NAME="chronyd"
if ! systemctl list-unit-files --type=service 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^${SERVICE_NAME}\.service"; then
    if systemctl list-unit-files --type=service 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^chrony\.service'; then
        SERVICE_NAME="chrony"
    fi
fi

# Restart the service only if the configuration changed, or if the service
# is not currently running (to ensure the running instance reflects the
# desired state). This keeps the script idempotent while still enforcing
# state on repeated runs.
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if [[ "${CONFIG_CHANGED}" -eq 1 ]] || ! systemctl is-active --quiet "${SERVICE_NAME}"; then
        systemctl restart "${SERVICE_NAME}"
    fi
    systemctl enable "${SERVICE_NAME}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

# Remediation script to disable and blacklist the USB storage kernel module
# on RHEL 8 systems. This is idempotent and safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

MODULE_NAME="usb-storage"
MODPROBE_CONF_DIR="/etc/modprobe.d"
MODPROBE_CONF_FILE="${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}/usb-storage-blacklist.conf"

# Ensure the modprobe.d directory exists
if [ ! -d "${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}"
fi

# Create or update the modprobe configuration file to:
# 1. Blacklist the module so it is not automatically loaded
# 2. Set install to /bin/false to prevent it from being loaded via modprobe
TMP_FILE="$(mktemp)"

{
    echo "install ${MODULE_NAME} /bin/false"
    echo "blacklist ${MODULE_NAME}"
} > "${TMP_FILE}"

if [ ! -f "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}" ] || ! diff -q "${TMP_FILE}" "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    install -m 0644 "${TMP_FILE}" "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}"
fi

rm -f "${TMP_FILE}"

# If the module is currently loaded, attempt to unload it.
if lsmod | grep -q "^${MODULE_NAME//-/_}[[:space:]]"; then
    modprobe -r "${MODULE_NAME}" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Verify the module cannot be loaded (best effort check, does not fail script)
if command -v modprobe >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if modprobe -n -v "${MODULE_NAME}" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^install /bin/false"; then
        :
    fi
fi

exit 0

if [ ! -d "${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}"
fi

# Create or update the modprobe configuration file to:
# 1. Blacklist the module so it is not automatically loaded
# 2. Set install to /bin/false to prevent it from being loaded via modprobe
TMP_FILE="$(mktemp)"

{
    echo "install ${MODULE_NAME} /bin/false"
    echo "blacklist ${MODULE_NAME}"
} > "${TMP_FILE}"

if [ ! -f "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}" ] || ! diff -q "${TMP_FILE}" "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    install -m 0644 "${TMP_FILE}" "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}"
fi

rm -f "${TMP_FILE}"

# If the module is currently loaded, attempt to unload it.
if lsmod | grep -q "^${MODULE_NAME//-/_}[[:space:]]"; then
    modprobe -r "${MODULE_NAME}" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Verify the module cannot be loaded (best effort check, does not fail script)
if command -v modprobe >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if modprobe -n -v "${MODULE_NAME}" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^install /bin/false"; then
        :
    fi
fi

exit 0

# Remediation script: Ensure GNOME screensaver lock delay is 0 seconds
# so the screen locks immediately upon screensaver activation.
# Idempotent - safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
DCONF_LOCKS_DIR="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/locks"
DCONF_PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"
DCONF_PROFILE_FILE="${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}/user"
SETTINGS_FILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/00-screensaver"
LOCK_FILE="${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}/00-screensaver"

mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}"

# Ensure dconf profile file exists and contains required entries
if [ ! -f "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" ]; then
    printf 'user-db:user\nsystem-db:local\n' > "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
else

    grep -qxF 'system-db:local' "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" || \

if [ ! -f "${SETTINGS_FILE}" ]; then
    printf '[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]\nlock-delay=uint32 0\n' > "${SETTINGS_FILE}"
else
    if ! grep -q '^\[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver\]' "${SETTINGS_FILE}"; then
        printf '\n[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]\nlock-delay=uint32 0\n' >> "${SETTINGS_FILE}"

            sed -i 's/^lock-delay=.*/lock-delay=uint32 0/' "${SETTINGS_FILE}"

# Ensure the lock entry exists so users cannot override the setting
if [ ! -f "${LOCK_FILE}" ]; then
    echo '/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay' > "${LOCK_FILE}"
else
    grep -qxF '/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay' "${LOCK_FILE}" || \
        echo '/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay' >> "${LOCK_FILE}"
fi

# Apply the dconf database changes

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Enforce maximum consecutive same-class characters
# in passwords via pam_pwquality (maxclassrepeat = 4)
#
# This script is idempotent: it can be run multiple times safely.

set -euo pipefail

PWQUALITY_CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
PWQUALITY_DIR="/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d"
PARAM="maxclassrepeat"
VALUE="4"

# Ensure pwquality.conf exists
if [[ ! -f "$PWQUALITY_CONF" ]]; then
    touch "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
fi

# Ensure the pwquality.conf.d directory exists (for drop-in configs, if used)
if [[ ! -d "$PWQUALITY_DIR" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$PWQUALITY_DIR"
fi

configure_maxclassrepeat() {
    local file="$1"

    if [[ -f "$file" ]] && grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=" "$file"; then
        # Parameter exists; update its value if different
        current_value=$(grep -E "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=" "$file" | tail -n1 | sed -E "s/^\s*${PARAM}\s*=\s*([0-9]+).*/\1/")
        if [[ "$current_value" != "$VALUE" ]]; then
            sed -i -E "s/^(\s*${PARAM}\s*=\s*).*/\1${VALUE}/" "$file"
        fi
    else
        # Parameter does not exist; append it
        echo "${PARAM} = ${VALUE}" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Apply to main config file
configure_maxclassrepeat "$PWQUALITY_CONF"

# Also create/update a dedicated drop-in file for clarity and persistence
DROPIN_FILE="${PWQUALITY_DIR}/50-maxclassrepeat.conf"
configure_maxclassrepeat "$DROPIN_FILE"

# Verify the setting is present and correct in at least one location
if grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=\s*${VALUE}\s*$" "$PWQUALITY_CONF" || \
   grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=\s*${VALUE}\s*$" "$DROPIN_FILE"; then
    echo "SUCCESS: ${PARAM} is set to ${VALUE} in pwquality configuration."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to verify ${PARAM} setting." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Remediation script to ensure USBGuard is installed on RHEL 8
# USBGuard is the software framework that protects against
# rogue or unauthorized USB devices through device-attribute
# allow/block listing.

set -euo pipefail

PACKAGE="usbguard"

# Check if the package is already installed
if rpm -q "${PACKAGE}" &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' is already installed. No action needed."
else
    echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' is not installed. Installing now..."
    if command -v dnf &>/dev/null; then
        dnf install -y "${PACKAGE}"
    elif command -v yum &>/dev/null; then
        yum install -y "${PACKAGE}"
    else
        echo "Error: Neither dnf nor yum package manager found." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Verify installation succeeded
    if rpm -q "${PACKAGE}" &>/dev/null; then
        echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' successfully installed."
    else
        echo "Error: Failed to install package '${PACKAGE}'." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

exit 0
