#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure permissions on interactive users' shell initialization
# files (and root's) are no more permissive than u=rwx,g=r,o= (0740).
# This script is idempotent - it will only tighten permissions that exceed
# 0740, and will never loosen permissions that are already stricter.

set -u

MAX_PERM_OCTAL="0740"
MAX_PERM_DEC=$((8#${MAX_PERM_OCTAL}))

# List of common shell/login initialization files to check in each home dir.
INIT_FILES=(
    ".bash_profile"
    ".bashrc"
    ".bash_login"
    ".bash_logout"
    ".profile"
    ".cshrc"
    ".login"
    ".logout"
    ".kshrc"
    ".zshrc"
    ".zprofile"
    ".zlogin"
    ".zlogout"
    ".tcshrc"
)

process_home_dir() {
    local home_dir="$1"
    local user="$2"

    [ -d "$home_dir" ] || return 0

    for init_file in "${INIT_FILES[@]}"; do
        local target="${home_dir}/${init_file}"

        # Only operate on regular files, not symlinks or directories,
        # and only if they actually exist.
        if [ -f "$target" ] && [ ! -L "$target" ]; then
            local current_perm_octal
            current_perm_octal=$(stat -c '%a' "$target" 2>/dev/null)
            [ -z "$current_perm_octal" ] && continue

            local current_perm_dec=$((8#${current_perm_octal}))
            local new_perm_dec=$((current_perm_dec & MAX_PERM_DEC))

            if [ "$new_perm_dec" -ne "$current_perm_dec" ]; then
                local new_perm_octal
                new_perm_octal=$(printf '%03o' "$new_perm_dec")
                chmod "$new_perm_octal" "$target"
                echo "Tightened permissions on ${target} (user: ${user}) from ${current_perm_octal} to ${new_perm_octal}"
            fi
        fi
    done
}

# --- Process root account explicitly ---
root_home=$(getent passwd root | cut -d: -f6)
if [ -n "${root_home:-}" ]; then
    process_home_dir "$root_home" "root"
fi

# --- Process all human/interactive users ---
# Determine minimum UID considered "human" from login.defs, default to 1000.
MIN_UID=$(awk '/^\s*UID_MIN/ {print $2}' /etc/login.defs 2>/dev/null)
if ! [[ "$MIN_UID" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
    MIN_UID=1000
fi

while IFS=: read -r username _ uid _ _ home shell; do
    # Skip root here, already handled above.
    [ "$username" = "root" ] && continue

    # Only consider human/interactive users based on UID threshold.
    [[ "$uid" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || continue
    if [ "$uid" -lt "$MIN_UID" ]; then
        continue
    fi

    # Skip users with a non-interactive shell.
    case "$shell" in
        */nologin|*/false|"")
            continue
            ;;
    esac

    # Skip if home directory doesn't exist or is not a real directory.
    [ -d "$home" ] || continue

    process_home_dir "$home" "$username"
done < <(getent passwd)

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Enforce GNOME screensaver lock-delay via dconf lockdown
# so that ordinary users cannot override it.
#
# This script is idempotent - running it multiple times results in the
# same final state.

set -euo pipefail

DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
DCONF_LOCKS_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks"
DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/00-screensaver"
DCONF_LOCK_FILE="${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}/00-screensaver"

LOCK_DELAY_VALUE="uint32 5"

# Ensure required directories exist
mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}"

# --- Write/update the settings file idempotently ---
if [[ ! -f "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}" ]]; then
    cat > "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}" <<EOF
[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-delay=${LOCK_DELAY_VALUE}
EOF
else
    # Ensure the section header exists
    if ! grep -q '^\[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver\]' "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"; then
        printf '\n[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]\n' >> "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"
    fi

    # Ensure lock-delay key is set correctly within that section
    if grep -q '^lock-delay=' "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"; then
        sed -i "s/^lock-delay=.*/lock-delay=${LOCK_DELAY_VALUE}/" "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"
    else
        # Insert lock-delay line right after the section header
        sed -i "/^\[org\/gnome\/desktop\/screensaver\]/a lock-delay=${LOCK_DELAY_VALUE}" "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"
    fi
fi

# --- Write/update the lock file idempotently ---
LOCK_KEY="/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay"

if [[ ! -f "${DCONF_LOCK_FILE}" ]]; then
    cat > "${DCONF_LOCK_FILE}" <<EOF
${LOCK_KEY}
EOF
else
    if ! grep -qxF "${LOCK_KEY}" "${DCONF_LOCK_FILE}"; then
        printf '%s\n' "${LOCK_KEY}" >> "${DCONF_LOCK_FILE}"
    fi
fi

# --- Update the dconf database so the policy takes effect immediately ---
if command -v dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dconf update
else
    echo "Warning: dconf command not found; policy files written but database not updated." >&2
fi

if [ -f "$SYSCTL_CONF" ]; then

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Disable Linux kernel user namespaces
#
# This script sets user.max_user_namespaces to 0 to disable the
# creation of new user namespaces, both at runtime and persistently
# across reboots. Idempotent - safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

readonly SYSCTL_PARAM="user.max_user_namespaces"
readonly SYSCTL_VALUE="0"
readonly PERSIST_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/70-disable-user-namespaces.conf"
readonly PERSIST_LINE="${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${SYSCTL_VALUE}"

# Must be root to modify sysctl settings and files under /etc
if [[ "${EUID}" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "Error: this script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# --- Ensure the runtime value is set immediately ---
current_value="$(sysctl -n "${SYSCTL_PARAM}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"

if [[ "${current_value}" != "${SYSCTL_VALUE}" ]]; then
    if ! sysctl -w "${SYSCTL_PARAM}=${SYSCTL_VALUE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Error: failed to set ${SYSCTL_PARAM} at runtime." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# --- Ensure persistence across reboots ---
# Create the drop-in directory if it doesn't already exist
if [[ ! -d /etc/sysctl.d ]]; then
    mkdir -p /etc/sysctl.d
fi

if [[ -f "${PERSIST_FILE}" ]]; then
    # If file exists, ensure it has exactly the desired setting
    if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_PARAM}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "${PERSIST_FILE}"; then
        # Remove any existing (possibly conflicting) entries for this parameter
        sed -i "/^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_PARAM//./\\.}[[:space:]]*=/d" "${PERSIST_FILE}"
        echo "${PERSIST_LINE}" >> "${PERSIST_FILE}"
    fi
else
    echo "${PERSIST_LINE}" > "${PERSIST_FILE}"
fi

chmod 0644 "${PERSIST_FILE}"

# --- Reload sysctl settings from the persistent file to confirm consistency ---
sysctl -p "${PERSIST_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# --- Final verification ---
final_value="$(sysctl -n "${SYSCTL_PARAM}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"

if [[ "${final_value}" == "${SYSCTL_VALUE}" ]]; then
    echo "Success: ${SYSCTL_PARAM} is set to ${SYSCTL_VALUE} (runtime and persistent)."
    exit 0
else
    echo "Error: verification failed; ${SYSCTL_PARAM} is '${final_value}', expected '${SYSCTL_VALUE}'." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure AIDE checks extended attributes (xattrs) for all rulesets

AIDE_CONF="/etc/aide.conf"

if [ ! -f "$AIDE_CONF" ]; then
    echo "Error: $AIDE_CONF not found. Is AIDE installed?" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Backup original file once
if [ ! -f "${AIDE_CONF}.bak" ]; then
    cp -p "$AIDE_CONF" "${AIDE_CONF}.bak"
fi

# Function to check if a line defines a ruleset (NAME = ...)
# We only touch lines that look like: SomeName = something
# and that are not comments, and don't already contain xattrs

TMP_FILE=$(mktemp)

awk '
BEGIN { FS="=" }
{
    line = $0
    # Preserve blank lines and comments unchanged
    if (line ~ /^[[:space:]]*#/ || line ~ /^[[:space:]]*$/) {
        print line
        next
    }

    # Match ruleset definition lines: NAME = VALUE
    # NAME can contain letters, digits, underscore
    if (match(line, /^[[:space:]]*[A-Za-z0-9_]+[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*.+$/)) {
        # Split into name and value parts using first = only
        eq_pos = index(line, "=")
        name_part = substr(line, 1, eq_pos)
        value_part = substr(line, eq_pos+1)

        # Trim leading/trailing whitespace from value_part for checking
        trimmed_value = value_part
        gsub(/^[[:space:]]+/, "", trimmed_value)
        gsub(/[[:space:]]+$/, "", trimmed_value)

        # Skip if this looks like a group/macro definition that is not a ruleset
        # (Heuristic: ruleset values typically contain a list of + separated attributes,
        #  e.g., p+i+n+u+g+s+m+c+acl+selinux+xattrs)
        # We only add xattrs if the value looks like an attribute list (contains a '+')
        # and does not already contain "xattrs"
        if (trimmed_value ~ /\+/ && trimmed_value !~ /(^|\+)xattrs(\+|$)/) {
            # Append +xattrs to the value
            new_value = trimmed_value "+xattrs"
            print name_part " " new_value
            next
        } else {
            print line
            next
        }
    } else {
        print line
    }
}
' "$AIDE_CONF" > "$TMP_FILE"

# Only replace if changes were made (idempotent check via diff)
if ! diff -q "$AIDE_CONF" "$TMP_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    cp "$TMP_FILE" "$AIDE_CONF"
    chmod --reference="${AIDE_CONF}.bak" "$AIDE_CONF" 2>/dev/null || chmod 644 "$AIDE_CONF"
    echo "Updated $AIDE_CONF to include xattrs checking in all rulesets."
else
    echo "$AIDE_CONF already has xattrs checking enabled for all rulesets. No changes made."
fi

rm -f "$TMP_FILE"

exit 0

#
# Idempotent remediation: ensure the root account is subject to the same
# pam_faillock lockout policy as regular users on RHEL 8, using an

# and reboots.

    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

    echo "authselect not found on this system; aborting." >&2
    exit 1

CURRENT_FEATURES="$(cut -s -d' ' -f2- <<<"${CURRENT_RAW}")"

# This script is idempotent: running it multiple times will not

#!/bin/bash
#
# Idempotent remediation: ensure /boot mount (if separate) has nosuid option
# set on the running system and persisted in /etc/fstab.

set -euo pipefail

BOOT_MOUNT="/boot"
FSTAB="/etc/fstab"

# If /boot is not a separately mounted filesystem, there's nothing to do.
if ! mountpoint -q "$BOOT_MOUNT"; then
    echo "INFO: '$BOOT_MOUNT' is not a separate mount point. No remediation needed."
    exit 0
fi

# Backup fstab before making any changes.
if [[ -w "$FSTAB" ]]; then
    cp -p "$FSTAB" "${FSTAB}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
fi

# Locate the fstab line corresponding to the /boot mount point.
line_num=$(awk -v mp="$BOOT_MOUNT" '$1 !~ /^#/ && $2 == mp {print NR; exit}' "$FSTAB")

if [[ -z "${line_num:-}" ]]; then
    echo "WARNING: No entry for '$BOOT_MOUNT' found in $FSTAB. Cannot persist nosuid option there."
else
    current_opts=$(awk -v mp="$BOOT_MOUNT" '$1 !~ /^#/ && $2 == mp {print $4; exit}' "$FSTAB")

    if [[ ",${current_opts}," == *",nosuid,"* ]]; then
        echo "INFO: '$FSTAB' entry for '$BOOT_MOUNT' already contains 'nosuid'."
    else
        if [[ -z "$current_opts" || "$current_opts" == "defaults" ]]; then
            new_opts="defaults,nosuid"
        else
            new_opts="${current_opts},nosuid"
        fi

        tmp_fstab=$(mktemp)
        awk -v mp="$BOOT_MOUNT" -v newopts="$new_opts" '
            BEGIN { OFS="\t" }
            $1 !~ /^#/ && $2 == mp { $4 = newopts }
            { print }
        ' "$FST

#
# Idempotent remediation: ensure /boot mount (if separate) has nosuid option
# set on the running system and persisted in /etc/fstab.

set -euo pipefail

BOOT_MOUNT="/boot"
FSTAB="/etc/fstab"

# If /boot is not a separately mounted filesystem, there's nothing to do.
if ! mountpoint -q "$BOOT_MOUNT"; then
    echo "INFO: '$BOOT_MOUNT' is not a separate mount point. No remediation needed."
    exit 0
fi

# Backup fstab before making any changes.
if [[ -w "$FSTAB" ]]; then
    cp -p "$FSTAB" "${FSTAB}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
fi

# Locate the fstab line corresponding to the /boot mount point.
line_num=$(awk -v mp="$BOOT_MOUNT" '$1 !~ /^#/ && $2 == mp {print NR; exit}' "$FSTAB")

if [[ -z "${line_num:-}" ]]; then
    echo "WARNING: No entry for '$BOOT_MOUNT' found in $FSTAB. Cannot persist nosuid option there."
else
    current_opts=$(awk -v mp="$BOOT_MOUNT" '$1 !~ /^#/ && $2 == mp {print $4; exit}' "$FSTAB")

    if [[ ",${current_opts}," == *",nosuid,"* ]]; then

    else
        if [[ -z "$current_opts" || "$current_opts" == "defaults" ]]; then
            new_opts="defaults,nosuid"
        else
            new_opts="${current_opts},nosuid"
        fi

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation: Disable the GDM user list on the login screen and enforce
# this as a system-wide, non-overridable dconf policy on RHEL 8.
#
# This script is idempotent: it can be run multiple times safely.

set -euo pipefail

DCONF_PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"
DCONF_PROFILE_FILE="${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}/gdm"

DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/gdm.d"
DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/01-login-screen"

DCONF_LOCKS_DIR="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/locks"
DCONF_LOCK_FILE="${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}/00-login-screen-lock"

# Ensure required directories exist
mkdir -p "${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}"

# --- Configure dconf profile for gdm ---
# Ensures the gdm system database is used and defines the file-db location.
if [ ! -f "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" ]; then
    cat > "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" <<'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:gdm
file-db:/usr/share/gdm/greeter-dconf-defaults
EOF
else
    # Ensure required lines exist, avoiding duplicates
    grep -qxF 'user-db:user' "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" || echo 'user-db:user' >> "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
    grep -qxF 'system-db:gdm' "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" || echo 'system-db:gdm' >> "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
    grep -qxF 'file-db:/usr/share/gdm/greeter-dconf-defaults' "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" || \
        echo 'file-db:/usr/share/gdm/greeter-dconf-defaults' >> "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
fi

# --- Configure the setting to disable the user list ---
cat > "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}" <<'EOF'
[org/gnome/login-screen]
disable-user-list=true
EOF

# --- Lock the setting so it cannot be overridden ---
cat > "${DCONF_LOCK_FILE}" <<'EOF'
/org/gnome/login-screen/disable-user-list
EOF

# --- Apply the dconf database update ---
if command -v dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dconf update
else
    echo "Warning: 'dconf' command not found; database not updated." >&2
    exit 1
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure AIDE periodic check emails results to root
#
# This script configures the system so that the scheduled AIDE integrity
# scan (run via cron or systemd timer) sends its output via email to the
# local root mailbox. It is idempotent and safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

readonly CRON_FILE="/etc/cron.d/aide"
readonly AIDE_BIN="/usr/sbin/aide"
readonly MAIL_BIN="$(command -v mail || echo /usr/bin/mail)"
readonly CRON_SCHEDULE="0 5 * * *"
readonly CRON_USER="root"
readonly MAIL_SUBJECT="\$(hostname) - AIDE Integrity Check"
readonly MARKER="# Managed by AIDE remediation script - do not edit manually"

# Ensure mailx (or equivalent providing /usr/bin/mail) is installed
if ! command -v mail >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf install -y mailx
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum install -y mailx
    else
        echo "ERROR: No supported package manager found to install mailx." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Re-resolve mail binary path after potential install
MAIL_BIN="$(command -v mail)"

# Ensure aide binary exists
if [ ! -x "${AIDE_BIN}" ]; then
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf install -y aide
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum install -y aide
    else
        echo "ERROR: AIDE is not installed and no package manager was found." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# If AIDE database does not exist, initialize it (best effort, non-fatal)
if [ ! -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz ] && [ ! -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db ]; then
    if command -v aide >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        "${AIDE_BIN}" --init >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        if [ -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz ]; then
            mv -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz
        fi
        if [ -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new ]; then
            mv -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new /var/lib/aide/aide.db
        fi
    fi
fi

# Determine which systemd unit (if any) currently manages the periodic AIDE check
AIDE_TIMER_UNIT=""
if systemctl list-unit-files 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^aidecheck\.timer'; then
    AIDE_TIMER_UNIT="aidecheck.timer"
elif systemctl list-unit-files 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^aide-check\.timer'; then
    AIDE_TIMER_UNIT="aide-check.timer"
fi

# If a systemd timer/service pair manages AIDE checks, ensure the service
# pipes its output to mail. Otherwise, fall back to configuring cron.
if [ -n "${AIDE_TIMER_UNIT}" ]; then
    SERVICE_NAME="${AIDE_TIMER_UNIT%.timer}.service"
    SERVICE_FRAGMENT="$(systemctl show -p FragmentPath --value "${SERVICE_NAME}" 2>/dev/null || true)"

    if [ -n "${SERVICE_FRAGMENT}" ] && [ -f "${SERVICE_FRAGMENT}" ]; then
        OVERRIDE_DIR="/etc/systemd/system/${SERVICE_NAME}.d"
        OVERRIDE_FILE="${OVERRIDE_DIR}/mail-report.conf"

        mkdir -p "${OVERRIDE_DIR}"

        DESIRED_EXECSTART="/bin/sh -c '${AIDE_BIN} --check | ${MAIL_BIN} -s \"${MAIL_SUBJECT}\" root'"

        TMP_OVERRIDE="$(mktemp)"
        {
            echo "[Service]"
            echo "ExecStart="
            echo "ExecStart=${DESIRED_EXECSTART}"
        } > "${TMP_OVERRIDE}"

        if [ ! -f "${OVERRIDE_FILE}" ] || ! cmp -s "${TMP_OVERRIDE}" "${OVERRIDE_FILE}"; then
            install -m 0644 "${TMP_OVERRIDE}" "${OVERRIDE_FILE}"
        fi
        rm -f "${TMP_OVERRIDE}"

        systemctl daemon-reload
        systemctl enable "${AIDE_TIMER_UNIT}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    else
        AIDE_TIMER_UNIT=""
    fi
fi

# Fallback / additional layer: configure cron.d entry to guarantee email
# delivery even if no systemd timer is present, or to reinforce the
# scheduled check via cron as required by policy.
DESIRED_CRON_LINE="${CRON_SCHEDULE} ${CRON_USER} ${AIDE_BIN} --check | ${MAIL_BIN} -s \"${MAIL_SUBJECT}\" root"

TMP_CRON="$(mktemp)"
{
    echo "${MARKER}"
    echo "SHELL=/bin/sh"
    echo "PATH=/sbin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin"
    echo "${DESIRED_CRON_LINE}"
} > "${TMP_CRON}"

if [ ! -f "${CRON_FILE}" ] || ! cmp -s "${TMP_CRON}" "${CRON_FILE}"; then
    install -m 0644 "${TMP_CRON}" "${CRON_FILE}"
fi
rm -f "${TMP_CRON}"

# Ensure crond is enabled and running so the cron-based fallback persists
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable --now crond >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

echo "AIDE periodic check email reporting has been configured successfully."
exit 0

if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable --now crond >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

echo "AIDE periodic check email reporting has been configured successfully."
exit 0

#
# Remediation script: Ensure auditd collects information on
# discretionary access control permission modifications (chmod,
# fchmod, fchmodat) for regular users, on a 64-bit RHEL 8 system.
#
set -euo pipefail

RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod.rules"
KEY="perm_mod"
SYSCALLS="chmod,fchmod,fchmodat"

# Determine the UID threshold for "regular" (non-system) users.
UID_MIN="$(awk '/^\s*UID_MIN/ {print $2}' /etc/login.defs 2>/dev/null | head -n1)"
if [[ -z "${UID_MIN}" ]]; then
    UID_MIN=1000
fi

# Ensure the rules directory exists.
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Ensure the rules file exists.
touch "${RULES_FILE}"

# Build the two required rule lines (32-bit and 64-bit).
RULE_B32="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S ${SYSCALLS} -F auid>=${UID_MIN} -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}"
RULE_B64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S ${SYSCALLS} -F auid>=${UID_MIN} -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}"

add_rule_if_missing() {
    local rule="$1"
    local file="$2"

    # Build a regex that matches the rule regardless of minor
    # formatting differences (order of -F options is not checked
    # here; we look for the essential components together).
    if ! grep -qE -- "-F[[:space:]]+arch=$(echo "$rule" | grep -oP 'arch=\K[^ ]+')[[:space:]].*-S[[:space:]]+${SYSCALLS//,/\\,}.*-F[[:space:]]+auid>=${UID_MIN}[[:space:]].*-F[[:space:]]+auid!=unset.*-F[[:space:]]+key=${KEY}" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "${rule}" >> "${file}"
    fi
}

add_rule_if_missing "${RULE_B32}" "${RULES_FILE}"
add_rule_if_missing "${RULE_B64}" "${RULES_FILE}"

# Deduplicate the file, preserving order, in case of repeated runs.
if [[ -s "${RULES_FILE}" ]]; then
    awk '!seen[$0]++' "${RULES_FILE}" > "${RULES_FILE}.tmp" && mv "${RULES_FILE}.tmp" "${RULES_FILE}"

# the audit.rules file used at boot by the auditd service).

# in a container/chroot environment without full systemd support).
if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd 2>/dev/null; then
    systemctl restart auditd || true
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Disable GDM automatic login on RHEL 8
# This script ensures that AutomaticLoginEnable is set to false
# in all relevant GDM custom configuration files, persistently
# and idempotently.

set -euo pipefail

GDM_CONF="/etc/gdm/custom.conf"

# Ensure the GDM configuration directory exists
if [ ! -d /etc/gdm ]; then
    mkdir -p /etc/gdm
fi

# If the config file doesn't exist, create it with a proper [daemon] section
if [ ! -f "$GDM_CONF" ]; then
    cat > "$GDM_CONF" << 'EOF'
[daemon]
AutomaticLoginEnable=false
EOF
    chmod 644 "$GDM_CONF"
    echo "Created $GDM_CONF with AutomaticLoginEnable=false"
    exit 0
fi

# Backup the original file before modifying, only once
BACKUP_FILE="${GDM_CONF}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
if [ ! -f "${GDM_CONF}.bak" ]; then
    cp "$GDM_CONF" "${GDM_CONF}.bak"
fi

# Ensure a [daemon] section exists
if ! grep -qE '^\[daemon\]' "$GDM_CONF"; then
    printf '\n[daemon]\n' >> "$GDM_CONF"
fi

# Function to set a key=value under [daemon] section, idempotently
set_daemon_key() {
    local key="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local tmpfile
    tmpfile=$(mktemp)

    awk -v key="$key" -v value="$value" '
        BEGIN { in_daemon = 0; found = 0 }
        /^\[daemon\]/ {
            print
            in_daemon = 1
            next
        }
        /^\[/ {
            if (in_daemon && !found) {
                print key "=" value
                found = 1
            }
            in_daemon = 0
            print
            next
        }
        {
            if (in_daemon && $0 ~ "^[[:space:]]*"key"[[:space:]]*=") {
                print key "=" value
                found = 1
                next
            }
            print
        }
        END {
            if (in_daemon && !found) {
                print key "=" value
            }
        }
    ' "$GDM_CONF" > "$tmpfile"

    mv "$tmpfile" "$GDM_CONF"
}

# Remove any commented-out or duplicate AutomaticLoginEnable lines outside [daemon]
# and ensure the correct one is set within [daemon]
set_daemon_key "AutomaticLoginEnable" "false"

# Also remove AutomaticLogin key if present, to avoid confusion (not required but ensures clean state)
if grep -qE '^\s*AutomaticLogin\s*=' "$GDM_CONF"; then
    sed -i -E '/^\s*AutomaticLogin\s*=/d' "$GDM_CONF"
fi

# Ensure correct permissions
chmod 644 "$GDM_CONF"

# Verify the setting is correctly applied
if grep -qE '^\s*AutomaticLoginEnable\s*=\s*false\s*$' "$GDM_CONF"; then
    echo "AutomaticLoginEnable is set to false in $GDM_CONF"
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to set AutomaticLoginEnable=false in $GDM_CONF" >&2
    exit 1
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure pwquality enforces dictionary-word checking
# on newly set passwords (RHEL 8) via /etc/security/pwquality.conf
# and /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf
#
# This script is idempotent: it can be run multiple times safely.

set -euo pipefail

PWQUALITY_CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
PWQUALITY_CONF_DIR="/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d"
SETTING_NAME="dictcheck"
SETTING_VALUE="1"
SETTING_LINE="${SETTING_NAME} = ${SETTING_VALUE}"

# Ensure pwquality package is installed (provides pwquality.conf and PAM integration)
if ! rpm -q libpwquality >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf install -y libpwquality
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum install -y libpwquality
    else
        echo "ERROR: No supported package manager found to install libpwquality." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Ensure main config file exists
if [ ! -f "${PWQUALITY_CONF}" ]; then
    touch "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
fi

# Function to remove any existing dictcheck settings from a file
remove_setting_from_file() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -f "${file}" ]; then
        # Remove any line (commented or not) that sets dictcheck
        sed -i -E '/^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*dictcheck[[:space:]]*=.*/d' "${file}"
    fi
}

# Step 1: Remove any conflicting dictcheck settings from all conf.d files
# so there is a single authoritative source of truth.
if [ -d "${PWQUALITY_CONF_DIR}" ]; then
    for f in "${PWQUALITY_CONF_DIR}"/*.conf; do
        [ -e "${f}" ] || continue
        remove_setting_from_file "${f}"
    done
fi

# Step 2: Remove any existing dictcheck setting from the main config file
remove_setting_from_file "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"

# Step 3: Append the correct setting to the main config file
# Ensure file ends with a newline before appending
if [ -s "${PWQUALITY_CONF}" ] && [ -n "$(tail -c1 "${PWQUALITY_CONF}")" ]; then
    echo "" >> "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
fi
echo "${SETTING_LINE}" >> "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"

# Step 4: Verify the setting is present and correct
if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*dictcheck[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*1[[:space:]]*$" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"; then
    echo "SUCCESS: dictcheck=1 is set in ${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to set dictcheck=1 in ${PWQUALITY_CONF}" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Step 5: Ensure PAM is configured to use pwquality (authselect/pam profiles)
# Verify pam_pwquality.so is referenced in system-auth / password-auth
for pam_file in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth; do
    if [ -f "${pam_file}" ]; then
        if ! grep -q "pam_pwquality.so" "${pam_file}"; then
            echo "WARNING: pam_pwquality.so not found in ${pam_file}. Consider running 'authselect enable-feature with-pwquality' or verifying your authselect profile." >&2
        fi
    fi
done

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Disable ctrl-alt-del.target reboot action on RHEL 8
# Idempotent, persistent across reboots and system updates.

set -euo pipefail

UNIT="ctrl-alt-del.target"
OVERRIDE_DIR="/etc/systemd/system/${UNIT}.d"
OVERRIDE_FILE="${OVERRIDE_DIR}/disable-caD.conf"

# 1. Mask the ctrl-alt-del.target unit.
#    This creates/ensures a symlink to /dev/null for the unit,
#    which survives package updates (since it's in /etc, not /usr/lib).
if [[ "$(systemctl is-enabled "${UNIT}" 2>/dev/null || true)" != "masked" ]]; then
    systemctl mask "${UNIT}"
fi

# 2. Additionally, ensure via an explicit drop-in override that the target
#    has no operation, as defense-in-depth in case masking is ever reversed.
mkdir -p "${OVERRIDE_DIR}"

DESIRED_CONTENT='[Unit]
Description=Ctrl-Alt-Del Reboot Action Disabled by Security Remediation
Documentation=
DefaultDependencies=no
Conflicts=
Requires=
AllowIsolate=no
'

if [[ ! -f "${OVERRIDE_FILE}" ]] || ! diff -q <(printf '%s' "${DESIRED_CONTENT}") "${OVERRIDE_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    printf '%s' "${DESIRED_CONTENT}" > "${OVERRIDE_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${OVERRIDE_FILE}"
fi

# 3. Reload systemd manager configuration so changes take effect immediately.
systemctl daemon-reload

# 4. Verify the target is masked; re-mask if reload somehow unmasked it.
if [[ "$(systemctl is-enabled "${UNIT}" 2>/dev/null || true)" != "masked" ]]; then
    systemctl mask "${UNIT}"
    systemctl daemon-reload
fi

exit 0

SYSCTL_PARAM="net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_source_route"
SYSCTL_VALUE="0"
SYSCTL_CONF_DIR="/etc/sysctl.d"
SYSCTL_CONF_FILE="${SYSCTL_CONF_DIR}/60-ipv6-disable-source-route.conf"

if [ ! -d "${SYSCTL_CONF_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${SYSCTL_CONF_DIR}"
fi

if [ -f "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" ]; then
    if grep -qE "^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"; then
        sed -i -E "s/^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=.*/${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${SYSCTL_VALUE}/" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
    else
        echo "${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${SYSCTL_VALUE}" >> "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
    fi
else
    echo "${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${SYSCTL_VALUE}" > "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
fi

for CONF_FILE in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
    if [ -f "${CONF_FILE}" ] && [ "${CONF_FILE}" != "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" ]; then
        if grep -qE "^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=" "${CONF_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
            sed -i -E "s/^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=.*/# \0 (superseded by ${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE})/" "${CONF_FILE}"
        fi
    fi
done

sysctl -w "${SYSCTL_PARAM}=${SYSCTL_VALUE}"

sysctl --system > /dev/null 2>&1 || true

CURRENT_VALUE=$(sysctl -n "${SYSCTL_PARAM}")
if [ "${CURRENT_VALUE}" != "${SYSCTL_VALUE}" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to apply ${SYSCTL_PARAM}=${SYSCTL_VALUE}. Current value: ${CURRENT_VALUE}" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "Successfully configured ${SYSCTL_PARAM}=${SYSCTL_VALUE} (runtime and persistent)."
exit 0

    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1

    local opts="$1"
    if [[ ",${opts}," != *",${REQUIRED_OPT},"* ]]; then

# Remediation script: Disable IPv6 ICMP redirect acceptance on all interfaces
# Idempotent - safe to run multiple times

set -euo pipefail

SYSCTL_CONF_DIR="/etc/sysctl.d"
SYSCTL_CONF_FILE="${SYSCTL_CONF_DIR}/60-disable-ipv6-accept-redirects.conf"

PARAMETERS=(
    "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects"
    "net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects"
)

# Ensure the sysctl.d directory exists
if [ ! -d "${SYSCTL_CONF_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${SYSCTL_CONF_DIR}"
fi

# Ensure the configuration file exists
if [ ! -f "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" ]; then
    touch "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
fi

for PARAM in "${PARAMETERS[@]}"; do
    # Update persistent configuration file idempotently
    if grep -qE "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i -E "s|^\s*${PARAM}\s*=.*|${PARAM} = 0|" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
    else
        echo "${PARAM} = 0" >> "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
    fi

    # Apply setting at runtime
    sysctl -w "${PARAM}=0" >/dev/null
done

# Remove any conflicting settings in other sysctl configuration files
CONFIG_FILES=(
    "/etc/sysctl.conf"
    /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf
    /run/sysctl.d/*.conf
    /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf
)

for PARAM in "${PARAMETERS[@]}"; do
    for FILE in "${CONFIG_FILES[@]}"; do
        for F in $FILE; do
            if [ -f "$F" ] && [ "$F" != "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" ]; then
                if grep -qE "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=" "$F" 2>/dev/null; then
                    sed -i -E "/^\s*${PARAM}\s*=/d" "$F"
                fi
            fi
        done
    done
done

# Reload sysctl settings to ensure consistency
sysctl --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

#
# Remediation script: Ensure audit rules exist for file deletion via
# rename/renameat syscalls, for auid >= 1000 (excluding unset), on
# both 32-bit and 64-bit syscall interfaces, tagged with key "delete".
#
# This script is idempotent: existing matching rules are not duplicated.
#

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/audit_delete.rules"

# Ensure the rules directory exists
mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"

# Ensure the rules file exists
if [ ! -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    touch "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Determine system architecture to know which arch rules to add
ARCH=$(uname -m)

declare -a RULES=()

RULES+=("-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S rename,renameat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k delete")

if [ "${ARCH}" = "x86_64" ] || [ "${ARCH}" = "aarch64" ] || [ "${ARCH}" = "ppc64" ] || [ "${ARCH}" = "ppc64le" ] || [ "${ARCH}" = "s390x" ]; then
    RULES+=("-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S rename,renameat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k delete")
fi

for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -Fxq -- "${rule}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "${rule}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    fi
done

# Also ensure no duplicate identical rule lines remain in the file
if [ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    TMP_FILE=$(mktemp)
    awk '!seen[$0]++' "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" > "${TMP_FILE}"
    mv "${TMP_FILE}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

chmod 0640 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Load the rules so they take effect immediately and persist across reboots
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" || true
fi

# Ensure auditd service is enabled and restarted to apply changes
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl is-enabled auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl enable auditd
    systemctl restart auditd || true
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Enable Kernel Page-Table Isolation (KPTI)
# to mitigate Meltdown (CVE-2017-5754) and harden KASLR.
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

readonly PARAMETER="pti"
readonly VALUE="on"
readonly ARG="${PARAMETER}=${VALUE}"
readonly GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE="/etc/default/grub"

# Determine the correct grub.cfg path (BIOS vs UEFI)
determine_grub_cfg_path() {
    if [ -d /sys/firmware/efi ]; then
        if [ -f /etc/grub2-efi.cfg ]; then
            readlink -f /etc/grub2-efi.cfg
        else
            find /boot/efi -maxdepth 3 -type f -name grub.cfg 2>/dev/null | head -n1
        fi
    else
        if [ -f /etc/grub2.cfg ]; then
            readlink -f /etc/grub2.cfg
        else
            find /boot -maxdepth 2 -type f -name grub.cfg 2>/dev/null | head -n1
        fi
    fi
}

# This ensures it is carried over to any newly installed kernels
# (since grubby / kernel-install / new-kernel-pkg read GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX).

if [ ! -f "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}" ]; then
    touch "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
fi

if grep -qE '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"; then
    CURRENT_LINE=$(grep -E '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}" | tail -n1)
    CURRENT_VALUE=$(echo "${CURRENT_LINE}" | sed -E 's/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="(.*)"$/\1/')

    if echo "${CURRENT_VALUE}" | grep -qE "(^|[[:space:]])${PARAMETER}=[^[:space:]]*"; then
        # Parameter exists; ensure its value is correct
        if ! echo "${CURRENT_VALUE}" | grep -qE "(^|[[:space:]])${ARG}([[:space:]]|$)"; then
            NEW_VALUE=$(echo "${CURRENT_VALUE}" | sed -E "s/(^|[[:space:]])${PARAMETER}=[^[:space:]]*/\1${ARG}/")
            sed -i -E "s|^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=.*|GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${NEW_VALUE}\"|" "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
        fi
    else
        # Parameter not present; append it
        if [ -z "${CURRENT_VALUE}" ]; then
            NEW_VALUE="${ARG}"
        else
            NEW_VALUE="${CURRENT_VALUE} ${ARG}"
        fi
        sed -i -E "s|^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=.*|GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${NEW_VALUE}\"|" "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
    fi
else
    # No GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX line at all; add one
    echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${ARG}\"" >> "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
fi

# --- Step 2: Regenerate grub.cfg so future kernel installs inherit the setting ---

GRUB_CFG_PATH="$(determine_grub_cfg_path)"

if [ -n "${GRUB_CFG_PATH}" ] && command -v grub2-mkconfig >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    grub2-mkconfig -o "${GRUB_CFG_PATH}"
fi

# --- Step 3: Apply the parameter to all currently installed kernels via grubby ---
# This ensures existing kernel boot entries have the parameter set immediately.

if command -v grubby >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    NEEDS_UPDATE=0
    while IFS= read -r kernel_path; do
        [ -z "${kernel_path}" ] && continue
        if ! grubby --info="${kernel_path}" 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^args=' | grep -qE "(^|[[:space:]\"])${ARG}([[:space:]\"]|$)"; then
            NEEDS_UPDATE=1
            break
        fi
    done < <(grubby --info=ALL 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^kernel=' | sed -E 's/^kernel="?([^"]*)"?$/\1/')

    if [ "${NEEDS_UPDATE}" -eq 1 ]; then
        grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="${ARG}"
    fi

    # Remove any conflicting duplicate/opposite settings, ensure clean single entry
    while IFS= read -r kernel_path; do
        [ -z "${kernel_path}" ] && continue
        current_args=$(grubby --info="${kernel_path}" 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^args=' | sed -E 's/^args="(.*)"$/\1/')
        occurrences=$(echo "${current_args}" | grep -oE "(^|[[:space:]])${PARAMETER}=[^[:space:]]*" | wc -l)
        if [ "${occurrences}" -gt 1 ]; then
            grubby --update-kernel="${kernel_path}" --remove-args="${PARAMETER}"
            grubby --update-kernel="${kernel_path}" --args="${ARG}"
        fi
    done < <(grubby --info=ALL 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^kernel=' | sed -E 's/^kernel="?([^"]*)"?$/\1/')
fi

exit 0

    if [[ "$DEFINED_UID_MIN" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        UID_MIN="$DEFINED_UID_MIN"
    fi
fi

# Ensure audit rules directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Create the rules file if it doesn't exist
touch "$RULES_FILE"

# Define the rules for fchownat syscall, both b32 and b64
declare -a RULES=(
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S fchownat -F auid>=${UID_MIN} -F auid!=unset -k perm_mod"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fchownat -F auid>=${UID_MIN} -F auid!=unset -k perm_mod"
)

for RULE in "${RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -qxF "$RULE" "$RULES_FILE"; then
        echo "$RULE" >> "$RULES_FILE"
    fi
done

# Reload audit rules if augenrules is available
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "$RULES_FILE" || true
fi

exit 0

# Remediation script: Disable acceptance of IPv6 ICMP redirect messages
# on all interfaces (default and all), applied at runtime and persisted
# across reboots on RHEL 8.

set -euo pipefail

SYSCTL_CONF="/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl-ipv6-redirects.conf"

    "net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects"
    "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects"
)

    # Remove any existing entries for this parameter (idempotency)
    sed -i -E "/^\s*${param//./\\.}\s*=/d" "${SYSCTL_CONF}"

    # Append the desired setting
    echo "${param} = 0" >> "${SYSCTL_CONF}"

    # Apply at runtime immediately
    sysctl -w "${param}=0" >/dev/null
done

# Reload sysctl settings from all configuration files to ensure consistency
sysctl --system >/dev/null

exit 0

#
# Remediation: Disable legacy vsyscall support in kernel boot configuration
# This prevents predictable virtual syscall page addresses from being used
# in exploitation of memory corruption vulnerabilities.
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

readonly PARAM="vsyscall"
readonly VALUE="none"
readonly SETTING="${PARAM}=${VALUE}"
readonly GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE="/etc/default/grub"

echo "Remediating: ensure ${SETTING} is set for all kernels (current and future)."

if [[ -f "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}" ]]; then
    if grep -qP '^\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"; then
        if grep -P '^\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}" | grep -qP "\b${PARAM}=\S+"; then
            # Parameter present; ensure it is set to the correct value
            if ! grep -P '^\s*GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}" | grep -qP "\b${SETTING}\b"; then
                sed -ri "s/^(GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"[^\"]*)\b${PARAM}=\S+([^\"]*\")/\1${SETTING}\2/" "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
                echo "Updated existing ${PARAM} setting in ${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}."
            else
                echo "${SETTING} already present in ${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}."
            fi
        else
            # Parameter not present; append it inside the quotes
            sed -ri "s/^(GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\")([^\"]*)(\")/\1\2 ${SETTING}\3/" "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
            # Clean up potential double spaces introduced
            sed -ri 's/^(GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=")\s+/\1/' "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
            sed -ri 's/(GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="[^"]*)\s{2,}/\1 /g' "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
            echo "Added ${SETTING} to ${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}."
        fi
    else
        # No GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX line at all; create one
        echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${SETTING}\"" >> "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
        echo "Created GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX with ${SETTING} in ${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}."
    fi
else
    echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${SETTING}\"" > "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
    echo "Created ${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE} with ${SETTING}."
fi

# --- Step 2: Apply setting to all currently installed kernels via grubby ---

if command -v grubby >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Determine which kernels already have the setting to keep this idempotent/no-op safe
    NEED_UPDATE=0
    while IFS= read -r kernel_path; do
        [[ -z "${kernel_path}" ]] && continue
        if ! grubby --info="${kernel_path}" 2>/dev/null | grep -qP "args=\"[^\"]*\b${SETTING}\b"; then
            NEED_UPDATE=1
            break
        fi
    done < <(grubby --info=ALL 2>/dev/null | awk -F= '/^kernel=/{gsub(/"/,"",$2); print $2}')

    if [[ "${NEED_UPDATE}" -eq 1 ]]; then
        grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="${SETTING}"
        echo "Applied ${SETTING} to all installed kernels via grubby."
    else
        echo "${SETTING} already applied to all installed kernels."
    fi
else
    echo "WARNING: grubby not found; unable to update installed kernel entries directly." >&2
fi

# --- Step 3: Regenerate grub.cfg so persisted config matches /etc/default/grub ---

GRUB_CFG=""
if [[ -d /boot/efi/EFI ]]; then
    # UEFI system: locate grub.cfg under EFI
    GRUB_CFG=$(find /boot/efi/EFI -maxdepth 2 -name grub.cfg 2>/dev/null | head -n1)
fi

if [[ -z "${GRUB_CFG}" ]]; then
    if [[ -f /boot/grub2/grub.cfg ]]; then
        GRUB_CFG="/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"
    fi
fi

if command -v grub2-mkconfig >/dev/null 2>&1 && [[ -n "${GRUB_CFG}" ]]; then
    grub2-mkconfig -o "${GRUB_CFG}"

else

fi

#!/bin/bash
#

#
# This script ensures that unprivileged (non-root) processes cannot
# invoke the BPF syscall by setting the kernel.unprivileged_bpf_disabled
# sysctl parameter to 1, both at runtime and persistently across reboots.
#
# The script is idempotent and safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

SYSCTL_PARAM="kernel.unprivileged_bpf_disabled"
DESIRED_VALUE="1"

SYSCTL_CONF_DIR="/etc/sysctl.d"
SYSCTL_CONF_FILE="${SYSCTL_CONF_DIR}/60-bpf-hardening.conf"

# Ensure we are running as root
if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "Error: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure the sysctl configuration directory exists
if [[ ! -d "${SYSCTL_CONF_DIR}" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "${SYSCTL_CONF_DIR}"
fi

# --- Persist the setting across reboots ---
#
# Remove any existing (possibly conflicting) settings for this parameter
# from all sysctl configuration files, then write the desired setting
# to a single, dedicated configuration file. This avoids duplicate or
# conflicting entries and keeps the change idempotent.

# Search locations typically read by sysctl
SYSCTL_SEARCH_PATHS=(
    "/etc/sysctl.conf"
    "/etc/sysctl.d"
    "/run/sysctl.d"
    "/usr/lib/sysctl.d"
)

for path in "${SYSCTL_SEARCH_PATHS[@]}"; do
    if [[ -f "${path}" ]]; then
        if grep -Eq "^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=" "${path}" 2>/dev/null; then
            sed -ri "s/^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=.*/${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}/" "${path}"
        fi
    elif [[ -d "${path}" ]]; then
        while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
            if grep -Eq "^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
                if [[ "${file}" == "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" ]]; then
                    sed -ri "s/^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=.*/${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}/" "${file}"
                else
                    sed -ri "/^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=/d" "${file}"
                fi
            fi
        done < <(find "${path}" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' -print0 2>/dev/null)
    fi
done

# Ensure the desired setting exists in the dedicated config file
if [[ -f "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" ]] && grep -Eq "^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"; then
    sed -ri "s/^\s*${SYSCTL_PARAM}\s*=.*/${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}/" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
else
    echo "${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}" >> "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
fi

# --- Apply the setting at runtime ---
sysctl -w "${SYSCTL_PARAM}=${DESIRED_VALUE}" >/dev/null

# --- Reload sysctl settings to ensure consistency ---
sysctl --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# --- Verify the setting is correctly applied ---
CURRENT_VALUE="$(sysctl -n "${SYSCTL_PARAM}")"
if [[ "${CURRENT_VALUE}" -ne "${DESIRED_VALUE}" ]]; then
    echo "Error: Failed to set ${SYSCTL_PARAM} to ${DESIRED_VALUE} at runtime." >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "Success: ${SYSCTL_PARAM} is set to ${DESIRED_VALUE} (runtime and persistent)."
exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation script: Enable OCSP-based certificate revocation checking
# for smart-card / certificate authentication in SSSD (RHEL 8).
#
# This script is idempotent - it can be run multiple times safely.

set -euo pipefail

SSSD_CONF="/etc/sssd/sssd.conf"
SECTION="[sssd]"
OPTION="certificate_verification"
VALUE="ocsp_dgst"
DESIRED_LINE="${OPTION} = ${VALUE}"
BACKUP_SUFFIX=".bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Ensure sssd package/config exists
if [ ! -f "${SSSD_CONF}" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: ${SSSD_CONF} does not exist. Is SSSD installed/configured?" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Take a backup only if we are about to change something (checked later),
# but keep a safety backup path ready.
backup_config() {
    cp -p "${SSSD_CONF}" "${SSSD_CONF}${BACKUP_SUFFIX}"
    echo "Backup created: ${SSSD_CONF}${BACKUP_SUFFIX}"
}

# Check if the [sssd] section exists
if ! grep -qE '^\s*\[sssd\]\s*$' "${SSSD_CONF}"; then
    backup_config
    {
        echo ""
        echo "${SECTION}"
        echo "${DESIRED_LINE}"
    } >> "${SSSD_CONF}"
    echo "Added [sssd] section with ${DESIRED_LINE} to ${SSSD_CONF}"
else
    # Extract the [sssd] section boundaries
    SECTION_START=$(grep -nE '^\s*\[sssd\]\s*$' "${SSSD_CONF}" | head -n1 | cut -d: -f1)
    TOTAL_LINES=$(wc -l < "${SSSD_CONF}")

    SECTION_END=$(awk -v start="${SECTION_START}" '
        NR > start && /^\s*\[.*\]\s*$/ { print NR-1; exit }
        END { if (!found) print NR }
    ' "${SSSD_CONF}")

    if [ -z "${SECTION_END}" ] || [ "${SECTION_END}" -lt "${SECTION_START}" ]; then
        SECTION_END="${TOTAL_LINES}"
    fi

    # Check if certificate_verification is already set correctly within [sssd] section
    EXISTING_LINE_NUM=$(awk -v start="${SECTION_START}" -v end="${SECTION_END}" -v opt="${OPTION}" '
        NR >= start && NR <= end && $0 ~ "^[[:space:]]*"opt"[[:space:]]*=" { print NR; exit }
    ' "${SSSD_CONF}")

    if [ -n "${EXISTING_LINE_NUM}" ]; then
        CURRENT_LINE=$(sed -n "${EXISTING_LINE_NUM}p" "${SSSD_CONF}")
        CURRENT_VALUE=$(echo "${CURRENT_LINE}" | sed -E 's/^[^=]+=[[:space:]]*//' | xargs)

        if [ "${CURRENT_VALUE}" == "${VALUE}" ]; then
            echo "OCSP certificate verification is already correctly configured in ${SSSD_CONF}."
        else
            backup_config
            sed -i "${EXISTING_LINE_NUM}s|.*|${DESIRED_LINE}|" "${SSSD_CONF}"
            echo "Updated existing ${OPTION} setting to '${VALUE}' in ${SSSD_CONF}"
        fi
    else
        backup_config
        sed -i "${SECTION_START}a ${DESIRED_LINE}" "${SSSD_CONF}"
        echo "Inserted ${DESIRED_LINE} into [sssd] section of ${SSSD_CONF}"
    fi
fi

# Ensure correct permissions and ownership on sssd.conf
chown root:root "${SSSD_CONF}"
chmod 600 "${SSSD_CONF}"

# Validate config syntax if sssd is installed and provides a config check tool
if command -v sssctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sssctl config-check >/tmp/sssd_config_check.log 2>&1; then
        echo "WARNING: sssctl config-check reported issues:" >&2
        cat /tmp/sssd_config_check.log >&2
    fi
fi

# Restart SSSD to apply changes, only if service is active or enabled
if systemctl is-enabled sssd >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl is-active sssd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl restart sssd
    echo "SSSD service restarted to apply OCSP certificate verification settings."
else
    echo "SSSD service is not enabled/active; settings will apply on next start."
fi

echo "OCSP-based certificate revocation checking has been configured in SSSD."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure successful mount() syscalls by regular users
# (UID >= 1000, excluding unset UID) are audited under key 'export'.
#
# This script is idempotent - it can be run multiple times without
# duplicating rules, and it persists the configuration across reboots.

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/50-export.rules"
KEY="export"

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"

# Ensure the rules file exists
touch "${RULES_FILE}"

# Determine system architecture to decide whether b32 rules are relevant
ARCH="$(uname -m)"

# Build the list of rules that should be present
declare -a DESIRED_RULES=()

# 64-bit rule is always applicable on 64-bit capable systems
DESIRED_RULES+=("-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S mount -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -F key=${KEY}")

# Only include the b32 rule if the architecture supports 32-bit compatibility
# (i.e., x86_64 systems typically support b32 syscalls via multilib).
case "${ARCH}" in
    x86_64|i686|i386)
        DESIRED_RULES+=("-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S mount -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -F key=${KEY}")
        ;;
    *)
        # For architectures without 32-bit compatibility (e.g., aarch64 without
        # multilib support), skip the b32 rule.
        :
        ;;
esac

# Function to check if a rule (ignoring exact whitespace) already exists in file
rule_exists() {
    local rule="$1"
    local file="$2"
    grep -Fxq -- "${rule}" "${file}"
}

CHANGED=0

for rule in "${DESIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! rule_exists "${rule}" "${RULES_FILE}"; then
        echo "${rule}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
        CHANGED=1
    fi
done

# Remove duplicate lines while preserving order, in case of prior manual edits
if [ -s "${RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    TMP_FILE="$(mktemp)"
    awk '!seen[$0]++' "${RULES_FILE}" > "${TMP_FILE}"
    if ! cmp -s "${TMP_FILE}" "${RULES_FILE}"; then
        mv "${TMP_FILE}" "${RULES_FILE}"
        CHANGED=1
    else
        rm -f "${TMP_FILE}"
    fi
fi

chmod 640 "${RULES_FILE}"

# Reload audit rules to apply changes immediately, and ensure persistence
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Ensure auditd is enabled and running so rules persist across reboots
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    systemctl is-active --quiet auditd || systemctl start auditd
fi

if [ "${CHANGED}" -eq 1 ]; then
    echo "Audit rules for 'export' key have been added/updated in ${RULES_FILE}."
else
    echo "Audit rules for 'export' key already present in ${RULES_FILE}. No changes made."
fi

exit 0

    awk '!seen[$0]++' "${RULES_FILE}" > "${TMP_FILE}"
    if ! cmp -s "${TMP_FILE}" "${RULES_FILE}"; then
        mv "${TMP_FILE}" "${RULES_FILE}"
        CHANGED=1
    else
        rm -f "${TMP_FILE}"
    fi
fi

chmod 640 "${RULES_FILE}"

# Reload audit rules to apply changes immediately, and ensure persistence
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Ensure auditd is enabled and running so rules persist across reboots
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    systemctl is-active --quiet auditd || systemctl start auditd
fi

if [ "${CHANGED}" -eq 1 ]; then
    echo "Audit rules for 'export' key have been added/updated in ${RULES_FILE}."
else
    echo "Audit rules for 'export' key already present in ${RULES_FILE}. No changes made."
fi

exit 0
```

set -euo pipefail

readonly AUDIT_FILE="/var/log/lastlog"
readonly AUDIT_PERMS="wa"
readonly AUDIT_KEY="logins"
readonly RULE_LINE="-w ${AUDIT_FILE} -p ${AUDIT_PERMS} -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"

readonly RULES_D_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
readonly RULES_D_FILE="${RULES_D_DIR}/${AUDIT_KEY}.rules"
readonly AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

rule_matches() {
    # $1 = line to check
    local line="$1"
    # Matches "-w /var/log/lastlog" possibly with different perms ordering,
    # followed eventually by "-k logins" or "-F key=logins"
    echo "${line}" | grep -Eq -- '^-w[[:space:]]+'"${AUDIT_FILE//\//\\/}"'([[:space:]]|$)' \
        && echo "${line}" | grep -Eq -- '\-p[[:space:]]+[wa]{1,2}([[:space:]]|$)' \
        && echo "${line}" | grep -Eq -- '(-k[[:space:]]+'"${AUDIT_KEY}"'|-F[[:space:]]+key='"${AUDIT_KEY}"')([[:space:]]|$)'
}

ensure_rule_in_file() {
    local file="$1"

    [ -e "${file}" ] || touch "${file}"

    if grep -vE '^\s*#' "${file}" 2>/dev/null | while IFS= read -r line; do
        [ -z "${line}" ] && continue
        rule_matches "${line}" && echo "match"
    done | grep -q "match"; then
        return 0
    fi

    if ! grep -Fxq -- "${RULE_LINE}" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "${RULE_LINE}" >> "${file}"
    fi
}

ensure_rule_in_file "${RULES_D_FILE}"

if [ -e "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ] || [ -d "$(dirname "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}")" ]; then

        rule_matches "${line}" && echo "match"

        auditctl -w "${AUDIT_FILE}" -p "${AUDIT_PERMS}" -k "${AUDIT_KEY}" || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation script: Disable IPv6 packet forwarding (RHEL 8)
#
# This script ensures the kernel does not forward IPv6 packets between
# network interfaces. It is idempotent and applies the setting both
# at runtime and persistently across reboots.

set -euo pipefail

SYSCTL_CONF_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-ipv6-forwarding.conf"

PARAMETERS=(
    "net.ipv6.conf.all.forwarding"
    "net.ipv6.conf.default.forwarding"
)

DESIRED_VALUE="0"

# Ensure the sysctl configuration file exists
if [[ ! -f "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" ]]; then
    touch "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
fi

for PARAM in "${PARAMETERS[@]}"; do
    # Update or add the parameter in the persistent sysctl config file
    if grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -ri "s/^\s*${PARAM}\s*=.*/${PARAM} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}/" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
    else
        echo "${PARAM} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}" >> "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
    fi

    # Remove any conflicting settings for this parameter in other sysctl files
    # to avoid ambiguity about which value takes effect.
    for FILE in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
        [[ -e "${FILE}" ]] || continue
        [[ "${FILE}" == "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" ]] && continue
        if grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=" "${FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
            sed -ri "s/^\s*${PARAM}\s*=.*/# &  (managed by ${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE})/" "${FILE}" || true
        fi
    done

    # Apply the setting at runtime
    sysctl -w "${PARAM}=${DESIRED_VALUE}" >/dev/null
done

# Reload sysctl settings to ensure consistency
sysctl --system >/dev/null

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Enforce lcredit requirement in pwquality configuration
# This ensures every newly set password contains at least one lowercase letter.

set -euo pipefail

PWQUALITY_CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
PWQUALITY_DIR="/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d"
DESIRED_VALUE="-1"
PARAM="lcredit"

# Ensure the main config file exists
if [ ! -f "${PWQUALITY_CONF}" ]; then
    touch "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
fi

# Ensure the pwquality.conf.d directory exists (RHEL 8 supports drop-in configs)
if [ ! -d "${PWQUALITY_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${PWQUALITY_DIR}"
fi

# Use a dedicated drop-in file for idempotent, isolated management of this setting
DROPIN_FILE="${PWQUALITY_DIR}/50-lcredit.conf"

configure_lcredit() {
    local file="$1"

    if [ -f "${file}" ] && grep -qE "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=" "${file}"; then
        # Parameter exists - ensure it has the desired value
        if ! grep -qE "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=\s*${DESIRED_VALUE}\s*$" "${file}"; then
            sed -i -E "s/^\s*${PARAM}\s*=.*/${PARAM} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}/" "${file}"
        fi
    else
        # Parameter does not exist - append it
        echo "${PARAM} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}" >> "${file}"
    fi
}

# Remove any conflicting lcredit settings from the main config file to avoid
# ambiguity, since drop-in files take precedence but duplicate/conflicting
# entries in the main file could cause confusion. We manage the setting
# exclusively in the drop-in file for idempotency and clarity.
if grep -qE "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"; then
    sed -i -E "/^\s*${PARAM}\s*=/d" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
fi

# Configure the drop-in file idempotently
configure_lcredit "${DROPIN_FILE}"

# Set appropriate permissions
chmod 0644 "${DROPIN_FILE}"
chown root:root "${DROPIN_FILE}"

# Verify the setting is correctly applied
if grep -qE "^\s*${PARAM}\s*=\s*${DESIRED_VALUE}\s*$" "${DROPIN_FILE}"; then
    echo "Password quality policy successfully enforces: ${PARAM} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}"
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to enforce ${PARAM} = ${DESIRED_VALUE} in ${DROPIN_FILE}" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Disable IEEE 1394 (FireWire) kernel module (firewire_core)
# Ensures the module cannot be loaded and is blacklisted persistently.

set -euo pipefail

MODULE_NAME="firewire_core"
MODPROBE_DIR="/etc/modprobe.d"
MODPROBE_FILE="${MODPROBE_DIR}/${MODULE_NAME}.conf"

# Ensure the modprobe.d directory exists
if [ ! -d "${MODPROBE_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${MODPROBE_DIR}"
fi

# Ensure the configuration file exists
if [ ! -f "${MODPROBE_FILE}" ]; then
    touch "${MODPROBE_FILE}"
fi

# Add "install firewire_core /bin/false" line if not already present
INSTALL_LINE="install ${MODULE_NAME} /bin/false"
if ! grep -Fxq "${INSTALL_LINE}" "${MODPROBE_FILE}"; then
    echo "${INSTALL_LINE}" >> "${MODPROBE_FILE}"
fi

# Add "blacklist firewire_core" line if not already present
BLACKLIST_LINE="blacklist ${MODULE_NAME}"
if ! grep -Fxq "${BLACKLIST_LINE}" "${MODPROBE_FILE}"; then
    echo "${BLACKLIST_LINE}" >> "${MODPROBE_FILE}"
fi

# If the module is currently loaded, attempt to unload it
if lsmod | grep -q "^${MODULE_NAME}[[:space:]]"; then
    modprobe -r "${MODULE_NAME}" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Rebuild the initramfs to ensure changes persist across reboots
if command -v dracut >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dracut -f
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Configure auditd disk_error_action to SINGLE
# This ensures that if auditd encounters a disk error while writing
# audit records, the system switches to single-user mode for
# corrective action, preventing silent loss of audit data.
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times, persists across reboots.

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_CONF="/etc/audit/auditd.conf"
PARAMETER="disk_error_action"
VALUE="SINGLE"

# Ensure the configuration file exists
if [[ ! -f "${AUDIT_CONF}" ]]; then
    echo "Error: ${AUDIT_CONF} not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Check if the parameter already exists (commented or not, case-insensitive)
if grep -qiE "^[[:space:]]*${PARAMETER}[[:space:]]*=" "${AUDIT_CONF}"; then
    # Parameter exists; ensure it is set to the correct value (case-insensitive comparison)
    CURRENT_VALUE=$(grep -iE "^[[:space:]]*${PARAMETER}[[:space:]]*=" "${AUDIT_CONF}" | tail -n1 | sed -E "s/^[[:space:]]*${PARAMETER}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*//I" | tr -d '[:space:]')

    if [[ "${CURRENT_VALUE^^}" != "${VALUE}" ]]; then
        # Update existing line(s) to the correct value
        sed -i -E "s/^[[:space:]]*${PARAMETER}[[:space:]]*=.*/${PARAMETER} = ${VALUE}/I" "${AUDIT_CONF}"
    fi
else
    # Parameter does not exist; append it
    echo "${PARAMETER} = ${VALUE}" >> "${AUDIT_CONF}"
fi

# Verify the change was applied correctly
if ! grep -qiE "^[[:space:]]*${PARAMETER}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "${AUDIT_CONF}"; then
    echo "Error: Failed to set ${PARAMETER} to ${VALUE} in ${AUDIT_CONF}." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Reload/restart auditd to apply the change immediately, if service is active
if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd 2>/dev/null; then
    if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
    service auditd restart >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl restart auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

echo "auditd ${PARAMETER} successfully set to ${VALUE} in ${AUDIT_CONF}."

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure audit rules exist to monitor write and
# attribute-change events on /etc/shadow, tagged with the "identity" key.
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times, works whether rules are managed
# via augenrules (rules.d) or directly via auditctl (audit.rules).

set -euo pipefail

readonly AUDIT_FILE="/etc/shadow"
readonly AUDIT_KEY="identity"
readonly RULE="-w ${AUDIT_FILE} -p wa -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"

readonly RULES_D_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
readonly RULES_D_FILE="${RULES_D_DIR}/${AUDIT_KEY}.rules"
readonly AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

# Ensure the rules.d directory exists (augenrules mechanism)
if [ ! -d "${RULES_D_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${RULES_D_DIR}"
fi

# Add rule to rules.d file if not already present (augenrules mechanism)
if [ -f "${RULES_D_FILE}" ]; then
    if ! grep -qE -- "^[[:space:]]*-w[[:space:]]+${AUDIT_FILE}[[:space:]]+-p[[:space:]]+wa[[:space:]]+-k[[:space:]]+${AUDIT_KEY}[[:space:]]*$" "${RULES_D_FILE}"; then
        echo "${RULE}" >> "${RULES_D_FILE}"
    fi
else
    echo "${RULE}" > "${RULES_D_FILE}"
fi

# Add rule to audit.rules file if not already present (auditctl direct mechanism)
if [ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    if ! grep -qE -- "^[[:space:]]*-w[[:space:]]+${AUDIT_FILE}[[:space:]]+-p[[:space:]]+wa[[:space:]]+-k[[:space:]]+${AUDIT_KEY}[[:space:]]*$" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"; then
        echo "${RULE}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    fi
else
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}")"
    echo "${RULE}" > "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Reload rules to apply immediately, preferring augenrules if available
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load || true
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" || true
fi

# Ensure the rule is active in the running kernel audit rules (idempotent check)
if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -qE -- "-w[[:space:]]+${AUDIT_FILE}[[:space:]]+-p[[:space:]]+wa[[:space:]]+-k[[:space:]]+${AUDIT_KEY}"; then
        auditctl -w "${AUDIT_FILE}" -p wa -k "${AUDIT_KEY}" || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Ensure audit generates a record for execution of
# the kernel module management utility (kmod) by privileged/unprivileged
# users transitioning to auid >= 1000 (i.e., login users).
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.
#

set -u

readonly KMOD_PATH="/usr/bin/kmod"
readonly AUDIT_KEY="modules"
readonly RULES_D_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules"
readonly AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

# The canonical rule (path-based, arch-independent since it targets a
# specific executable path rather than a syscall/arch pair).
RULE="-a always,exit -F path=${KMOD_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"

# Some audit tooling normalizes auid!=unset to auid!=4294967295 when
# rules are compiled/loaded. Treat both forms as equivalent when checking
# for existing rules to preserve idempotency.
RULE_ALT="-a always,exit -F path=${KMOD_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"

ensure_rule_in_file() {
    local file="$1"

    # Create the file if it does not exist yet.
    if [ ! -f "${file}" ]; then
        mkdir -p "$(dirname "${file}")"
        touch "${file}"
    fi

    # If an equivalent rule already exists (either form), do nothing.
    if grep -q -- "path=${KMOD_PATH}" "${file}" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -q -- "perm=x" "${file}" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -q -- "auid>=1000" "${file}" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -q -- "-k ${AUDIT_KEY}\b" "${file}" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -q -- "path=${KMOD_PATH}" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
        # More precise check: look for a line that already matches either
        # the exact rule or its alternate representation.
        if grep -qF -- "${RULE}" "${file}" 2>/dev/null || \
           grep -qF -- "${RULE_ALT}" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Double-check with a strict exact-line match before appending, to
    # avoid duplicate insertion in edge cases where the loose check above
    # did not trigger.
    if ! grep -qF -- "${RULE}" "${file}" 2>/dev/null && \
       ! grep -qF -- "${RULE_ALT}" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
        printf '%s\n' "${RULE}" >> "${file}"
    fi
}

# 1. Ensure the rule is present for augenrules (rule-compilation program),
#    which reads from /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules and compiles them into
#    /etc/audit/audit.rules.
ensure_rule_in_file "${RULES_D_FILE}"

# 2. Ensure the rule is also present directly in /etc/audit/audit.rules,
#    covering the case where auditd is configured to load rules directly
#    via auditctl at daemon startup instead of through augenrules.
ensure_rule_in_file "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# 3. Attempt to load the rules immediately so the running audit daemon
#    reflects the change without requiring a reboot. Failures here (e.g.,
#    immutable audit configuration requiring a reboot) are non-fatal for
#    this remediation, since the persistent configuration is already in
#    place for the next boot.
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

set -euo pipefail

MODULE_NAME="can"
MODPROBE_CONF_DIR="/etc/modprobe.d"
MODPROBE_CONF_FILE="${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}/${MODULE_NAME}.conf"

# Ensure /etc/modprobe.d exists
if [ ! -d "${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}"
fi

    echo "install ${MODULE_NAME} /bin/false" >> "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}"

if ! grep -qE '^\s*blacklist\s+can\s*$' "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}"; then
    # Remove any existing (possibly malformed) blacklist lines for this module first
    sed -i -E '/^\s*blacklist\s+can\s*$/d' "${MODPROBE_CONF_FILE}"

    if ! lsinitrd 2>/dev/null | grep -q "modprobe.d/${MODULE_NAME}.conf"; then
        dracut -f >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# augenrules), and is idempotent.

FILE="/etc/group"
KEY="identity"

# RHEL 8 uses augenrules by default, which reads rules from
# /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules and compiles them into
# /etc/audit/audit.rules. We handle both possibilities.

AUDIT_RULES_D="/etc/audit/rules.d"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
RULES_D_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_D}/${KEY}.rules"

# --- Configure rules.d (used by augenrules) ---
if [ -f "${RULES_D_FILE}" ]; then
    if ! grep -qE "^-w[[:space:]]+${FILE//\//\\/}[[:space:]]+-p[[:space:]]+wa[[:space:]]+-k[[:space:]]+${KEY}[[:space:]]*$" "${RULES_D_FILE}"; then
        echo "${RULE_W}" >> "${RULES_D_FILE}"
    fi
else
    echo "${RULE_W}" > "${RULES_D_FILE}"
fi

# Deduplicate lines in the rules file while preserving order
if [ -f "${RULES_D_FILE}" ]; then
    TMP_FILE=$(mktemp)
    awk '!seen[$0]++' "${RULES_D_FILE}" > "${TMP_FILE}"
    mv "${TMP_FILE}" "${RULES_D_FILE}"
fi

chmod 640 "${RULES_D_FILE}"

# --- Configure audit.rules (used directly by auditctl on some setups) ---
if [ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    if ! grep -qE "^-w[[:space:]]+${FILE//\//\\/}[[:space:]]+-p[[:space:]]+wa[[:space:]]+-k[[:space:]]+${KEY}[[:space:]]*$" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"; then
        echo "${RULE_W}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
        TMP_FILE=$(mktemp)
        awk '!seen[$0]++' "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" > "${TMP_FILE}"
        mv "${TMP_FILE}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    fi
fi

# --- Load the rule into the running kernel audit system ---
# Prefer augenrules if available (handles merging rules.d files properly)
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load || true
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Fallback: load directly via auditctl if the rule isn't already active
    if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -qE "^-w[[:space:]]+${FILE}[[:space:]]+-p[[:space:]]+wa[[:space:]]+-k[[:space:]]+${KEY}[[:space:]]*$"; then
        auditctl -w "${FILE}" -p wa -k "${KEY}" || true
    fi
fi

# --- Ensure auditd service is enabled and running so changes persist ---
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl is-enabled auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl enable auditd
    systemctl is-active auditd >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl start auditd
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation script: Configure auditd to send an email alert
# when disk space falls below the low-space warning threshold
# (space_left_action = email), persisting across reboots.
#
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_CONF="/etc/audit/auditd.conf"
ACTION_KEY="space_left_action"
DESIRED_VALUE="email"

# Ensure the auditd.conf file exists
if [[ ! -f "${AUDIT_CONF}" ]]; then
    echo "Error: ${AUDIT_CONF} not found. Is auditd installed?" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Backup the original file once, if no backup exists yet
if [[ ! -f "${AUDIT_CONF}.bak" ]]; then
    cp -p "${AUDIT_CONF}" "${AUDIT_CONF}.bak"
fi

# Check if the key exists (commented or uncommented, case-insensitive)
if grep -Eqi "^[#[:space:]]*${ACTION_KEY}[[:space:]]*=" "${AUDIT_CONF}"; then
    # Get current active (uncommented) value, if any
    CURRENT_VALUE=$(grep -Ei "^[[:space:]]*${ACTION_KEY}[[:space:]]*=" "${AUDIT_CONF}" \
        | tail -n1 \
        | sed -E 's/^[[:space:]]*[^=]+=[[:space:]]*//' \
        | tr -d '[:space:]')

    if [[ "${CURRENT_VALUE}" == "${DESIRED_VALUE}" ]]; then
        echo "auditd.conf already has ${ACTION_KEY} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}. No changes needed."
    else
        # Replace all matching lines (commented or not) with the desired setting,
        # keeping only the last one active; comment out any duplicates first.
        # Simplify: comment out all existing occurrences, then append correct one.
        sed -i -E "s/^[#[:space:]]*(${ACTION_KEY}[[:space:]]*=.*)$/# \1/" "${AUDIT_CONF}"
        echo "${ACTION_KEY} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}" >> "${AUDIT_CONF}"
        echo "Updated ${ACTION_KEY} to ${DESIRED_VALUE} in ${AUDIT_CONF}."
    fi
else
    # Key not present at all; append it
    echo "${ACTION_KEY} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}" >> "${AUDIT_CONF}"
    echo "Added ${ACTION_KEY} = ${DESIRED_VALUE} to ${AUDIT_CONF}."
fi

# Ensure only one active (uncommented) instance of the key exists
ACTIVE_COUNT=$(grep -Ec "^[[:space:]]*${ACTION_KEY}[[:space:]]*=" "${AUDIT_CONF}")
if [[ "${ACTIVE_COUNT}" -gt 1 ]]; then
    # Keep the last occurrence active, comment out earlier ones
    TMP_FILE=$(mktemp)
    awk -v key="${ACTION_KEY}" '
        BEGIN { IGNORECASE=1 }
        {
            if ($0 ~ "^[[:space:]]*" key "[[:space:]]*=") {
                lines[++count] = NR
            }
            all[NR] = $0
        }
        END {
            last = lines[count]
            for (i = 1; i <= NR; i++) {
                if ((i in lines_lookup) ) {}
            }
        }
    ' "${AUDIT_CONF}" > /dev/null 2>&1 || true

    # Simpler and more robust approach: use grep -n and sed to comment all but last
    LAST_LINE=$(grep -nEi "^[[:space:]]*${ACTION_KEY}[[:space:]]*=" "${AUDIT_CONF}" | tail -n1 | cut -d: -f1)
    LINE_NUM=0
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        LINE_NUM=$((LINE_NUM+1))
        if echo "$line" | grep -Eqi "^[[:space:]]*${ACTION_KEY}[[:space:]]*=" && [[ "${LINE_NUM}" -ne "${LAST_LINE}" ]]; then
            echo "# ${line}"
        else
            echo "$line"
        fi
    done < "${AUDIT_CONF}" > "${TMP_FILE}"

    mv "${TMP_FILE}" "${AUDIT_CONF}"
    echo "Cleaned up duplicate ${ACTION_KEY} entries; kept the last one active."
fi

# Set correct permissions (auditd.conf should be root:root, 0640 typically)
chown root:root "${AUDIT_CONF}"
chmod 0640 "${AUDIT_CONF}"

# Reload/restart auditd to apply changes, if service is active
if command -v systemctl &>/dev/null; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
        # augenrules/service reload may not re-read auditd.conf without restart
        systemctl restart auditd || {
            echo "Warning: failed to restart auditd service. Please restart manually." >&2
        }
    else
        echo "auditd service is not active; configuration will apply on next start."
    fi
else
    echo "systemctl not found; please restart auditd manually to apply changes." >&2
fi

echo "Remediation complete: ${ACTION_KEY} is set to '${DESIRED_VALUE}' in ${AUDIT_CONF}."

#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation: Disable GNOME Ctrl-Alt-Del reboot action and lock the setting
# so that local users logged into the graphical desktop cannot trigger an
# accidental reboot, and cannot override this via their own dconf settings.
#
# Idempotent: safe to re-run; checks for existing content before writing.

set -euo pipefail

DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
DCONF_LOCKS_DIR="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/locks"
SETTINGS_FILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/00-security-settings"
LOCKS_FILE="${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}/00-security-settings-lock"

SETTINGS_SECTION="[org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys]"
SETTINGS_KEY="logout=''"
LOCK_KEY="/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys/logout"

# Ensure required packages are present (dconf should already be installed
# on any system with GNOME, but verify anyway).
if ! command -v dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf install -y dconf
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum install -y dconf
    fi
fi

# Ensure the dconf database directories exist
mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}"

# Ensure the settings file exists
touch "${SETTINGS_FILE}"

# Idempotently ensure the [org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys]
# section exists with logout='' set beneath it.
if grep -qxF "${SETTINGS_SECTION}" "${SETTINGS_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    # Section exists; check if the key is present anywhere under it.
    if ! awk -v section="${SETTINGS_SECTION}" -v key="logout" '
        $0 == section {infile=1; next}
        /^\[/ {infile=0}
        infile && $0 ~ "^"key"=" {found=1}
        END {exit !found}
    ' "${SETTINGS_FILE}"; then
        # Section exists but key missing; insert key right after the section header.
        tmpfile=$(mktemp)
        awk -v section="${SETTINGS_SECTION}" -v keyline="${SETTINGS_KEY}" '
            {
                print $0
                if ($0 == section && !inserted) {
                    print keyline
                    inserted=1
                }
            }
        ' "${SETTINGS_FILE}" > "${tmpfile}"
        mv "${tmpfile}" "${SETTINGS_FILE}"
    else
        # Key exists; make sure its value is correct, fix if not.
        sed -i "/^${SETTINGS_SECTION//\//\\/}$/,/^\[/ s/^logout=.*/${SETTINGS_KEY}/" "${SETTINGS_FILE}"
    fi
else
    # Section doesn't exist; append it along with the key.
    {
        echo ""
        echo "${SETTINGS_SECTION}"

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#
# Remediation: Disable GNOME Ctrl-Alt-Del reboot action and lock the setting
# so that local users logged into the graphical desktop cannot trigger an
# accidental reboot, and cannot override this via their own dconf settings.
#
# Idempotent: safe to re-run; checks for existing content before writing.

set -euo pipefail

DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
DCONF_LOCKS_DIR="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/locks"
SETTINGS_FILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/00-security-settings"
LOCKS_FILE="${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}/00-security-settings-lock"

SETTINGS_SECTION="[org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys]"
SETTINGS_KEY="logout=''"
LOCK_KEY="/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys/logout"

# Ensure required packages are present (dconf should already be installed
# on any system with GNOME, but verify anyway).
if ! command -v dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf install -y dconf
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum install -y dconf
    fi
fi

# Ensure the dconf database directories exist
mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}"

# Ensure the settings file exists
touch "${SETTINGS_FILE}"

# Idempotently ensure the [org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys]
# section exists with logout='' set beneath it.
if grep -qxF "${SETTINGS_SECTION}" "${SETTINGS_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    # Section exists; check if the key is present anywhere under it.
    if ! awk -v section="${SETTINGS_SECTION}" -v key="logout" '
        $0 == section {infile=1; next}
        /^\[/ {infile=0}
        infile && $0 ~ "^"key"=" {found=1}
        END {exit !found}
    ' "${SETTINGS_FILE}"; then
        # Section exists but key missing; insert key right after the section header.
        tmpfile=$(mktemp)
        awk -v section="${SETTINGS_SECTION}" -v keyline="${SETTINGS_KEY}" '
            {
                print $0
                if ($0 == section && !inserted) {
                    print keyline
                    inserted=1
                }
            }
        ' "${SETTINGS_FILE}" > "${tmpfile}"
        mv "${tmpfile}" "${SETTINGS_FILE}"
    else
        # Key exists; make sure its value is correct, fix if not.
        sed -i "/^${SETTINGS_SECTION//\//\\/}$/,/^\[/ s/^logout=.*/${SETTINGS_KEY}/" "${SETTINGS_FILE}"
    fi
else
    # Section doesn't exist; append it along with the key.
    {
        echo ""
        echo "${SETTINGS_SECTION}"
        echo "${SETTINGS_KEY}"
    } >> "${SETTINGS_FILE}"
fi

# Ensure the locks file exists and contains the lock entry exactly once.
touch "${LOCKS_FILE}"
if ! grep -qxF "${LOCK_KEY}" "${LOCKS_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "${LOCK_KEY}" >> "${LOCKS_FILE}"
fi

# Apply the dconf database updates.
dconf update

exit 0
```
